inscription.head:
No. 1.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga II, 2nd year = A.D. 1135.

Gift of an amount of 90 kāsu, deposited with some Śivabrāhmaṇas, for a lamp to the temple of
Tirumaṇañjēri-uḍaiyār, by a native of Gaṅgaikoṇḍa-chōḻapuram.

inscription.head:
No. 2.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Rājādhirāja I, 27+1st year = A.D. 1046.

Following a brief version of the king's eulogy is mentioned a gift of land, by a Paṇṇaṉ Chatu-
rasaṅgātaṉ, for feeding annually 1000 devotees including śivayōgis and tapasvis, in the temple
of Tirukkaṟṟaḷi-Mahādēva at Tirumaṇañjēri, a part (pāl) of Gaṅgaikoṇḍachōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅ-
galam in Kuṟukkai-nāḍu in Rājēndrasimha-vaḷanāḍu, on the occasion of the Paṅguni-uttiram
festival. The land located at Mūlaṅguḍi in Nallāṟṟūr-nāḍu, which was a dēvadāna of the temple,
had been reclaimed from an uncultivable garden land.

inscription.head:
No. 3.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Rājādhirāja I, 27th year Rhishabha, ba. di. 6,
Thursday, Śravaṇa = A.D. 1045, May 9, Thursday.

The mahāsabhā of Gaṅgaikoṇḍchōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam sold some land to the temple of
Tirumaṇañjēri after making it tax-free dēvadāna, for which they received money from the tem-
ple. One Ñānaśivar was asked to take possession of the land and arrange for the stipulated
services.

edition.l: ṉeṉṉum uyarnta perum pukaṭ kōvirājakēsari pa[ nmarā]-

inscription.head:
No. 4.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Vikramachōḻa, 5th year, Makara, sudi. 10,
Wednesday, Mṛigaśīra = A.D. 1123, February 7, Wednesday.

Sale of land by the mahāsabhā of Karikālachōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Kuṟukkai-nāḍu
of Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu on the north bank, which assembled at the temple of
Karikālachōḻa-viṇṇagar-Āḻvār in the village. The sale was made to pay off some tax arrears of
those landholders of the village who left the village without paying their land dues. The land
was purchased by a Pūṇḍi-uḍaiyāṉ Uyyavandāṉ Ñānaśivar for instituting some services in the
temple praying for the health of the king (tiruvāymoḻindaruḷum tirumēni) and his family.

inscription.head:
No. 5.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north, west and south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, 8th year = A.D. 1078.

The king's eulogy is given in lines 1-44. States that at the request of Muṉaiyadaraiyar,
the king ordered for the proper arrangement of the different services in the temple using
the income of some tirunāmattukkāṇi land at Uttaṅguḍi alias Kōṭṭaiyūr in Kuṟukkai-nāḍu in
Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu. It is said that until the current year there was no inscription
and proper record of the services that depended on the income. On representation (viṇṇappam)
regarding this by Muṉaiyadaraiyar the king issued the relevant order to set it right. The tiru-
mandira-ōlai, Vikkiramasiṅga-Viḻupparaiyar wrote the royal order and the purvuvari-tiṇaik-
kaḷa-nāyagam Nittavinōda-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ got it inscribed on stone.

inscription.head:
No. 6.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja-Rājakēsarivarman (Rājarāja I), 16th year = A.D. 1001.

Gift of 96 sheep for a lamp to the temple of Maṇavāḷapperumāḷ at Tirumaṇañjēri, a part of
Viḍēlviḍugu-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Kuṟukkai-nāḍu on the northern bank, by
Māṟan Tiṭṭai of Ambar, a village in the same Nāḍu.

l.ta: r ūḻiuḷ ellāyāṇṭil toḻuta kai vi-

inscription.head:
No. 7.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra II, 9th year = A.D. 1061.

Unfinished. Gift of a gold diadem to the temple of Tiruverudupāḍi-uḍaiya-Mahādēva at
Gaṅgaikoṇḍa-chōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Kuṟukkai-nāḍu, in Rājādhirāja-vaḷanāḍu, by
Kōṭṭaiyūr-uḍaiyān Karuṇākaraṉ Muḍikoṇḍachōḻan alias Vaḷavarāja-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ in the
same nāḍu.

l.ta: kkum āka iṭṭa tiruppaṭṭam oṉṟināl pon

inscription.head:
No. 8.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Vikramachōḻa, 5th year = A.D. 1123.

Unfinished. Seems to record a tax-free gift of land by the mahāsabhā of Gaṅgaikoṇḍa
chōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Kuṟukkai-nāḍu in Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu, to the temple
of Tirumaṇañjēri-uḍaiya-Mahādēva. Details lost.

edition.l: 5- tu virutarājapayaṅkara vaḷanā-

inscription.head:
No. 9.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja Rājakēsarivarman (Rājarāja I), 6th year = A.D. 991.

Registers that at the command of Uḍaiyapirāṭtiyār (Queen Dowager), the mother of Utta-
ma-chōḻadēva, Ārūraṉ Kambaṉ alias Tirukkaṟṟaḷi-Pichchaṉ of Tirumaṇañjēri arranged for
offering sandal paste and food and for lamps using the income of an amount of 16 kaḻañju
accrued through the pīḍiligai-vāri (offerings of the devotees placed on the pulpit in front of the
deity) gifts, both in gold and rice, in the temple of Mahādēva at Tirumaṇañjēri a brahmadēya
of Kaḍalaṅguḍi in Kuṟukkai-nāḍu on the northern bank.

l.ta:
ṭai[ya] pirāṭṭiyār aruḷiñceyya āruraṉ kampanākiya tirumaṇañcērit
tirukkaṟṟaḷip piccanēṉ ni

ñ cēta paricāvatu ittēvarkku mikutik kuṟaimai uḷḷaṭaṅka orāṭṭai nāḷāl
vaṉta poṉ pa-

ṉnirukaḻaiñcum tiruvamitarici ōrāṭṭai nāḷāl vaṉta mikutikkuṟaimai
uḷḷaṭaṅka ariciyāl vanta

poṉ nālkkaḻaiñcumāka ippoṉ patiṉaṟu kaḻaiñcum uṭaiyārkku
orunāḷaikku pōtu nāḻic cantaṉakkuḻampāka muṉnāḻic cantaṉak ku-

ḻampuk kāvatākavum vantavariciyilē cantaṉattukkup pōkki nāḻi arici uccam
pōtaikku aṉṉa palikkāvatākavum nel vantaṉa mikutikkuṟaimai

uḷḷaṭaṅka āṭṭai viṭṭam iraṇṭu kācāy ikkāciraṇṭum paricaṭṭattukku
oru kācum tirunamanikaiyum vitāṉamum mēkkaṭṭiyum uttiriyamum
jalapavittirattukkum

oru kācāvatākavum vanta tiruviḷakkeṇṇai koṇṭu mikutik kuṟaimai
uḷḷaṭaṅka nicatamuḻāk keṇṇaiyāka ivveṇṇai koṇṭu ciṟukālaic
canti añcum uccam pō-

tai canti añcum irāppatiṉālum viḷakkerivippatākavum ipparicu nivantañ
ceytēṉ āruraṉ kampanākiya tirumaṇañcēri tirukkaṟṟaḷip piccanēṉ ivai
paṉmā-

inscription.head:
No. 10.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the west and south walls of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja I, ? = c. A.D. 991.

This is the decision taken in a meeting of the Uḍaiyapirāṭṭiyār (Queen Dowager), obviously
the one mentioned in the previous inscription, the officers, Pichchaṉ Ārūraṉ Kambaṉ who made
the temple of Tirumaṇañjēri in stone and the sabhā's representative (savai kaṇkāṇi), in relation
to the arrangement of various daily services in the temple, like midnight food offering, the rec-
itation of tiruppadiyam, the cleaning of the temple, the making of flower garlands, the mainte-
nance of the musicians. From the similarity of contents, this inscription has to be considered a
sequel and continuation of the previous inscription.

l.ta:
uṭaiyapirāṭṭiyārum atikārañceyvārum cātaṉañ ceyvitta
kantāṭai nampiyum tirumaṇañcErit tirukkaṟṟaḷi eṭuppitta piccaṉ āruraṉ
kampaṉum iruntu nivantamaṭaittu āṟkkāṭukiḻāṉ eḻuttāl

pukunta tīṭṭuppaṭi kaṭalaṅkuṭic cavaik kaṇkāṇiyum iruntu veṭṭuvittapaṭi
tiruvārātiṉai ceyyum nampikku nicatam patakku nellum iraṇṭu kācukku
ākap patiṉaṟu kalanellum tirunontāviḷakku nālukkum āka nicatam

tūṇiyum tiruvamirtukku pōtu kuṟuṇi aricikkum arttacāmam amirtiṉukku
nāṉāḻiyum āka arici mukkuṟuṇi nāṉāḻikku nellu irutūṇiyum kōyil
iraṇṭu māṇukku nicatam patakkum neyyamirtu nicatam uḻakkukku

nelluk kuṟuṇiyum tayiramirtu irunāḻikku āka nicatam nellu aṟu nāḻiyum
nālu cantikkum kaṟi amirtukku nicatam nellu nāṉāḻiyum aṭaikkāyamitukku
nicata nellu nāṉāḻiyum tiru-

ppatiyam pāṭuvār nālvarkku nicatam nellut tūṇiyum tirunantavāṉa
miṟaippāriruvarkku maṭaiviṭuvā ṉoruvaṉukkumāka nicatam nelluppa[ta*]
kku nāṉāḻiyum tirumeḻukkiṭuvā riruvarkku nicata nellu aṟunāḻi-

yum tiruppaḷḷittāmam paṟittut toṭuppāriruvarkku ni[ca*]tam nellukku
ṟuṇiyum ukaccu koṭṭuvār pattāḷukku āḷāl aṟunāḻiyum [va]ṭakaṅ
koṭṭuvāṉukku

iraṭṭiyum āka nellu nicatam irutūṇi irunāḻiyum caṅkiraṇṭu mūtuvārkkum
kāḷamiraṇṭumūtuvārkkum ceyakaṇṭikai yoṉṟu koṭṭuvāṉukkum āḷāl
aṟunāḻiyā-

ka nicatam nellu mukkuṟuṇi aṟunāḻiyum ārātikku nampikkup pōkki
ippaṇikaḷ ceyvārkkē koṟṟukkum puṭavai mutalum tiruviḻāppuṟamum
tirumeykāppāṉ oruva-

nṉukku nicatam nellukkuṟuṇiyum āka nellu āyirattu nāṉūṟṟu eṇkalanē
tūṇi patakku ||—

inscription.head:
No. 11.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman, 4th year = c. A.D. 950.

Gift of 96 sheep for a lamp to the temple of Paramasvāmi at Tirumaṇañjēri, by a woman-ser-
vant (peṇḍāṭṭi) attached to the (royal) kitchen at Tañjāvūr. She also presented a lamp-stand and
the stone on which this inscription is engraved.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIX, No. 93.]

inscription.head:
No. 12.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III, 17th year = A.D. 1223.

Gift of 45 kāsu along with a lamp-stand made of tarā for a lamp in the Tirumaṇañjēri temple
by a Tūṟṟukkuḍaiyāṉ Nāḍuḍaiyāṉ in Paṉaiyūr-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 13.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
c. 10th Century.
inscription.p:
Gift of 96 sheep for a lamp by Chembiyaṉ Ārkkāṭṭu-vēḷān, a kiḻāṉ of Ārkāḍu in Ārkkāṭṭu-
kūṟṟam.

inscription.head:
No. 14.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Parāntaka I, 4th year = A.D. 911.

Gift of a dish, a trumpet, a hanging lamp, a bell and an image to the temple by a chōḻiya-vāṇi-
yaṉ called Muṇḍan Araṅgaṉ alias Nārpatteṇṇāyira-panmāhēśvara-Māyilaṭṭi. The stone with
the inscription was also his gift.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIX, No. 99.]

l.ta: m tirumēniyum ivveṭṭina ik kallumivaṉ vaca[m]

inscription.head:
No. 15.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
c. 10th Century.
inscription.p: Records the gift of a stone slab by the ūrār of Tiruppūnallūr.

inscription.head:
No. 16.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
c. 10th Century.
inscription.p: Records that a Mīṉavaṉ Maṉṉaṉ alias Vīrācheṭṭi presented the stone slab.

inscription.head:
No. 17.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
c. 10th Century.
inscription.p:
Refers to a player on the pot-drum (kuḍamiḻā) in the temple of Paramasvāmi at Tirumaṇañjēri.
Rest of the details lost.

inscription.head:
No. 18.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
c. 10th Century.
inscription.p:
Records the presentation of a stone slab to the temple of Tirumaṇañjēri-Āḻvār by one Araṅgaṉ
Chandirāttaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 19.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Vikramachōḻa, 3rd year = A.D. 1121.

Fragmentary. Seems that some Śivabrāhmaṇa priests received a gift from Araiyan Marundan
of Nāgamaṅgalam. The donor is said to belong to the iḷaiya-tiru-amudavēlai-nilai, perhaps a
regiment.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇarappuvimātu vaḷara nāmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu malara tanniru patumalar
mannavar cūṭa ma-

nniya vurimaiyil maṇimuṭicūṭi ceṅkōl cenṟu ticai tōṟum vaḷarpa
veṅkali nīṅki

meyyaṟantaḻaippa kali[ṅ*]kaviriya kaṭamalai naṭātti valaṅkaḷāḻi
varaiyāḻinaṭātti iru cuṭaraḷavum o-

rukuṭai niḻaṟṟi vīrasiṅṅāsaṉattu mukkōṟkiḻānaṭikaḷōṭu vīṟṟiruntaruḷiya
kōpparakecari pa

ttan pañcanativāṇanāna tillaināyakappa[ṭṭa*]num ceyapātamāṉa varuṇaci • • •
vappaṭṭanum mātavana-

rumarutu paṭṭanum paramapuruṣan etirkoḷpāṭipaṭṭanum ivvaṉaivōm
innāṭṭu nākamaṅka-

[la]ttu irukkum iḷaiya tiruamutavēlai nilai uṭai aṟaiyan maruntan
pakkal nāṅkaḷ ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 20.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
c. 10th Century.
inscription.p: Gift of stone slabs by Tāḻi Tiruvaḍikkāri and another person.

inscription.head:
No. 21.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [Rājarāja I], 2nd year = A.D. 987.

Records an arrangement made by Uḍaiyapirāṭṭiyār Chembiyaṉ-Mahādēviyār, the mother of
Uttamachōḻadēva, for various items of expenditure (for priests, food offerings, drummers, gar-
deners and cleaners) in the temple of Tirukkaṟṟaḷi-Mahādēva at Tirumaṇañjēri, using the in-
come of paddy from some 14 vēlis of land in Mūlaṅguḍi in Nallāṟṟūr-nāḍu.

l.ta:
varkku nallāṟṟū[r*] nāṭṭu mūlaṅkuṭi nilam patiṉnālvEliyum nivantañ
ceytapaṭiyāvatu tirucceṉṉaṭaikkut tiruvamirtarici patakku ārātikkum
nampikku neṟpatakkum āka irunūṟṟumuppatiṉ kalam māṇira-

ṇṭukku ne[l*]luppatakku tiruviḷakku nālukku nelluttūṇiyum āka nellu
nūṟṟu eṇpatiṉkalam neyamirtukku nicatam uḻakku neykku nellu kuṟuṇiyum
aṭaikkāy amirtukku nāṉāḻiyum kaṟiamirtukku nāṉāḻiyum

tiruppatiyam p[ā*]ṭuvār nālvarkku ne[l*]lut tūṇiyum uvaccar pattāḷukku
ne[l*]lu irutūṇi irunāḻiyumāka nellu nānūṟṟu irupattu eḻukalaṉe
tūṇippatakku kāḷamum cekaṇṭikaiyum āka nellu patakku nanta

vāṉam iṟaippār mūvarkku nellu mukkuṟuṇiyum tiruppaḷḷittāmam paṟippār
iruvarkku neṟkuṟuṇiyum tirumeḻukkiṭuvār iruvarkku ••••••nāḻiyumāka
nellu nūṟṟuttoṇṇūṟṟu aiyṅkalaṉ eṅka(ḷ)ḷukkē vaḻi •••mutal varai
it

inscription.head:
No. 22.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the main gōpura of the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple, left of entrance.
c. 17th century.
inscription.p: Records that the gōpura was made by Nallama-Nāyakkar of Neḍuṅguṉṟam.

inscription.head:
No. 23.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the secon gōpura of the Udvāhanāthasvāmin temple, right of entrance.
c. 17th Century.
inscription.p: Gives some Sanskrit verses from the Agnipurāṇa in praise of the god Udvāhanātha.

inscription.head:
No. 24.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Madhyānēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, Sunday, Hasta = A.D. 1251-70.

This much damaged record refers to the regnal years 11 to 13 of the Kāḍava chief Kōpperuñ-
jiṅga. It seems that certain persons absconded without paying taxes (kaḍamai) and those who
stood surety (puṇai) were held responsible for the taxes.

l.ta:
nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa atta[t]tu nāḷ ikōyil tirukkāmakkoṭṭa
muṭaiya peruṅ • • •

kku ivar niṉṟa iṭattē kaikoṇṭu kāṭṭikkuṭukkaiyil ivaraiyum ivar
pēr kaṭamaikku • • •
iṟu eṉṟu iṉakku icaivu tīṭṭu tantu puṇaiyalum viṭuvittukkoṇṭu mu ••••••

inscription.head:
No. 25.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Madhyānēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 11th year, Ṛishabha, badi. 14, Sunday,
Rōhiṇi = A.D. 1261, May 29, Sunday (Month should be Mithuna not Ṛishabha).

Relates to a transaction made in the 11th year of Kōpperuñjiṅgadēva regarding certain dēvadā-
na lands of the temple of Tiru-Edirkoḷpāḍi-uḍaiyār in Kuṟukkai-nāḍu in Virudarājabhayaṅka-
ra-vaḷanāḍu. Pūṇḍiyuḍaiyāṉ Viśvēsuradēvaṉ, the māṭāpatyam of the temple who had kāṇi in
this land made it a tirunāmattukkāṇi. Some other land which he got in ōlai-iḻavu-kaiyīḍu from
Udaiyaṅguḍaiyār and others (line 6-7) is also mentioned but the context is not clear as the end
portion is broken.

l.gr:
svastiśrī samasta jagadādhāra sōmakula tilaka madhurāpūri mādhava kēraḷavaṅśa
nirmmūla laṅkādvīpalaṇḍhana dvitīyarāma karṇṇāṭarāja vidrāvaṇacōḷa kula
śailaku[la*]kāṅka

pūdurggamarddana vīrakaṇṭagōpāla vipana vipina
vidaha kā

gaṇapatihariṇaśārddū
la nēllura puraviracita vīrābhiṣēka praṇatarājapratiṣṭhāpaka
mahārājā ••••••••

l.ta:
vatu iṣapanāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu
nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaiyum catutteciyum peṟṟa rOcaṇi nā-

ḷ virutarāca payaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭuk kuṟuk[kaināṭṭu] uṭaiyār
tiruvetirkoḷpāṭi uṭaiyār kōyil āticaṇṭēcura tēvar kaṉmikku
kōpperuñ[ciṅka]rkku patiṉ oṉṟāvatu nāḷil ivvūr kāṇiyāḷaril
innāyaṉār kōyil māṭāpattiyamāy aṉusantaritta pūṇṭiyuṭaiyāṉ
vi

innāyaṉār tēvatāṉamāy eṉkāṇiyāy n[āṉ tirunāma]ttuk kāṇiyāka
kuṭutta tiruvetir koḷpāṭiyil eṉṉutāṉa paṉañcey nilam •••m attikkāṇi

teṟkut tiruccūlattāpaṉam paṇṇi •••• vum

yum irā ūr pacupati tēvaṟkku mēlai eṉṟa aḷavil •••iḻavu mūlaṅkuṭiyil
paṭṭa[r*]kaḷil tēvakaḷ tēvarum utaiyaṅkuṭaiyār tirucciṟṟampala
muṭaiyārum kōṭṭaiyūr peṇṭukaḷil tēviyum eṉṟa aḷavil ōlai iḻavu

kaiyīṭu tanta kācu aṟunūṟum āka kācu iraṇṭāyirat teḻuṉūṟum paṭṭakaḷ
coṉṉa virttiyum kācum peṟa inilam araiye muntirikaiyum •••ril eṉṟa
nilam • • aḷavil ōlai iḻavu ālattuṭaiyāṉ nilam

lum eṅkaḷ ciṟiya tevappaṉār
pukali vēntarkku valacilampu puṟam peṟṟa nilam

vum kaṇṭiyuṭaiyāṉ ariyāṉ
nilam • • tirucceṅkāṭṭaṅkuṭaiyāṉ pēril aṉṉiyaṉāmattu vilaikoṇṭa

nīkki āṟāyiraṅkācu niyāyattāril tani • • eṭṭa maḻakiyāṉ taruvatāka puṇai
iṭṭu ikkācu iṟṟaivaraiyum peṟāmaiyil • • yāṉai viḷākam nilam •••• pavum
āka nilam •••• yum tiruvētikkuṭi maṇavāḷa nampitēvatāṉam tiruvēḻvikkuṭi
nallūril kīḻaikkuḻali nilam
vum ciṅka viḷākam nilam yum āka nilam

inscription.head:
No. 26.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Madhyānēśvara temple.
c. 17th century.
inscription.p:
Records some unspecified provision made for the worship and repairs in the temple of
Edirkoḷpāḍi-tambirāṉār at Tirumaṇañjēri by one Deyvanāyakaṉ of Arivalam.

inscription.head:
No. 27.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Madhyānēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 3rd year = A.D. 1181.

Gift of a night-lamp to the temple of Tiru-Edirkoḷpāḍi-uḍaiyār at Tirumaṇañjēri in Kuṟuk-
kai-nāḍu in Virudarājabhaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu, by Arasukkaḍiyān Tirunīlakaṇḍaṉ Chēramāṉ-tōḻaṉ
of Kīraṉūr who constructed the temple in stone and who had kāṇi rights in the dēvadāna land
of the temple.

l.ta: vatu virutarājapayaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭu kuṟukkai nāṭṭu uṭaiyā-
kōyil ittirukkaṟṟaḷi ceyta kīranūrkiḻavaṉ aracukkaṭiyān tirunī-
lakaṇṭan cēramān tōḻan irāvaic cantikku vaitta viḷakku oṉṟu
onṟum cantirātittavaṟ upaiyañ celuttakkaṭavōm ic civappirāmaṇarō[m]

inscription.head:
No. 28.
Tirumaṇañjēri, Mayiladuturai Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Madhyānēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājarāja III
6+1st year = A.D. 1223.

Records that Kūṟṟattaikkumaittār, the wife of Kaṇṇamaṅgalamuḍaiyār Bīmappiḷḷai and daugh-
ter of Puliyūrḍaiyāṉ Ādittadēvaṉ Miḻalaināṭṭukkōṉ built the shrine of Tirukkāmakkōṭṭam-uḍai-
ya-Nāchchiyār and gifted land, which was her sīdana (śrīdhana) property, in Udaiyaṅguḍi a
dēvadāna village of the temple, for offerings, lamps, etc in the shrine. She further renovated a
monastery (guhai) called Ālālasundara for the use of Pugalivēndar of Pūṇḍi. It is said that the
lady borrowed money on hand loan (edirppai) from several places for building the shrine. She
also gave some land and house-sites to a Brāhmaṇa priest and two others who helped her in
several ways.

l.ta:
tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīirācarāca tEvarkku yāṇṭu āṟāvatiṉ
etirāmāṇṭu kaṇṇamaṅkalamuṭaiyār vīmappiḷḷai pāriyai ••••••pā[ti]
ripuliyūruṭaiyāṉ ātittatEvarmiḻalaināṭṭukkOṉār makaḷār [kūṟṟattaik]
kumaittār •••vattuṭaiyār tiruvetirkoḷpāṭi uṭaiyārkkut tiruppaḷḷi
aṟai nācciyārai eḻuntaruḷivikka vEṇumeṉṟu ivar colli ikkOyil
tāṉattāraiyum utayaṅkuṭaiyārkaḷaiyum aḻaittu eṉakku naṉ • •

t tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai nācciyārai eḻuntaruḷivikka vēṇumeṉṟu
vēṇṭikkoṇṭu nāṉ pala iṭaṅkaḷilum etirppai kuṭutta poruḷkaḷ
ivarai eḻuntaruḷivikka uṭalākavum eḻuntaruḷivikka vēṇṭuvatukkum
nācciyāṟkku tiruvamutu[kkuṭalāka] innāyaṉār tēvatāṉam utaiyaṅkuṭiyil
nāṉ cītaṉam peṟṟuṭaiyēṉāy eṉṉutāy nāṉ aṉupavittu varukiṟa nilattu

irukaraiyaṉ eṉṟu pērkūvappaṭṭa nilamāy nāṉ kuṭutta nilaṅkālē ciṉṉamum
eṉakku naṉ • • •

etirkoḷpāṭiyil kīḻait tiruvītiyil pūṇṭi uṭaiyār pukalivēntar
irukkiṟa ālāla cuntaraṉ kukai eṭuttukkaṭṭi mēyntu cantirātittavarai
cellavum ikkukaikkum uppukkum cāttukkum viḷakkeṇṇeykkum
ikkukai nōkkukaikkum maṭa ••••••māka kuṭutta utayaṉ kuṭiyil nāṉ
cītaṉam peṟṟu aṉupavittu varukiṟa tāṉṟippaḷḷameṉṟu pēr kūvappaṭṭa
nilam eḻumācciṉṉamum innilattuṭpaṭṭa tiṭalun teṅkuḷḷiṭṭa maraṅkaḷum
kukaikkuṭalākavum pukali vēntaru •••••••••

tāmē nōkkavum ivarkkuppiṉpu ittaṉmam nōkkavallār kaikkoṇṭu
nōkkavum vaṭa utaiyaṉkuṭiyil eṭṭumāvil kiḻakkaṭaiya eṉkāṇiyāṉa
nilam orumāvaraiyum utaiyaṉ kuṭaiyār akarattu irukkum pirāmaṇaril
tillaimūvāyira nampi makaṉ tirumaṇañcēri uṭaiyāṉ paṭṭarkku
perumpaṟṟappuliyūr nālu tirunāḷum kumpiṭa innilaṅ kuṭuttēṉeṉṟum
utaiyāṉkuṭaiyār tirucciṟṟampala muṭaiyār cōḻa vēḷārkku naṭuvil
teruvil kīḻciṟakil eṉ maṉaikuḻi muppattaiñcum imaṉaiyil

teṅkum ivaṉ eṉakku vēṇṭuvaṉa cēkaiyālē kuṭuttēṉeṉṟum
ivvūrkkīḻaitteruvil kīḻ ciṟakil kollaṉ pāḻil maṉai ku[ḻi]
muppattucciṉṉamum imaṉaiyil niṉṟa teṅkukaḷum utaiyaṉ kuṭaiyār
araṅkappiḷḷaikku kuṭuttēṉeṉṟum ippaṭi kūṟṟattaikkumaittār
colla eḻutiṉēṉ ālattūruṭaiyāṉ nampi irāmapirāṉēṉ ivai
eṉṉeḻutteṉṟum ippaṭik kūṟṟattaikkumaittār coṉṉamaikku
utaiyaṅkuṭaiyāṉ tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ cōḻa vēḷāṉ eḻutteṉṟum

ippaṭi aṟiveṉ parantavaṉ cōḻa vēḷāṉ eḻutteṉṟum ippaṭi aṟivēṉ
vēḷāṉ tiruvaraṅkamuṭaiyāṉ eḻutteṉṟum itu cempaṅkuṭaiyāṉ
vētaṉ kaimāṭṭāṅkāṉamaikku ālattūruṭaiyāṉ eḻutteṉṟum ippaṭi
ivar aruḷicceyyak kēṭṭēṉ ivvūr taṭṭārak kāṇi uṭaiya āḷ
uriyāṉ aṟputak kūttap peruntaṭṭāṉ eḻutteṉṟum itu maṇalūr
kiḻavaṉ pārutāṅkiṉāṉ eḻutteṉṟum itu kōṭṭaiyūril kāṇiuṭaiya
kōṭināyakaṉ kaimāṭṭāṅkāṉamaikkum ippaṭikkum ivai pūṇṭiyuṭaiyāṉ
tirunāvukkaracāṇṭāṉ eḻuttu ||—

inscription.head:
No. 29.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the east wall of the central shrine in the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra I
8th year = A.D. 1020.

Partly built in. Gift of 96 sheep for a lamp to the temple of Tiruttāṭakēśvaram-uḍaiyār at Tirup-
panandāḷ.

l.ta:
tirumaṉṉivaḷara iru nilamaṭantaiyum pōrccaiyap pāvaiyum cīrttanic
celviyum tanperuntēviyarāki iṉpuṟa neṭuttuyal ūḻiyuḷ iṭai •••
māpperu taṇṭālk koṇṭa kōpparakēcari pa

edition.l:
tiruttāṭakēśvaram uṭaiya[ dēvarkku] paṉaiyūruṭaiyar aḷavili cūṟṟiyāṉ
akamuṭaiyāḷ ve •••maiyāḷ tanmakaṉ cūṟṟiyan tiruviḷaṅkōyilukku • •
viḷakkukaḷukkum eṟiya neyyuḻakkukku āṭu 96 ivvāṭu toṇṇūṟṟāṟum
[ koṇṭu candirātittavaraiyi] lum neyyiṭakkaṭavōm manṟāṭi ivvūr
veṇkāṭa āyaṉum aṇiyanpaṉ •••••••••• ṉum ivviruvom ••••••

inscription.head:
No. 30.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the southern entrance into the central shrine, left side, in the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 18th year = c. 10th Century.

Gift of 90 sheep for a lamp to the temple of Tiruttāṭagai-īśvarattu-Mahādēva at Tirupparandāḷ,
a dēvadāna in Maṇṇi-nāḍu on the north bank by Kōvaṉ Amudaṉ a uḍaiyāṉ of Karuppūr in
the same nāḍu. The second part of this inscription refers to the supply of coconuts from some
coconut gardens for offerings to the deities.
Part I

inscription.head:
No. 31.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the southern entrance into the central shrine, right side, in the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
[Rājēndra I], •••= 1012-44.
inscription.p:
Beginning part lost. Records a gift of land after purchase from the sabhā of the village for
offerings to the god Śivalōkaviṭaṅkadēva and the goddess, by Kīrttirājarājan alias Rājarāja-
Gandaruva-mārāyaṉ. The inscription may belong to Rājēndra I (See No. 34 below).

edition.l: ṅka nilam (1/2)(1/4)- m

inscription.head:
No. 32.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the northern entrance into the central shrine, right side, in the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parāntaka I, 29th year = A.D. 936.

Gift of 90 sheep for a lamp to the temple of Tiruttāṭakēśvarattu-Mahādēva at Tiruppanandāḷ a
dēvadāna in Maṇṇi-nāḍu on the north bank, by Iṟaiyāṉ Mādēvaṉ a uḍaiyāṉ of Kumāramaṅ-
galam in Ambar-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 33.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the northern entrance into the central shrine, left side, in the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parāntaka I, 29th year = A.D. 936.

Gift of land after purchase for offerings to the temple by a Kāmaṉṟāḻi, a kiḻāṉ of Karambiyam
in Eyi-nāḍu on the south bank.

inscription.head:
No. 34.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
[Rājēndra I], •••= 1012-44.
inscription.p:
Beginning of lines built in. Records the consecration of the image of Kshētrapāladēva, made
of metal, by Rājarāja Gandiruva-mārāyar (same person figuring in 31 above) and also a gift
of a piece of land to it for feeding the devotees in a maṭha. The donor originally purchased the
land measuring 12(1/2) vēli from the sabhā of Tirupparandāḷ. Subsequently he got some other
land in exchange with the permission of the king as the original land had become a part of Rā-
jēndranallūr created as dēvadāna by the order of the king. From the mention of the place name
Rājēndranallūr and the title of the donor the king is taken to be Rājēndra I.

l.ta:
muṭaiya
mahātēvar kōyil mārāyaṉ rājarājanāṉa rājarāja kantiruvamārāyar
olōkattāleḻuntaruḷuvitta kṣē

aiyār pakkal aṟupattunāṟkaḻañcu poṉ tiruvulakaḷantaruḷiṉapaṭi nilam
paṉṉiru māvaraikku k

mākak kuṭuttu variyiliṭṭa nilattilē
kuṭukkaveṉṟu tiruvāy moḻintaruḷiṉamaiyil itukku

•••••••••pēr kūvappaṭṭa nilam araiyē yiraṇṭum itil vaṭamēl mūlaiyil
maṉai kāṇi araikāṇi uṭpaṭa

pālatēvarkku canti iraṇṭukku nāḷ
oṉṟukku nelluppatakkāka nāḷ muṉṉūṟṟu aṟupatukku nellu aṟu

le amutu ceyun tēvaraṭiyārkkut tiruvamutukku nellu aṟupatiṉ kalam āka
nellu nūṟṟirupatiṉ kalam ||—

inscription.head:
No. 35.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 20th year = A.D. 1198.

Gift of a piece of dēvadāna land for a lamp by a native of Kaṉṉi-kuḷattūr in Pulivala-nāḍu in
Kāliyūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. It is said the donor reclaimed the land which
had been damaged in floods.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉa cakkaravarttikaḷ śrīkulOttuṅkacOḻa tEvarkku yāṇṭu
irupatāvatu virutarājapuyaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭu maṇṇi nāṭṭu tiru •••[tāḻ] • • vara •

ṇṭa cōḻamaṇṭalattuk kāliyūrkkōṭṭattup pulivala nāṭṭuk
kaṉṉikuḷattūrk koṭiyālamuṭaiyāṉ caṭai[yan] •••••••••• ta

mūvēli nilam veḷḷaṅkoḷḷiyāy orupū naṭṭu varukaiyil itukkuc
cuṟṟukkulaiyaṭṭavun tiruttavum tanta ni •••ḷavu •••ṉṉu •••• muṉṉūṟṟu • • patin

tin kalam iṉnellu muppatin kalattukkum iccuṟṟukkulaiyaṭṭiṉa
nilattiṉ kiḻakkaṭaiya vēli nīkki naṭuvaṭainta vēliyilli • • mu •••nilattukku
vanta antarāyakkācu ••••••māk kācum vēli • • ṭiyum nīkki •••muppatinkala
nellukkoṇṭu

it tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟum cantirātittaval erikka kaṭavitāka •••• makku itu
[paṉmāyēcurakṣai]

inscription.head:
No. 36.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Perumāl Kulaśēkhara, 2nd year, Makara, sudi. 3, Monday, Śravaṇa =
A.D. 1237, December 21 Monday.

Built in in the beginning. Sale of garden (kollai) land to the temple at Tiruppanandāḷ, a brah-
madēya in Maṇṇi-nāḍu in Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu on the north bank by the sabhā of
the village. Several Brāhmaṇas were the kāṇi holders of the sold land.

l.ta:
•••• cakkaravattikaḷ śrīḥ matu •••perumāḷ kulacēkara tēvaṟku yāṇṭu iraṇṭāvatu
makara nāyaṟṟu

[ti]yaiyum tiṅkaḷ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa
tiruvōṇattu nāḷ vaṭakarai virutarāca payaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭu maṇṇi nāṭṭu

yārōm eṅkaḷ kāṇiyāṉa
kollaikaḷāy iṉṉāyanāṟku viṟṟukkuṭutta irātarāca vākkāllukku
vaṭakku kalampūcipperuvaḻikku teṟku araṅkoḻukkaikku mēṟku u[ḷ*]
ḷaṉapaṭi

ikkollai kuḻi iraṇṭāyirattaññūṟum ivvaṉaivarum
viṟṟu paṇam paṟṟikkoṇṭōm §

inscription.head:
No. 37.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, . . ., Kanya, sudi. 8, Sunday,
Utarāshāḍha. = A.D. 1251-70.

Built in at the end. Gift of land for offerings and repair works (kuṟaivaṟuppu) on festival days
in the temple of Tiruttāṭakēśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Tiruppanandāḷ by the sabhā of the vil-
lage. The living quarters of tachchan, ambaṭṭaṉ and Veḷḷāḷaṉ are mentioned separately while
describing the boundaries of the gift land.

edition.l:
svastiśrīḥ cimaṟkittikku mEl kOṟcaṭapaṉmar tirupuvaṉa cakkaravattikaḷ
śrī perumāḷ cuntarapā •••••••••vatu kanni nāyaṟṟu pūrvvapakṣattu
a[ ṣṭamiyum] nāyaṟṟuk kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttirāṭattu nāḷ vaṭakarai virutarāca
payaṅka[ra*] vaḷanāṭṭu maṇṇi nāṭṭu brahmadēśam tiruppaṉantāḷ uṭaiyār
tiruttāṭakīśvaramuṭaiya nāyaṉār kOyil āticaṇḍēśvara tEvakaṉmikaḷukku
tiru[ppaṉa]ntāḷ sabhaiyārOm iṉṉāyanār eḻuntaruḷum tirunāṭkaḷil
[nācciya] •••

l.ta:
eṅkaḷ kuṟaivaṟuppukku amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi tiruviḷakkeṇṇai śrīpātam
tāṅkum āḷukku koṟṟum uṭpaṭa •••tukku nāṅkaḷ tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka
viṭṭa ivvūril iruṉūṟṟu patiṉaṟuvvatikku mēṟku irācātarācavāykkālukku
vaṭakku eṅkaḷutāṉa uṉṉappuṟam

pōkameṉṟu pēr [kūva]ppaṭṭa
vilai nilattukkum kollaikkum kīḻpāṟkellai iṉṉāyanār
tirunāmattukkāṇik kollaikku mēṟkum teṉpāṟkellai irācātarāca
vāykkalukku vaṭakkum mēṟpāṟkel •••

•••ṟ kellaiyum peruvaḻikku teṟku āka iṉṉāṉkellaikku uṭpaṭṭa
vilainilam kollaiyum mikitikkuṟai u •••kuḻi aṟunūṟum ivatikku mēṟku
ivāykkālukku teṟku ūṟkuḷattukku vaṭakaraippaṭṭapaṭṭaippāḻnilattukku
kīḻpāṟkellai taccaṉ ampaṭṭaṉ uḷḷiṭṭa natattukku teṉpāṟkellai
ūṟkuḷattukku nīrpāykiṟa vāykkālukku mēṟkum vaṭakaraiyāy veḷḷāḷaṉ
ṉatattukku pōkiṟavaḻikku vaṭakkum mēlpāṟkellai tiruvītikku kiḻakkum
vaṭapāṟkellai [paṇimakkaḷ ca] ••••

vaḻikku teṟkum āka iṉṉāṉkellaikku uṭpaṭṭa mikitikkuṟai uṭpaṭṭa
paṭṭapāḻ kuḻi

ikkuḻiṉūṟum āka •••••••••ṉācciyār tirunāḷkaḷukku
vēṇṭum kuṟaivaṟuppukkaḷukku uṭalāka kuṭuttōm iṉṉilaṅkaḷ kārum
maṟuvum tiruttōpput tirunantavaṉam uḷḷiṭṭavai payiṟ ceytukkoḷḷavum
iṉṉilaṅkaḷ iṉṉācciyār tirunāḷaikku iṟaiyiliyāka kaḻittut ta[rak]
kaṭavōmmākavum iṉṉāyanār kōyilil tirukkaṭṭaḷaiyilē kallum
veṭṭikkoḷḷak kaṭavārkaḷākavum ippaṭi cammatit •••

aiyārōm •••ippaṭikku
ivai maturāntakaṉ śrīmātavapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭi aṟivēṉ vaṟsan
nārāyaṇa śrīmātavapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai tirumiḻalaiyā •••

inscription.head:
No. 38.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Vīrapāṇḍya (I), 25th year, Vrhśchika, sudi. 14, Sunday,
Pushya = A.D. 1278, January 9, Sunday.
Sale of garden land to the temple by some members of the sabhā of the village.

l.ta:
kOmāṟapaṉmar tirupuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrīHvīrapāṇṭiya tEvaṟku
yāṇṭu

yum •••kkiḻamaiyum
peṟṟa [pūcattunāḷ] vaṭakarai virutarāca payaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭu maṇṇi nāṭṭu
tiruppaṉantāḷ uṭaiyār tiruttāṭakai

ṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu i •••••••••• ykkālukku vaṭakku
araṅkoḻukkaikku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai iṉṉāyanār tiruttOppukku
teṟkum kīḻ •••
ṟkellai iṉṉāyanār tirunāmattukkāṇi ūr nattattukku mēṟkum

ikkuḻi āyirattu muṉṉūṟṟu
orupattiraṇṭum iṉṉāyanāṟku vilaikuṭuttu vilaippaṭi paṇa[m*]

ṉ nārāyaṇapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ivai
tiruviṭaimarutuṭaiyāṉ paṭṭaṉ eḻuttu nārāyaṇaṉ śrīmātē •••

inscription.head:
No. 39.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 9th year = A.D. 1187.

Gift of money by a Nachchiṉāṟkkiṉiyāṉ alias Poṟkōyil-Pichchaṉ to the temple at Tiruppanandāḷ
in Maṇṇi-nāḍu in Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu on the north bank for bathing the deity daily
in pañcha-gavya. Several Śvabrāhmaṇas received the money with promise to maintain the
service as stipulated.

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 40.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 8th year, Makara, sudi. 2, Wednesday, Śravaṇa =
A.D. 1185, December 25 Wednesday.

Built in at the beginning. Gift of land for offerings to the images of Tirukkaḍavūr Kuṅgiliyakka-
laiya-Nāyaṉār and other Śaiva saints set up in the temple at Tiruppanandāḷ, by a devotee of the
temple, called Tīyilum-piriyātāṉ Aḻiyāviradamkoṇḍāṉ alias Kuṅgiliyakkalaiyaṉ.

l.ta:
•••ṉacakkaravaṟttikaḷ śrīkulōttuṅka cōḻa tēvarkku yāṇṭu eṭṭāvatu
makaranāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu

tiyaiyum putaṉkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa tiruvōṇattu
nāḷ virutarāja payaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭu maṇṇi nāṭṭut tirupparantāḷ

•••ī[cu]paramuṭaiyāraicevvē eḻuntaruḷuvitta tirukkaṭavūr kuṅkiliyakkalaiyar
cīpātattukkup paṇiceytirukkum tīyilum piriyātāṉ aḻiyāviratam
koṇṭāṉāṉa kuṅkuliyak kalaiyanēṉ tāṉappiramāṇam eṅkaḷukku
ārmārttamākap pūjai•

•••ṉārkkun tiruvēṭavemperumāḷukkum maṟṟuḷḷa tēvakaḷukkun tiruppaṭi
māṟṟukkuṭalāka śrīpātattu nīrvārttuk kuṭutta nilamāvatu uṭaiyār tiruttā
ṭakai [ī]curamuṭaiyār tēvatāṉam rājēntiracōḻa nallūril kīḻvāykkāṟ
kaṇṭattu eṅkaḷukkuk kiramāketamāṉa iṟaiyili(yā)yāṉa kāṇi

•••innilam oruvēliyum innāyaṉmārkku kāṇi iṟaimikutiyā
luḷḷutum iṟaiyiliyā luḷḷutum tiruppaṭimāṟṟukkuṭalāka śrīpātattu
nīrvārttuk kuṭuttēn tīyilum piriyātāṉ aḻiyāvirataṅkoṇṭāṉāṉa
kuṅkuliyakkalaiyanēṉ ippaṭikku ivai tīyilum piriyātāṉ aḻi-

•••• ṉakuṅkuliyakkalaiyaṉeḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai aḻiyāvirataṅkoṇṭāṉ
tīyilu piriyātāṉeḻuttu ippaṭi aṟivēṉ cāttaṉuṭaiyāṉ
cīruṭaikkaḻale[ḻuttu] ippaṭi aṟivēṉ kīḻaiyiluṭaiyāṉ tiruparantāḷu
ṭaiyānēn ippaṭi aṟivēṉ ātaṉuṭaiyāṉēṉ ippaṭi aṟivēṉ
uṭaiyāraip pūjikkum tiruciṟ•

•••ippaṭi aṟivēṉ nāṟppatteṇṇāyira paṭṭanēṉ ippaṭi aṟivēṉ
parantāḷuṭaiyāṉ piriyātānēn ippaṭi aṟivēṉ cīpātan tāṅku[vaṟ]
kuc cāmutāyam vītiviṭaṅkappiccanēṉ ippaṭi aṟivēṉ tirupparantāḷ
tāṉiuṭaiya sarvakkirutu nā[rā*]yaṇa paṭṭaṉēṉan §

inscription.head:
No. 41.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga III, 9th year, Rhishabha, badi 10, Mon-
day, Pūrva-bhādrapada. = A.D. 1187, May 4, Monday.

Unfinished. Gift of land to the temple by the mahāsabhā of Tirupparandāḻ, a brahmadēya
in Maṇṇi-nāḍu in Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu for the use of the goddess Tirukkāmak-
kōṭṭamuḍaiya-Periyanāchchiyār. Some part of the gift land was set apart for the path to a cre-
matorium (chuḍukāḍu) and for the purpose of the bathing of those visiting the crematorium.

l.ta:
puyalvāyppa vaḷamperuka poyyāta nāṉmaṟaiyiṉ ceyalvāyppat
tirumakaḷuñ jeyamakaḷuñ ciṟantuvāḻa veṇmatipOla kuṭaiviḷaṅka
velvEntaraṭivaṇaṅka maṇmaṭantai maṉaṅkaḷippa manunīti taḻaittOṅka
cakkaramuñ [ce]ṅkOlun tikkaṉaittuñcela naṭappa

vīrapāṇṭiyaṉ makaṉ paṭamē mukappaṭac ciṅkaḷappaṭai mūkkaṟupuṇ ṭalai
kaṭalpuka vīrapāṇṭiyaṉ mutukiṭumpaṭi tākki maturaiyum araicuṅ koṇṭu
jayattampam naṭṭataṟpiṉ ammaturaiyum araicum nāṭum

āviṉ kaṉṟu keṅkātarapaṭṭaṉ paṇiyālum ka-

inscription.head:
No. 42.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Immaḍi •••, s/o Dēvarāya-Mahārāya II,
Śaka 1369, Kshaya = 1446-47.

Damaged and incomplete. Gift of 1/2 vēli of land to the temple of Tiruppanandāḷ in Chēññalūr-Tir-
uppanandāḷ in Maṇṇi-nāḍu in Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu on the north bank. The donor,
a resident of Iḍavai alias Pāṇḍiyaṉai-veṉkaṇḍa-chōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Maṇṇi-nāḍu,
purchased the land from the sabhā of Māṇikkap-padukkaḍai in Iḍavai-paṟṟu for conducting
the festival of Mārgaḻi-Tiruvādirai in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 43.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the east wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga II, •••= A.D. 1138.

Badly worn out. The sabhā of a Chaturvēdimaṅgalam (name lost) is said to have made some
temple land tax-free in lieu of some money that they had borrowed from the temple in the 15th
year of Vikrama-chōḻa, that is in 1133. Most probably the purpose of the earlier borrowal was
to pay off tax arrears (See No. 4 for comparison).

l.ta:
puṟattiṉuṅ k[e]ṭattaṉi •••••••••kālinitulāva mīṉamuñ cilaiyuñ citaintu
vāṉuyarap poṉṉ[e]ṭu nemi • • li vīṟṟiruppa ampariyāṉai yo •••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 44.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the east wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja II, 3rd year = A.D. 1149.

Restoration of some land as ūrkīḻ-iṟaiyili to the temple of Tiruvajanīśvaram-uḍaiyār at Tirup-
panandāḷ on king's order to the sabhā of the place. It seems the land had been assigned for the
enjoyment of the sabhā's magaṉār (chief servant ?) until the 15th year (of the previous king).
The tirumandira-ōlai Paṉaiyūr-uḍayāṉ wrote the royal order and the other official signatories
were Vayirādarāyaṉ, Virāḍarājaṉ and Toṇḍaimāṉ.

l.ta:
rkkut tāṅkaḷ makaṉārkkup patiṉaiñcāvatuvarai viṭṭuvanta nilam
mūṉṟāvatu mutal paḻampaṭiyē ittēvarkku ūr kīḻiṟaiyiliyāy niṟka

inscription.head:
No. 45.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājarāja II, •••, Makara,
badi 10, Monday, Viśāka = A.D. 1146-73.

Gift of land in the northern hamlet called Āṉaipōki by the sabhā of some neighbour village
(name lost) in Miḻalai-nāḍu in Virudarāja-bhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu for opening a road, called Rā-
jagambīran through which the god of Tiruppaṉandāḷ temple had to be taken in procession to
the river Koḷḷiḍam on festive days. Detailed measurements of the road are given. The sabha also
arranged to plant 750 coconut saplings on both sides of the road. Nearly eighty Brāhmaṇa
members of the sabhā have signed the document.

inscription.head:
No. 46.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, 29th year = A.D. 1099.

Registers the sale by a temple priest (Śivabrāhmaṇa) of his right of worship to recoup a portion
of money which he had misappropriated from the temple treasury (in the form of gold and silver
ornaments, bronze utensils, etc.). He and several others of his colleagues had been habitual
offenders since long as known from previous official investigations carried out respectively by
Rājarāja-mūvēndavēḷār, the Sēnāpati Pallavarājar and the Sēnāpati Nandiyarājar in the 3rd
year of Adhirājēdra (1070), and in the 8th and 21st years of the present king (1078 and 1091
respectively). On the present occasion while the king visited the temple for worship he was
informed about the disruption of services due to the misappropriation of the temple funds
by the Śivabrāhmaṇas and thereupon he ordered for the recovery of the pilfered property.

nūṟṟaimpatiṉāl niṟai aiṅkaḻañcu veṇkalavuru patinēḻināl niṟai irunūṟṟu
aṟupatteṇ palane kaicum cempuru añcināl niṟai nūṟṟaiympalamum
tarāvuru eṭṭiṉāl niṟai muṉṉūṟṟu muppattaṟu

palamum tiripuvaṉacakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulōttuṅka cōḻatēvarkku yāṇṭu
eṭṭāvatu sēnāpatikaḷ pallavarājar ittēvar paṇṭārañ cōtittaviṭattu
nāṅkaḷ okkakkoṇṭu kaṭavōmāṉa ceppuru [nā]liṉāl niṟai
eṇpattoṉpatin palamum veṇkalavurup patināṟiṉāl niṟai irunūṟ-

ṟu muppattonpatinpalanē kaicum muttup patinaiñcināl niṟai araikkaḻañcē
mūṉṟu mañcāṭiyu mūṉṟumāvum tarāvuru mūṉṟināl niṟai irunūṟṟeḻupattoru
palamum yāṇṭu irupattoṉṟāvatu sēṉāpatikaḷ naṉtiyarājar ittēvar
paṇṭārañ cōtitta viṭattu nāṅkaḷ

aḻittu okkak koṇṭu kaṭavōmāṉa poṉ patinaiṅ kaḻañcē oṉpatu
mañcāṭiyu mūṉṟumā nūṟṟu nāṟpattēḻu māvum veṇkala niṟai irunūṟṟu
nāṟpa[ttē]ḻu [pa]lanē muppalamum veḷḷiy • •

l niṟai irupatiṉ palamum ākap poṉ muppattaṟu kaḻañcaraiyē mūṉṟu
mañcāṭiyu mūṉṟu māvukku kācu oṉṟukkup poṉ araikaḻañcākak kācu
eḻupattu mūṉṟē aṟumāvaraiyum [mu]ttu nūṟṟiṟupattaiñcināl niṟai
aiṅkaḻañcaraiyu mūṉṟu mañcāṭiyu mūṉṟumāvukku

[samma]titta kācu e •••••••••vum veṇkala niṟai eḻunūṟṟaimppattu
nāṟpalanē kaicukku kācu oṉṟukku veṇkala niṟai muppattaiympalamākak
kācu irupattoṉṟaraiyē orumāvum cempu niṟai irunūṟṟu
aṟupattoṉpatinpalanē kaicukku kācu oṉṟukku cempu niṟai cempu niṟai
muppatin palamāka kācu

•••m veḷḷiy niṟai irupatiṉ palattukku kācu mukkālum tarā niṟai āyirat
tirunūṟṟirupatiṉ palattukku kācu oṉṟukkut tarā niṟai eḻupatin palamākak
kācu patinēḻē eṭṭu māvum ākak kācu nūṟṟirupattoṉpatē orumāvarai itu
tiṅkaḷ vaṭṭattu nāḷoṉṟukkuk kācu nālē āṟu

muntirikaiyāka eṉ nāḷ nālaraikkuk kācu pattoṉpatē eḻumākkāṇik
kīḻaraiyumāka nāṉ kaṭavēnāṉa kācu muppattiraṇṭē mukkālē iraṇṭu
mākkāṇikkīḻarai ikkācu eṉakku vaikka vakai illāmaiyil eṉ nāḷ
nālaraiyaiyum nāḷ [oṉ]ṟukkuk kācu añcāka iṭṭu ikkōyili[l]

varkku nāṉ viṟṟatu kaikkoṇṭu eṉṉai rakṣikka
vēṇṭumeṉṟu ikkōyilil

rai vēṇṭik koṇṭu en[nāḷ]
nālaraikku nāḷ oṉṟukku kācu añcākak kācu irupattiraṇṭarai ikkācu
irupattiraṇṭaraikkum ikkōyi[li]lē āticaṇṭēycuvara tēvarkku viṟṟu

pan pāṇṭan [kuma]rar cuvāmiyēṉ ippāṇṭan kumāra
cuvāmiyār paṇikka ikkāṇi vilai[ppi]ramāṇam eḻutinēṉ yivvūr

ṉ
paṉantāḷuṭaiyāṉ kuṭi tāṅkiyāṉa va[rī]ca[ṉa]ṉ eḻuttu i •••••••••••••••

paṉ pāṇṭaṉ kumāracuvāmiyēṉ ivai enneḻuttu ippaṭi aṟivēṉ
tirupparantāḷ vācciyaṉ ūruṭaiya perumānārāyanēṉ ippaṭi aṟivēṉ
vācciyaṉ utaiya tivākaraṉ tattapaṭṭanēṉ ippaṭi aṟivēṉ ivvūr

ippaṭi aṟivēṉ puravuvaritiṇaikkaḷak kaṇakku neṟkuppai uṭaiyāṉ
vēḷāṉ •••uṭaiyānēṉ ivai en eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 47.
Tiruppanandāḷ, Kumbakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the first prākāra of the Arjunajaṭēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
This is related to the above inscription and seems to be in continuation of the above one as
it abruptly begins with the year 29th of the king, obviously Kulōttuṅga I (1099). It records
similar sales of their right of worship by five other temple priests on account of similar misap-
propriations.

l.ta:
vāhaṉanēṉ nāṉum ikkōyilil ittēvar cātti yaruḷun
tiruvāparaṇam poṉṉilum parikala paricciṉṉaṅkaḷilu makappaṭa nāṉum nā • • •

pāti aḻittukkoṇṭu kaṭamaikku kaṭavēnāṉa kācu irupattoṉpatē
nālumākkāṇi ikkācu irupattoṉpatē nālumākkāṇiyum
iṟukkamāṭṭāmaiyil ikkācilē irupatu kācukku nāṉ ikkōyililt
tiṅkaḷ vaṭṭattuḷēy nāḷ nā • • •

laiyum ikkōyilil āticaṇṭēycuvaratēvarkku viṟṟukkuṭutta
piramāṇapparicuram kā

pan pāṇṭaṉ kumāracuvāmi viṟṟukuṭutta
piramāṇapparicurappaṭiyē itukkum piramāṇam eḻutinēṉ paṉantāḷuṭaiyāṉ
kuṭi tāṅki vāṉavaricanēṉ ivai in eḻuttu

vāhanaṉ sa •••maikku ivai pā[ṇ*]
ṭan kumāra

mi eḻuttu ippaṭi aṟivēṉ tirupparantāḷ vācciyaṉ
utaiya tivākaraṉ tattapaṭṭanēṉ ippaṭi aṟivēṉ vācciyanūruṭaip
perumānārāyaṇanēṉ ippaṭi aṟivēṉ

ṉ neṭṭuruvaṉ cūriyatēvaṉēṉ ippaṭi aṟivēṉ puravuvari
tiṇaikkaḷattuk kaṇakku neṟkuppai uṭaiya •••ṉ ivai eṉ eḻuttu § yāṇṭu
irupatteṭṭāvite ikkōyilil civa

tēva paṭṭanēṉ nāṉum ikkōyilili[ttē]var cātti[ya]ruḷun tiruvāparaṇam
poṉṉilum parikala paricciṉṉaṅkaḷilum •••e •••aḻittu koṇṭu kaṭamaikku
kaṭavēnāṉa kācu muppattāṟaraiyē kāṇi muntirikai ikkācu muppattāṟaraiyē kā

ṇi muntirikaiyum iṟukka[muṭiyā]maiyil ik •••lē irupattaiñcu kācukku
nāṉ ikkōyililt tiṅkaḷ vaṭṭattuṭaiya nāḷ añcaiyum ikkōyilil
āticaṇṭēycuvara tēvarkku viṟṟukkuṭuttēṉ kā

paṉ tēvaṉ
mātēvapaṭṭanēṉ nāṉ viṟṟukkuṭutta piramā-

tiṇaikkaḷa-

ttukkaṇakku neṟkuppaiyuṭaiyānēṉ ivai enneḻuttu § yāṇṭu
irupatteṭṭāvitē ikkōyilil civa

ṇan ātaraiyaṉ civaṉ kā •••
raṉum ivaṉ tampi civaṉ •••ṉum ivaṉ tampi civaṉ muttaṉum immūvōm

tēvar cāttiyaruḷun tiruvāparaṇam poṉṉilum parikala paricciṉṉa[ṅ*]

kācu muppatteṭṭē āṟumā mukkāṇi muntirikaik kīḻkkāl ikkācu vaikka
māṭṭāmaiyil ikkāci-

lē irupattāṟēkālk kācukku eṅkaḷ nāḷ añcēkālaiyum ikkōyilil
tiruttāṭakacuvaratēvar ••••••••••••kkuṭuttōm immūvōm nāṅkaḷ viṟṟukku[ṭu*]
tta piramāṇapparicu kā

• • muttu āttiraiyaṉ •••• āṉamaikku ivai kuṭi tāṅki vāṉavaricaneḻuttu

ramuṭaiyāṉ sa • • āṉa • • i •••puravuvari tiṇaikkaḷattuk kaṇakku

aṟivēn • •

vaikkaveṉṟu nāṅkaḷ aṭai •••ciṟaiya • • lil •••ntāmōtiraṉum •••tāmōtiraṉ
tēvaṉum oḷittup po •••

inscription.head:
No. 48.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pallava, Nandivarman II, 22nd year = A.D. 753.

The characters of the inscription belong to the eleventh century. In fact it is stated at the end that
this is a copy of an earlier stone inscription. A woman called Chandippeliyār purchased land
from the sabhā of Tiribuvanamādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam as ūrkīḻ-iṟaiyili and endowed it for
offerings and a lamp, to the temple of Tiruvaigāvuḍaiya-Mahādēva.
(Published in SII, Vol. XII, No. 58).

l.ta: aiyārpakkal poṉ kuṭuttup polik kūlikkuc cela-

vākak kācu koḷḷā ūṟkiḻ iṟaiyiliyāka maṇalīṭil ••••••vēliyākak
koṇṭu viṭṭa vaṇṇakka viḷākam nilam
raiyum iṟakkātān tiruvaṭi iraṇṭu mentalaimēlaṉa itu iṟakkuvān taṅka

inscription.head:
No. 49.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman [mistake for Rājakēsarivarman] Kulōttuṅga II,
5th year = A.D. 1138.

Incomplete. It seems to record a gift by an individual of Moykuḍi in Ēmappērūr-nāḍu in Rājarā-
ja-vaḷanāḍu to the temple of Tiruvaigāvuḍaiyār in Iṉṉambar-nāḍu, in Vikramachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu
on the north bank.

l.ta:
pūmEvu vaḷara tiruponmātu puṇara nāmEvu kalai makaḷ nalam peritu
ciṟappa vicaiyaṉ maṉa

makaḷ velpuyattiruppa icai[yiṉ] celviyeṇṭicai viḷaṅka nirupar
vantiṟaiñca naṇṇilamaṭantaiyait tirumaṇa-

maṇam puṇantu cīrvaḷa tirumaṇimuṭi kavitteṉa aṇi muṭicūṭi mallaiñālattu
palluyiṟ kellā mellaiyil-

liṉpam miyalviṉil leyta veṇkuṭainiḻali ceṅkōllōcci vāḻiyūḻi
yāḻi naṭappa cempoṉ vīraciṅkāsaṉattu pu-

yāṇṭu aiñcāvatu vaṭakarai vikkiramacōḻavaḷanāṭṭu iṉṉampa nāṭṭu
uṭaiyār tiruvaikkāvuṭaiyāṟku rājarājavaḷa-
nāṭṭu ēmappēṟūr nāṭṭu moykuṭi moykuṭaiyāṉ tiru • • •

inscription.head:
No. 50.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājarāja II, 11th year = A.D. 1157.

Gift of 30 kasu for a lamp by a dancing girl called Taḍuttāṭkoṇḍāḷ Chaḍaimēlirundāḷ alias
Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-naṅgai of the temple of Tiruvaigāvuḍaiyār in Aṇḍāṭṭu-kūṟṟam of Vikramachōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
pūmaruviya tirumātum puvimātuñ ceyamātu nāmaruviya kalaimātum
pukaḻmātum nayantu pulka arumaṟaivi-

ti neṟiyanaittum taḻaippa varumuṟai urimai •••maṇimuṭicūṭi tiṅkaḷ
veṇkuṭai ticaikaḷiṟeṭṭun taṅkutaṉikūṭaṉ tāṉeṉa vi-

ḷaṅka karuṅkali paṭṭiyai ceṅkōl turappa peruṅkatirāḻi pulivaḷattuṭaṉ vara
villavariraṭṭa mīnava ciṅkaṇar pallava-

nutaliyar pārttiva paṇiya eṇṇaruṅkaṟpil maṇṇakam purantu cempon
vīrasiṅṅāsanattu puvaṉamuḻutuṭaiyā-

ḷōṭum vīṟṟirun taruḷiya kōpparakēcaripanmarāna tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ
śrīirājarājatēvarkku yā-

āvatu vikkirama cōḻavaḷanāṭṭu aṇṭāṭṭukkūṟṟattu uṭaiyār
tiruvaikāuṭaiyār kōyil muppatu vaṭṭattu

civappirāmaṇarōm ikkōyil tēvaraṭiyāḷ taṭuttāṭ koṇṭāḷ
caṭaimēliruntāḷāṉa nāṟpatteṇṇāyira naṅkai pakkal

it tiruṉuntāviḷakku onṟum santirātittavarai
erikka kaṭavomāka sammatittōm

inscription.head:
No. 51.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēśvara temple
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman [mistake for Rājakēsarivarman] Kulōttuṅga I, 40th
year = A.D. 1110.

Records that a Pūṇḍi-uḍaiyāṉ Sūriyaṉ Pavaḻakkuṉṟaṉār alias Vaṉṉāḍuḍaiyār rebuilt in
stone the old brick temple of Tiruvaigāvuḍaiya-Mahādēva in Aṇḍāṭṭu-kūṟṟam in Uyyak-
koṇḍachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. He hailed from Paravaichchuṟṟu-Pūṇḍi in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu. He also
added, with the king's permission, to the dēvadāna of the temple by a gift of land in Kaḷap-
pākuḍi in Viṟai-kūṟṟam, a unit of Ulaguyyavandachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The additional land mea-
sured 20 and odd vēli, yielding 1200 and odd kalam of paddy.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ puṇara
urimaiyiṟ ciṟanta muṭicūṭi mīnavar nilai keṭa villavar kulai-

tara ēṉaimaṉnavar iḻiyaluṟ ṟiḻitarat tikkaṉaittun taṉ cakkara naṭātti
vīrasiṅṅācaṉattu avaṉi muḻutuṭaiyāḷōṭum vīṟṟiruntaruḷiya kōppa-

[ra]kēcari paṟmarāṉa tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulōttuṅkacōḻa tēvaṟkku
yāṇṭu nāṟpatāvatu rājarājavaḷa nāṭṭup paravaiccuṟṟup

pūṇṭip pūṇṭiuṭaiyāṉ cūriyan pavaḻakku[ṉ]ṟiṉārāna vaṉṉāṭuṭaiyār
ulakuyyavanta cōḻa vaḷanāṭṭu aṇṭāṭṭuk kūṟṟattu uṭaiyār
tiruvaikāvuṭai-

kaiyāy jīnnittamaiyil ikkōyil
iḻiccit tirukaṟṟaḷiyāka ceykaiku yāṇṭu muppattiraṇṭāva-

kai iḻiccivittu tirukkaṟṟaḷiyum tiruviṭaikkaṭṭum
tirumaṇṭapamum ceyvittu ittēvar paḻantēvatānam[ā]-

na nilattu nellut tiruppaṭimāṟṟukkum nimantattukkum pōtāmaiyil
tēvatānam peṟukaikku viṇṇappañ ceytu ulakuya vanta cōḻavaḷa-

nāṭṭu viṟaikkūṟṟattu kaḷappākkuṭi pottakappaṭi nilam irupatē cinnattāl
nellu āyirattu irunūṟṟuc cinnamum tēvatānam[ā]-

ka iṭuvittu nivantañcella paṇṇuvittār rājarājavaḷanāṭṭup
paravaiccuṟṟup pūṇṭi pūṇṭi uṭaiyār cūriyaṉ pavaḻakkuṉṟiṉārā-

na vaṉṉāṭuṭaiyār ivar colla ittiruppaṇi ceyvittār ikkōyilil
śrīmāyē

inscription.head:
No. 52.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III, 7th year = A.D. 1223.

An individual from Paḻavālakkuḍi in Viḷai-nāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu made a gift of
gold for three lamps to be lit in the shrine of Tirumāḷigai-Piḷḷaiyār.

l.ta:
tiripuvanac[cak]karavattikaḷ śrīrājarājatēvarkku yāṇṭu ēḻāvatu vikkiramacōḻa
vaḷanāṭṭu aṇṭāṭṭukkūṟṟattu

uṭaiyār tiruvaikāvuṭaiyār kōyilil eḻuntaruḷi [irukkum] tirumāḷikaip
piḷḷaiyārkku jayaṅ-

(ṅ)koṇṭacōḻa vaḷanāṭṭu viḷaināṭṭu paḻavālakkuṭi paḻavālaṅkuṭaiyāṉ
nakkaṉ irāman pakkal muppatu

vaṭṭattu civappirāmaṇarōm pon koṇṭu po •••cantirātittavar
celuttakkaṭavōmāka vaitta santi

inscription.head:
No. 53.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājarāja II, 17th year = A.D. 1163.

This is partly built in and records a gift of money (200 kāsu) for offerings to the image of Nit-
tavinōda-kalyāṇasundara-dēva in the temple of Tiruvaigāvūr. The donor (name lost) is men-
tioned as a son of the aṇukkiyār Araṅga-patāgaiyār.

l.ta:
pūmaru[viya tirumātum] puvimātum jayamātum nāmaruviya kalaimātum
pukaḻmātum nayantu pulka arumaṟai vi-

ti neṟiyaṉaittum [taḻaippa varumuṟai] urimaiyil maṇimuṭicūṭic ceṅkōl
ceṉṟu [ticai t]ōṟum vaḷaṟppa veṅkali nī-

kki meyyaṟan taḻaippa [kaliṅka]meriyak kaṭalmalai naṭātti valaṅkoḷḷāḻi
varaiyāḻi naṭappa irucuṭaraḷavum oru kuṭai niḻaṟkīḻ ce-

vatu vikkiramacōḻa vaḷanāṭṭu aṇṭāṭṭukkūṟṟattu uṭaiyār
tiruvaikāvuṭaiyārkōyil śrīmāhē

ḷum śrīkāriyam ceyvārkaḷu tēvarkanmikaḷum kōyil kaṇakkaṉum uḷḷiṭṭa
tānattōm aṇukkiyār araṅka patākaiyār makaṉ uṭaiyā-

•••ikkōyilil eḻuntaruḷivitta nittavinōta kalliyāṇacuntara tēvaṟkum
nācciyārkkum amutu paṭikkum viñcaṉattukkum

inscription.head:
No. 54.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the south and west walls of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēś-
vara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 20th year = A.D. 1198.

The trustees of the temple received 30 kalam of paddy from Nānāndūr-uḍaiyār Pugalivēndar,
in lieu of which they remitted permanently the taxes due to temple from the monastery (gu-
hai) called Tirumuḍiyāl-naḍandāṉ that was presided over by the donor. The paddy was set
apart for the wages of the stone masons engaged for some renovation work in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 55.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja II, 7th year = A.D. 1153.

Unfinished. Mentions some land gift by Tillaināyakaṉ Kaṇavadi-nambi alias Madhurāntaka
Vaṉṉāḍuḍaiyāṉ of Paravaichchuṟṟu-Pūṇḍi in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu.

tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī rājarāja tēvarkku yāṇṭu ēḻāvatu vikkiramacōḻa
vaḷanāṭṭu aṇṭāṭṭuk kūṟṟattu u-

ka vaṉṉāṭuṭaiyāṉ ikkōyil tāṉattārku upaiyaṅkuṭutta paricāvatu
ivaṉ pitākkoṇṭu ṭaiyarāy ivar apāpattil • • •

inscription.head:
No. 56.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājēndra III, 5th, Mēsha, sudi 4, Monday, Rōhiṇi
= A.D. 1251, March 27, Monday.

Sale of land by the trustees of the temple of Tiruvaikāvuḍaiyār to Vāmaśiva-Mudaliyār of the
lineage of the Mudaliyārs of Chelva-Tiruvārūr (maṭha). The sale amount of 3000 kāsu was
meant for the supply of unguents to the image of Villavana-Perumāḷ in this temple.

inscription.head:
No. 57.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Tribhuvanavīradēva (Kulōttuṅga III), 33rd year, Mithuna, badi,
9, Monday, Rēvati = A.D. 1211, June 6, Monday.

A woman called Andappulaperumāḷ wife of Anapāyavaṇukkaṉ and her daughter had to sell
their dēvadāna land in Edirilichōḻa-nallūr, measuring one vēli and odd, and two houses for
28,800 kāsu for paying off the arrears of the taxes due on their holding. For the sale, one
Chērapāṇḍiyan Tambirān-aṇukkaṉ acted as the male guardian (mudukaṇ). The default in the
payment was found out by Piḷḷai Muṉaiyadaraiyar who was in charge of the temple lands, by
auditing the accounts from the 24th to 33rd year (of the king).

inscription.head:
No. 58.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Tribhuvanavīradēva (Kulōttuṅga III), 33rd year, Mithuna, badi, 9, Monday,
Rēvati = A.D. 1211, June 6, Monday.

Lines built in and incomplete. A sale of land for tax arrears, similar to that of the above inscrip-
tion (No. 57), by the daughter mentioned therein. This land was located in the dēvadāna village
Tribhuvanamādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam. It is said the seller had got the land from her mother
as śrīdhanam. Built in at the bottom.

muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇṭu vīrar

inscription.head:
No. 59.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bilvanātēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Dēvarāya-Mahārāya, Śaka 1351, Saumya, Vṛśchika, badi 10,
Monday, Hasta = A.D. 1429, October 21, Monday.

This is said to be a solidarity (orimai) deed made by a big assembly of the sabhaiyār of Śrī-parān-
taka-nāḍu of Iṉṉambar-nāḍu a unit of Vikkiramachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu on the north bank and and the va-
laṅgai-98 and the iḍaṅgai-98 communities opposing the unjust taxation of the government and the
exploiting nature of the jīvitam holders. The assembly decided that hereafter they should not yield
to the official and other exploitation and should pay the government taxes (irājagaram iṟai-muṟai-
mai) on their land and on various professions as fixed in this meeting. The detailed enumeration
of the rates of taxes is given. It is said that the assembled members came to the decision encour-
aged by an earlier revolt of their brethren elsewhere in several nāḍus of Vaḻudalampaṭṭu Uchāvaḍi.
To celebrate the solidarity achievement, the assembly decided to institute a service named Peri-
yanāṭṭāṉ-sandhi in the temple of Tiruvaigāvuḍaiya-nāyaṉār (where the inscription is put up) with
income from a vēli of land in each of the temple villages (taḷigai ūr).

inscription.head:
No. 60.
Tiruvaigāvūr, Papanasam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
On the east and north walls of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Bilvanātēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 33rd year, Mithuna, badi, 9, Monday, Rēvati =
A.D. 1211, June 6, Monday.

Incomplete. Three individuals were in default in payment of their kaḍamai and kuḍimai on their
dēvadāna kāṇi holdings from the 24th to 33rd year of the king. Hence they had to sell their
land to the temple of Tiruvaigāvūr as tirunāmattukkāṇi and deposited the money from the sale
proceeds in the temple treasury itself. The tax arrears were fixed by the officer Piḷḷai Muṉai-
yadaraiyar (see 57 above). Similar to the transactions mentioned in 57 and 58 above.

inscription.head:
No. 61.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 17th year = A.D. 1195.

There is mix-up of two different inscriptions in this. The first five lines give a part of the meyk-
kītti of Rājēndra I. The remaining lines (starting from the second half of line 5) form the actual
document belonging to the time of Kulōttuṅga III (also known as Vīrarājēndra). The king
made the village of Vīrarājēndra-nallūr measuring 52 and odd vēli as tax-free dēvabhōga to
the Mahādēva temple of Tirunallūr. It is said that this village which should have been a part
of Vikkiramachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu by some mistake was now lying as part of Kiḍāraṅgoṇḍachōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu and on representation of the piḍāraṉ (a temple official) of the above temple, the king
ordered for the restoration of the previous status of the village. Several officials are found to
be involved in the transaction.

inscription.head:
No. 62.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Rājarāja I, 20th year, Kanya, Wednesday, Kṛittikā =
A.D. 1004, September 29, Wednesday.

This is just a fragment referring to the kūṭṭa-perumakkaḷ (sabhā) containing 80 members which
met to decide something on king's order (Āḻvār niyōgam).

inscription.head:
No. 63.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Rājarājakēsarivarman Rājarāja I,
29th year = A.D. 1014.

Gift of 15 kaḻañju of gold, by two madhyasthas of the village, for a lamp to the temple of
Tirunallūr-Mahādēva at Kāmaravalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Miṟai-kūṟṟam in
Rājēndrasimha-vaḷanāḍu on the north bank.

inscription.head:
No. 64.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Rājarājakēsarivarman Rājarāja I,
26th year = A.D. 1014.

Gift of 10 kaḻañju of gold by a madhyastha of the brahmadēya village Kāmaravalli-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam in Viṟai-kūṟṟam for a lamp to the Tirukkārkōṭakīśvaram temple at Tirunallūr in the
same village.

inscription.head:
No. 65.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra I, 29th year = A.D. 1041.

The peruṅguṟi-perumakkaḷ (mahāsabhā) of Kāmaravalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, heeding to the
divine wishes, assigned land to the dancer Chākkai Mārāyaṉ Vikramachōḻaṉ for performing the
dance chākkai-kūttu in the temple of Tirukāṟkōṭaka-Īśvaram on Tiruvādirai festival days in the
months of Mārgaḻi and Vaigāsi. Four members one from each of the four quarters (chēri) of the
village signed the document on behalf of the assembly.

l.ta:

celviyum taṉperuntEviyarāki iṉpuṟa neṭutuyaramuyalliṭai tuṟaināṭun

inscription.head:
No. 66.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north, west and south walls of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, 27th year, Tulā, sudi 10, Sunday,
Śatabhisaj = A.D. 1097, October 18, Sunday.

The mahāsabhā of Kāmaravalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Viṟai-kūṟṟam of Gēyavinōda-vaḷanāḍu
decided to assign some temple land lying fallow over thirty years to a temple servant for re-
claiming it and making a temple garden.

inscription.head:
No. 67.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, [Rājarāja III], 17th year = A.D. 1233.

This is a counterpart of the record found in No. 71 below. On king's order the dēvadāna land
that was held under cultivation lease (uḻavaḍai) by a Kurugāḍi-Kiḻaiyān Kūttan who had mar-
ried the sister of Kiḷinallūr-kiḻavan Āḍavallāṉ, was sold in royal auction (Rājarāja-peruvilai)
and the sale amount was deposited in the government treasury.

l.ta:
ṭavallāṉu mivaṉaṭutta uṟavumuṟaiyārum kāṇiyu maṭimaiyum rājarājap
peruvilai

viṟai-

inscription.head:
No. 68.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa Rājarāja-Rājakēsarivarman (Rājarāja I), 15th year = A.D. 1000.

Gift of land by the madhyastha called Nārāyaṇaṉ Nānūṟṟuvaṉ alias Śrīragavap-peruṅkāviti for
the offering of akkāraḍalai on the festival day of Mārgaḻi-tiruvādirai.

inscription.head:
No. 69.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājarāja-Rājakēsarivarman (Rājarāja I), 22nd year, Dhanus, sudi 9,
Monday, Rēvati = A.D. 1006, December 2, Monday.

The mahāsabhā of Kāmaravalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Viṟai-kūṟṟam in Rājēndrasim-
ha-vaḷanāḍu gifted red lotuses (cheṅgaḻunīr pū) from all the tanks and ponds in their village for
offering to the deity in the Mahādēva temple of Tirunallūr daily during the month of Dhanus.

tirumakaḷ pOla perunilac celviyum taṉakkEyurimai pūṇṭamai
manakkoḷa kāntaḷūr cālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi vEṅkai

inscription.head:
No. 70.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Rājarāja I, 24th year = A.D. 1009.

Gift of land for sandal paste to the Tirunallūr-Mahādēva of the temple of Tirukkārkōṭīśvaram
at Kāmaravalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam by a resident of Rājakēsari-chēri, whose ancestral place
was Priyadhīra-maṅgalam.

l.ta:
tirumakaḷ pOlap perunilac celviyun taṉakkE yurimai pūṇṭamai
maṉakkoḷa kāntaḷūrccālai kalamaṟut taruḷi vEṅkai nāṭuṅ ka-

kaliṅkamum eṇṭicai pukaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamum raṭṭapāṭi ēḻaraiyilakkamum ti-

inscription.head:
No. 71.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III, 17th year = A.D. 1233.

On king's order the dēvadāna land that was held as cultivation lease (uḻavaḍai) by a Ku-
rugāḍi-Kiḻaiyān Kūttan who married the sister of Kiḷinallūr-kiḻavan Āḍavallāṉ, was sold in
royal auction (Rājarāja-peruvilai) and the sale amount was deposited in the government treasury.
It is said that the sale was made according to a royal order which said that since Kiḷinallūr-kiḻa-
vaṉ had become a traitor all his and his relatives' properties including their kāṇi and slaves
should be sold off in public auction under the supervision of the officials Jinattaraiyaṉ and the
puravuvari-sīkaraṇanāyagam Paḻaiyanūr-uḍaiyāṉ. The land sold in the present instance was in
Vaṇṇam alias Madurāntaka-nallūr, a tirunāmattukkāṇi village of the temple of Kārkōṭīśvaram
in Viṟai-kūṟṟam of Vikramachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. [For a corollary of this record see 67 above].

l.ta:
l vikkarama cOḻavaḷanāṭṭu
viṟaik kū[ṟ]ṟattu kāmatavalliccaruppEti maṅkalattu tirukkāṟkOṭi

ra tEvar tirunāmattāl rājarājapperuvilai

ppuṇṭa kiḷinallūr kiḻavaṉ āṭavallāṉ kāṇiyumaṭimaiyum ivanaṭutta
uṟavu muṟaiyār kāṇiyumaṭimaiyum jinattaraiyaṉum puravaricikaraṇa nāyakam
paḻaiyanūruṭaiyānu[m] vilaimatittapaṭi kācōṭukkik koḷvāṟkku
rājarājāpperuvilai viṟṟu ivarkaḷeḻuttiṭṭa

namaiyil kiḷina[l]lūr kiḻavan āṭavallāṉ uṭaṉ piṟantāḷaikkoṇṭa
kurukāṭi kiḻaiyāṉ kkūttanukku vikkaramacōḻa vaḷanāṭṭu viṟaikkūṟṟattu

inscription.head:
No. 72.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west and south walls of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra I, 8th year, Tulā, Friday, 9, Śravaṇa =
A.D. 1019, October 9, Friday.

The peruṅguṟi-perumakkaḷ of Kāmaravalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam met at the temple of Kay-
ilāsamuḍaiyār in the village and decided to pay taxes themselves on some temple land. In case
some persons transgressed this decision they were liable to pay each a sum of 100 poṉ indi-
vidually to the king (chakravatti) towards the kaṟpūravilai. The first line contains the Sanskrit
passage usually found on the seal of the king's copper plate grants. The writing has many errors.

l.gr:
drājan makuṭa śrēṇi ratnēṣu śāsanam ētandrājēndracōḷasya
parakēsari varmaṇaḥ svastiśrī

tirumaṉṉi[vaḷara] iru ni[la maṭantai]yum pOr

inscription.head:
No. 73.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west and south walls of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, 26th year, Makara, sudi 13, Thursday,
Ārdrā = A.D. 1096, January 10, Thursday.

This records the provision made by the peruṅguṟi-sabhā of the village of Kāmaraval-
li-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Viṟai-kūṟṟam in Gēyavinōda-vaḷanāḍu for the Tai-pūsam festival and
the śrī-bali service in the temple of Śrīkailāsamuḍaiyār in the village. It was decided for the
purpose to collect one kuṟuṇi of paddy from each mā of all the cultivated land in the village
and also to collect an uḻakku of oil from each house of the Veḷḷāḷas in the main village as well
as in the hamlets. The concerned vāriyam has to take itself the responsibility of the collections.

l.ta:
nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ puṇara urimaiyil ciṟanta maṇimuṭi cūṭi mīnavar nilai
keṭa villavar

inscription.head:
No. 74.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 5th year, Makara, Friday, Punarvasu = c. 10th Century.

Gift of land by purchase by Pālāśiriya-bhaṭṭan Sīlaṉ Kūttaṉ of Ādaṉūr, a dēvadāna and brah-
madēya in Iṉṉambar-nāḍu for arranging the burning of sidāri incense in the Tirunallūr Mahādē-
va temple. The sidāri incense is said to have included the aromatic substances of agil, sandal,
camphor, neriyāsam, and honey. The peruṅguṟi-perumakkaḷ of the village made the land tax-
free on receiving the tax capital from the donor.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 109.]

l.ta:
ti maṅkalattu peruṅkuṟi perumakkaḷOm ivvāṭṭai
makara nāyaṟṟu veḷḷi kiḻamai peṟṟa puṇarpūcattināṉṟu iraṭṭaikkāḷamūti

eṉ eḻuttu itupa

inscription.head:
No. 75.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 9th-10th Century.
Gift of two stones for the construction of the temple by a Kāḍaṉ Aḍigaḷ of Arunalūr.

inscription.head:
No. 76.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja-Rājakēsarivarman (Rājarāja I), 13th year = A.D. 998.

Some part of the inscription seems to the palimpsest. It records that a local Brāhmaṇa made an
endowment of 15 kaḻañju of gold for making gift to a flawless reciter (kalamaṟuttu nallārāṉār)
of some specified part of the Taḷavakāra-sāmavēda on the day of Tiruvādirai in the month of
Mārgaḻi. [Compare SII, Vol. XIII, No. 250 from Kōyiltēvarāyanpēṭṭai which is similar to this].

l.ta: ttiyāṟ polivatākak kuṭutta poṉ kaḻañciṟ pērttu mutal
m ippoṉ patiṉaiṅkaḻañcukkum palicaiyāṉa poṉ āṭṭaivaṭṭam

māṭa eḻuntaruḷuvutaṟku muṉpu itiṉ
muṉṉai iravu talavakāra sāmavē

karaiyiṭṭu piḻaiyāmal ovvor turuvum piḻaiyāmai coṉṉārai orukkā-
ṭṭu tīṭṭil mēkkāṭṭiṉār ellām iraṇṭu pā

inscription.head:
No. 77.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja Rājakēsarivarman (Rājarāja I), 17th year = A.D. 1002.

Nārāyaṇan Ambalattāḍi, a madhyastha of Kāmaravalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya in
Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu on the north bank made a gift of gold for the midday offerings on new-moon
days in the Tirukkāṟkōṭīśvaram temple.

tirumakaḷ pOlap perunilaccelviyum tanakkEy urimai pūṇṭamai
manakko-

inscription.head:
No. 78.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman (Uttamachōḻa), 9th year, Dhanus, Thursday, Kṛittikā
=A.D. 979.

Records a provision made for conducting the services śrībali and tiruppaḷḷi-eḻichchi in the
temple of Tirunallūr-Paramēśvarar. For this purpose the peruṅguṟi-perumakkaḷ of Kāmaraval-
li-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya on the north bank decided to set apart one kuṟuṇi of
paddy from out of the six kuṟuṇi being collected for the tank on every mā of the paddy fields
of the village. All the four chēris of the village took part in the deiberations and attested the
document through their respective madhyasthas.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIX, No. 231).

l.ta: ṟṟa kārtikai nāṉṟu taṭṭaḻi koṭṭi peruṅ-
kuṟi cāṟṟi maṇṭakattē kūṭi eḻuntaruḷi iru-
ku vaitta nellilē māttāl kuṟuṇi nel
pūttōṟum koḷḷa peṟuvatāka av av āṇ

inscription.head:
No. 79.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th century.

Records that one Araiyaṉ Tiruvaraṅgamuḍaiyāṉ Appaṉiṉum-nalla-Sembiyadaraiyan of Puduk-
kuḍi built the maṇḍapa.

inscription.head:
No. 80.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Vikramachōḻa, 14th year = A.D. 1132.

This is incomplete and records that the mahāsabhā of the village decided to give some land in
exchange for another tax-free land that they had given for archanābhōga earlier as it was found
difficult to cultivate due to lack of proper irrigation. The transaction took place in the presence
of the nāḍu officer Veṅkāḍaṉ Paṇḍaraṅgamuḍaiyāṉ alias Neṟiyuḍaichchōḻa-Pallavarāyar of
Marudāḍu in Veṅkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇarap puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu viḷaṅka jēyamātu virumpa tan tiruppatu
malar mannavar cūṭa maṉniya urimaiyāl maṇimuṭicūṭi ceṅkōl cenṟu

kaṇṇāṟṟu •••

inscription.head:
No. 81.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Vikramachōḻa, 14th year = A.D. 1132.

Beginning portion lost. A Veḷḷāḷa of Kōṉmaṅgalam, the eastern hamlet of Kāmaraval-
li-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Viṟai-kūṟṟam of Vikkiramachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu made a gift of 10 kaḻañju
of gold for a lamp to the Śiva temple in the village. The gift was taken charge of by some Śiv-
abrāhmaṇas.

l.ta:
••••••ytu camaittaruḷip paimpoṟ kuḻitta parikala mutalālc cempoṟ kaṟ(p)
pakattēr paricciṉṉamum

aḷavillātaṉa oḷipeṟa amaittup pattāmāṇṭu cittirait tiṅka ḷattam
peṟṟa ātitta vā-

rattut tiruvaḷar matiyiṉ tiraiyōtacip pakkattu ḷiṉṉaṉa palavum iṉitu
camaittaruḷit taṉ oru ku-

śrīvikkiramacōḻatēvarkku yā-

inscription.head:
No. 82.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga I, 50th year = A.D. 1120.

Incomplete. A sum of three kāsu was gifted for three sandhi lamps by a local Brāhmaṇa. The
Śivabrāhmaṇas who received the gift assured uninterrupted lamp service through a document
called miṉaiyān-tīṭṭu. Kāmaravalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam was a brahmadēya in Viṟai-kūṟṟam of
Ulaguyyavandachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu on the north bank.

l.ta: pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ puṇara uri

maiyil ciṟanta maṇimuṭi cūṭi mīṉavar nilai keṭa vilavar kulaitara ēṉai
maṉṉavar riyaluṟṟaḻitara

uṭaiyānuṭainu ikkuṭi arumoḻiciyān māṭilan kūttan pāṇṭanum
kāttaṉāṭkoṇṭānu mivvaṉaivōmic
nukku miṉaiyāntīṭṭu kuṭuttaparicāvatu ivarkaḷ pakkal koṇṭa kācu

edition.l:
yilil sandhi 1- kku viḷakku 1 mūṉṟu santikku mūṉṟu tiruviḷakku cantrātityavat
erikka kaṭavōmā

inscription.head:
No. 83.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 10th year = A.D. 1188.

This record says that Vīrasēkara-vēḷāṉ, a madhyastha of the brahmadēya village Ālaṅguḍi in
Muḍichōṇāḍu in Suttamali-vaḷanāḍu, made a gift of money amounting 350 current good kāsu
for a lamp and midnight offerings and for betel leaves, areca-nuts, a knife and a scissors or
nut-cracker (kattirigai) in the temple of Tirukkāṟkōtīśvaram. The Śivabrāhmaṇas who took
charge of the gift promised that the services would not be disrupted even if they sell or mort-
gage their right of conducting worship.

l.ta:
vanacakkaravattikaḷ maturaikoṇṭaruḷiy śrī kulOttuṅkacOḻa
tEvaṟku yāṇṭu pattāvatu vaṭakarai vikkamacOḻavaḷanāṭṭu viṟaikkūṟṟattu
śrīkāmatavalliccaruppEti maṅkalattu uṭaiyār tirukkāṟkOṭī

muṟṟum cuttamalivaḷa nāṭṭu muṭiccōṇāṭṭup piramma tēyam ālaṅkuṭiyāṉa
śrī o •••••••••petimaṅkalattu

ampalaṅkōyil koṇṭāṉāṉa vīracēkara vēḷāṉ pakkal innāyaṉār
keṟpakkiraka tirumuṉpu ivvāṭṭai mīṉa nāyaṟu mutal pacuvin-

naṟu neyyāl tiri nuntāviḷakkoṉṟēṟṟavum akal oṉṟum uttama tīpattukku
vēṇṭum •••••••••nāṅkaḷ koṇṭa anṟāṭu naṟkācu [iru]nūṟṟaimpatu

nācciyāṟkum tiruantappuraccēmaccuruḷāka amutu ceytaruḷa nāḷ oṉṟukku

aṭaikāyamutu pākku irupattu nālum ilai amutu paṟṟu araiyē
iraṇṭumāvum parikka kiḷikkatti oṉṟum naṟukka kattirikai oṉṟum nāṅkaḷ nā

ittaṉmaṅkaḷukku upaiyamāka i-

ṭōm ikkōyiṟkāṇi uṭaiya civappirāmaṇarōm iccivappirāmaṇakkāṇi

muppatu vaṭṭattukāṇi

inscription.head:
No. 84.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, 50th year = A.D. 1120.

This records a gift of 4 mā of land by the mahāsabhā of Kāmaravalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Viṟai-kūṟṟam in Ulaguyyavandachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu on the north bank for worship to the temple of
Tiruvagattīśvaram-uḍaiya-Mahādēva at Vaḷavaṉ-Puḷiyaṅguḍi in the same nāḍu.

l.ta: kōyil tirukkāvaṇattē kūṭṭaṅ kuṟaivaṟakkuṭi iruntu ittēvaṟku

namūr śrīkōtaṇṭarāma peruvāykāṟ kīḻttirunārāyaṇa vatikku mēṟku
avaṉi

teṟku mūṉṟāṅ kaṇṇāṟṟu mutalt tuṇṭattut tekkāka vittēva
nilamiraṇṭumāvumāka nilanālumā viṉṉila nālumā-
yuḷiruntu paṇittār kāmaravalliccEri veṭṭip poṉmalai paṭṭa • • •

paṇittu kācu koḷā iṟaiyiliyāy vi-

inscription.head:
No. 85.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Vikramachōḻa, 4th year = A.D. 1122.

A resident of Rājakēsari-chēri in Kāmaravalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Miṟai-kūṟṟam of
Gēyavinōda-vaḷanāḍu made a gift of money for a lamp to the temple Tirukkārkōṭīśvaram in the
same village.

l.ta: vaḷa nāṭṭu miṟai kūṟṟattu śrī kāmatavalli catiṟ vētimaṅkalattu uṭai
taṉ tampirānum uṭainum ciṟilānum ivaṉaivōm ivūr irāja kē

inscription.head:
No. 86.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th Century.

Records that Araiyaṉ Tiruvaraṅgamuḍaiyār alias Sembiyadaraiyar of Pudukkuḍi covered this
maṇḍapa with flat tiles (taṭṭōḍu).

inscription.head:
No. 87.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga II, 3rd year, Simha, sudi 12, Thursday,
Śravaṇa = A.D. 1135, August 22, Thursday.

One Īṅgai-uḍaiyāṉ Aḍigaḷ Dēvakaṇātaṉ of Rājēndrachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu gave some land for two
lamps. He purchased the land in Kāvērivalla-nallūr the western hamlet of Kāmadaval-
li-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and the mahāsabhā of the village made it tax-free without taking tax
capital (kāsu koḷḷā iṟaiyili).

l.ta:
pūmEvaḷara tirupponmātu puṇara nāmEvukalaimakaḷ nalam peritu
niṟppa vicaiya māmakaḷ velpuyattiruppa icaiyuñ celviyumiraṇṭicai

viḷaṅka tirupāvan tiṟaiñca nīṇilamaṭantaiyait tirumaṇampuṇantu cīr pār
aṇimuṭi kavitteṉa maṇimuṭi cūṭi mallai ñālattup pallu-

yirellām ellaiyili inpam iyalvinileyti veṇkuṭai niḻaṟṟic
ceṅkokō[lō*]cci vāḻiyūḻi ānaṭappa cempon vīrasiṅṅāsaṉat-

tu nāḷ uṭaiyār tirukkāṟkōṭī[cuvara] uṭaiyār tirumaṇṭapattē kūṭṭa
kuṟaivaṟakūṭi iruntu sa
