inscription.head:
No. 88.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the first prākāra of the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Vīrapāṇḍya, 10th year = A.D. 1263.

This relates to the assignment of certain voluntary levies on all articles of their merchandise
by the merchants of the Eighteen Vishayam guild from ‘the 79 vaḷanāḍu’ for repairs to the tem-
ple of Tirukkārkōṭīśvaram at Kāmadavalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

l.ta:
vatu kumpanāyaṟṟu vikkiramacōḻa vaḷanāṭṭu viṟaikkūṟṟattu
śrīkāmatava-

lliccaruppēti maṅkalattu uṭaiyār tirukkāṟkōṭīcuramuṭaiya nāyaṉār
kōyi-
l tiruppaṇikku eḻupattoṉpatu vaḷanāṭu patiṉeṇpūmi patiṉeṇ viṣaiya-
ttōm rakṣayāka niṟaivuṟa niṟaintu niccayittapaṭi aḷappaṉa niṟuppaṉa maṭippa-

ṉa uraippaṉa eṭuppaṉa ivaiyiṟṟukku puṭavaikkaṭṭu oṉṟukku paṇam kālum
miḷaku poti oṉṟukku paṇam kālum pākku poti oṉṟukku paṇam kālum
uraippaṉa

poṉ oṉṟukku paṇam kālum niṟaikku kācu oṉṟum aricippoti oṉṟukkuk
kācu ira-
ṇṭum nel poti oṉṟukku kācu oṉṟu talaiccumaikku kācu oṉṟum ivaiyi
ṟṟā varuvaṉavum vāṅkikoṇṭu ittiruppaṇi cantirātittavarai cellak kaṭavatāka
patiṉeṇ viṣayattār ellāruṅ kūṭi cammatittukkuṭuttōm ippaṭipaṇi-

yāl nakar kaṇakku aññūṟṟuva vēḷāṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku malai
maṇṭalamātākka[ḷ]

tavēṇum eṉṟu vēṇṭikkoḷḷa eḻutiṉēṉ imaiyavappatta curakuru nāṭṭu
kayillāya kiḻavaṉāṉa eyilkulattu

purattaracaṉ pāṇṭikulapati vaḷanāṭṭu eyilnāṭṭu pūtamaṅkalamuṭaiyāṉ
namaccivāya tēvaṉ |||—

inscription.head:
No. 89.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the entrance, right side, into the gōpura of the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III, 22nd year = A.D. 1238.

The oil merchants (vāṇiya-nagaram) of Chintāmaṇi-perunderu and those of Tiruchiṟṟambal-
ap-perunderu in the temple area made a gift of one nāḻi of gingili oil for bathing the god
with it every Saturday from the month of Kumbha. The document was written by Iḍaṅgai-ni-
yāya-chakravatti.

l.ta: ramuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku cantirātitavarai cani eṇṇaikkāppu cātti
yaruḷa ciṉacintāmaṇip perunteruvil vāṇiya nakarattōmum iṉ
ṉāyaṉār tirumaṭai viḷākattu tirucciṟṟampalap peruṉteruvil vāṇi-
ṟu mutal caṉi oṉṟukku nāḻi eḷḷeṇṇai āka aḷakka kaṭa
vōmāka kalveṭṭi kuṭuttōm ippaṭi ceyyātārai nammi-
ṉattil oruvar allarākavum civaturōkikaḷ paṭṭatuvum iṉa-
turōkikaḷ paṭṭatuvum paṭṭu naṉmmai tīmaikku puka kaṭavō-
m alār ākavum ippaṭi cammatitu kalveṭṭi kuṭuttōm vā-

inscription.head:
No. 90.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the entrance, right side, into the gōpura of the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III, 15+1 year = A.D. 1232.

Gift of a silver fillet to the deity of Tirukkāṟkōtīśvaram temple by an Ādichcha-baṭṭāraṉ Bi-
ramārāyar of Toḻumūr.

l.ta: ramuṭaiya ṉāyaṉārkku tiruvāparaṇam āka toḻumūr ā-
patinṉāṟ kaḻañcaraiyum i[tu] āticcapaṭṭā[raṉ] marutāntaka pira

inscription.head:
No. 91.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the entrance, left side, into the gōpura of the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājarāja III, 19th year, Vṛiśchika, sudi 11, Wednesday, Śatabhisaj =
A.D. 1234, October 4, Wednesday.

It records that a vāṇiya-mudali of Gaṅgaikoṇḍa-chōḻa-puram called Uyyavantāṉ Iṉtarāyaṉ
gifted a nāḻi of gingili oil from the oilmill (chekku) working in Jinachintāmaṇi-perunteru in
Kāmadavalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam for a perpetual lamp in the Tirukkāṟkōtīśvaram temple of
the place. He prayed that the future mudalis also should maintain the gift. Otherwise they
would be considered as outcaste members.

l.ta: kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa cataiyattu nāḷ vikkirama cōḻa

ramuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟkku tiruṉuntāviḷakkuk
kuṭalāka ivūr ciṉa
kkil nāḷ oṉṟukku upaiyamāka aḷakkum eḷ
m vāṇiyarōm ippaṭikku ivai nakarak kaṇakku ci-

inscription.head:
No. 92.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the Gaṇapati shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III, 15+1 year = A.D. 1232.

The mahāsabhā of Kāmadavalli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam decreed that hereafter the village admin-
istration should be carried on only by annual committees (samvatsara varaṇam).

inscription.head:
No. 93.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the Gaṇapati shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājēndra III, 14th year, Vṛiśchika, badi 5, Thursday, Punarvasu =
A.D. 1250, November 6, Thursday.

The trustees of Tirukkāṟkōtiśvaram temple assigned some land in a location called Tiruchiṟṟam-
bala-nallūr-Kaṭṭaḷai for the living (jīvana-śēsham) of the gardeners tending a flower-garden of
the temple.

l.ta:
ramuṭaiya nāyaṉār kōyil tāṉattōm innāyaṉārkku
tirunantavaṉañ
ṭṭaḷaiyil kōtaṇṭarāma peruvāykkāl kīḻkarait tuṇṭattu nīṅkal

inscription.head:
No. 94.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the Gaṇapati shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Hoysaḷa, Vīra-Sōmēśvara, 5th = A.D. 1240.

In the beginning there is a short Sanskrit praśasti of the king. The record gives the arbitration of
the king in favour of the temple on a land dispute between the temple trustees and an individual
regarding the ownership of the village Vaṇṇam alias Madurāntakanallūr.

inscription.head:
No. 95.
Kāmarasavalli, Udaiyarpalayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east, north and west walls of the Chaṇḍikēśvara shrine in the Kārkōṭakēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājēndra III, 12th year, sudi 3, Friday = A.D. 1258.

The record gives the details of the land possessions of a maṭha called Tiruñānasambandaṉ-tiru-
maḍam received from the temple and maintained by a devotee called Kuṉṟaṉ Kiḻāṉ Tirunāvuk-
karasudēvaṉ from Ūṟṟukkāṭṭu-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The latter gave some
land to the temple in exchange for the above. He also added much to the landed properties of
the maṭha by purchase and gift. He appealed to its future caretakers not to misuse the properties
by any means, even if ordered so by the temple trustees and the government officials.

edition.l: sva[ stiśrī tirapuva] ṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīrājEntira cOḻa dēvaṟku yāṇṭu 12

inscription.head:
No. 96.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, [Parāntaka I], 23rd year = A.D. 930.

The inscription is dated in the 23rd year of some king whose name is lost. Most probably he
is a Parakēsarivarman and from the high regnal year he is identified with Parāntaka I. This
interesting record tells that the sabhā of the village sold the water rights of an irrigation tank
to Chembiyaṉ Vēdivēḷār for 410 kaḻañju of gold. The latter was given the right to construct a
sluice from a kaṇṇāṟu (branch channel) and take water for irrigating his lands. It was however
clarified that the water rights of the old landowers should not be affected. The sale was made for
paying back some loan due to the temple of Kayilāyam in Tiruveḷḷaṟai. The loan (of 30 kaḻañju
of gold) was originally taken by a Tāyanīli and by default it became three-fold. Then the right
on the loan was transferred to the present temple by some individuals (who perhaps got it from
Tāyanīli). As the inscription is built in the beginning, the preceding circumstances are not
clear.

inscription.head:
No. 97.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Rājarāja I, 25th year = A.D. 1010.

An endowment of 5 kaḻañju of gold for a sandhi lamp to the temple of the Āḻvār on the hill
at Tiruveṟumbiyūr in Śrīkaṇṭha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya in Viḷā-nāḍu of Pāṇḍi-
kulāsani-vaḷanāḍu on the south bank. The donor was a member of the āḷuṅgaṇattār of the
village.

edition.l: ndhiviḷakku 1-

inscription.head:
No. 98.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja Rājakēsarivarman (Rājarāja I), 11th year = A.D. 996.

A gift of 34 sheep for a lamp to the shrine of Gaṇapati in the Tiruveṟumbiyūr temple by a
member of the āḷuṅgaṇattār of the village.

inscription.head:
No. 99.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra I, 5th year = A.D. 1017.

Ending part damaged. The inscription records a gift of land made in the 21st year of Rājarāja
I by the ūrār of Tiruveṟumbiyūr in Vaḍa-kavira-nāḍu a unit of Paṇḍikulāsani-vaḷanāḍu on the
south bank, for offerings to the temple of Tirumalai-Āḻvār. Interestingly the cardinal dimen-
sions of the land measuring 8 mā and odd are given as 24, 35, 26.5 and 35 rods respectively
starting from the east side. The measuring rod was called māḷigaik-kōl.

inscription.head:
No. 100.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra I, 3rd year = A.D. 1015.

Gift of 8 kāsu for a sandhi lamp to the temple of Tiruveṟumbīśvaram by a member of the
āḷuṅgaṇattār of Śṟikaṇṭha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Viḷā-nāḍu of Paṇḍi-
kulāśani-vaḷanāḍu.

edition.l: [pīśvaramuṭaiya mahādēvarkku sa]

inscription.head:
No. 101.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 7th year, Makara, Tuesday, Ārdrā = c. 10th Century.

Partly hidden. This records a big endowment of land measuring 4 vēli and 13 mā by a Brāh-
maṇa of Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam for feeding daily 3 śivayōgis and 2 Brāhmaṇas in the
choultry (chattiram) on the hill at Tiruveṟumbiyūr. The sabhā of the village which met at the
hall called Tiruchitrakūḍam decided to pay themselves the taxes on the endowed land in lieu of
the tax capital received from the donor.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 162.]

inscription.head:
No. 102.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [Gaṇḍarāditya], 7th year, Karkaṭaka, Friday,
Tiruvōṇam = 956.

Incomplete. The sabhā of Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam sold a part of the water rights of their
tank to Vēdi-vēḷār to irrigate some land by the latter from the sabhā of Peruveṅgūr. The sabhā
permitted water to be drawn from the fourth kaṇṇāṟu (branch canal) flowing to the west of the
main canal Śrīkaṇṭa-vāykkāl.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 163.]

inscription.head:
No. 103.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [probably Gaṇḍarāditya], 5th year = A.D. 954.

Endowment of land for feeding 15 Brāhmaṇas in the chattiram (feeding house) established
on the hill of Tiruveṟumbiyūr by Vēḷān Vīranāraṇaṉ alias Sembiyaṉ Vēdivēḷāṉ of Chiṟudavūr
in Kiḷiyūr-nāḍu. The latter purchased the land, altogether 2 vēli and 7 mā, from several Brāh-
maṇas of Śrīkaṇṭha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and got the entire land tax-free by paying the tax
capital to the sabhā of the village.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 110.]

inscription.head:
No. 104.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [Gaṇḍarāditya], 7th year, Makara, Thursday, Mūla =
A.D. 956.

This records sale of land, made tax-free, by the sabhā of Peruveṅgūr in Viḷā-nāḍu along with
several other Brāhmaṇas of the village to Vēḷān Vīranāraṇaṉ alias Sembiyaṉ Vēdivēḷāṉ, who is
said to have constructed the śrīvimāna of Tiruveṟumbiyūr-Āḻvār. Either the extent of the land
or the price amount are not specified. Murukkūr, a hamlet of Uttamaśīli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam is
mentioned among the boundaries. See No. 102 above for a related transaction.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 164.)

inscription.head:
No. 105.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [Gaṇḍarāditya], 7th year, Simha, Saturday, Rōhiṇi =
A.D. 956.

Sale, by the sabhā of Śrīkaṇṭha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, of the right to collect the tax mākkuṟuṇi
during each cropping season from the uḻutakuḍis of the main village as well as of the hamlets,
to Vēḷān Vīranāraṇaṉ alias Sembiyaṉ Vēdivēḷāṉ for a sum of 45 kaḻañju of gold. The tax in-
come was meant for the purpose of desilting the village tank. The madhyastha Dharmmapriyaṉ
Kalivichaiyaṉ Taruṇēndu-śēkharaṉ wrote the sale deed.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 165.)

inscription.head:
No. 106.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, 37th year = A.D. 1107.
Fragment containing only the name of the king.

l.ta:
kOvirāca kEcari panmarāṉa cakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulOttuṅka cOḻa
tEvaṟku yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 107.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: . . ., = c. 10-11th Century A.D.

The beginning portion relating to the ruling king and date is lost. Most probably the king was
Rājarāja I. This records that the sabhā of the village assigned some dēvadāna land for offer-
ings to Piḷḷaiyār Kshētrapāladēva in the temple on the hill in Śrīkaṇṭha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a
brahmadēya in Mīkōḻai-nāḍu in Paṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu. The transaction took place under the
orders (niyōga) of the nāḍu-vagai officer of the locality.

inscription.head:
No. 108.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north and west walls of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parāntaka I, 36th year = A.D. 943.

This records a sale of land by the sabhā of Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam to Chāttaṉ Taṇak-
kaṉ of Chiṟuvāli-nāḍu who set up the image of Umābhaṭṭāraki in the temple of Tiruveṟumbi-
yūr-Āḻvār. The sabhā made the land tax-free on receiving the tax capital from the purchaser.

l.ta:
yōm eṅkaṇṇilam ivvūrk kuḷattukkīḻ śrīkaṇṭa[vā]
ykkālukku mēkku añcāṅ kaṇṇāṟṟup-
palāṟṟukku vaṭakku eṭṭām pāṭakaṅ kaliyaṅkūṟṟa nuḷḷiṭṭa

kuṭuttōm tiruverumpiyūrāḻvā-

inscription.head:
No. 109.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north and west walls of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Rājarāja I, 27th year = A.D. 1012.

One Chiṟukuḍaiyār Kāḷi Ādittaṉ who investigated on behalf of the king (dēvarkaḷ sōdiṉai pōn-
da) the income and expenses of the temple of Tiruveṟumbiyūr Mahādēvar made some new
arrangement for the services in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 110.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [Āditya I], 19th year = c. 890.

A gift of land for a day lamp, by a Chandraśarmaṉ, who got the land tax-free by paying tax
capital to the sabhā of Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 286.)

l.ta:
ṉēṉ pakal
viḷakkeri[p]pataṟkku kaḷattuk kīḻaṭutta ce-
yil kīḻai eṉ orumāvarai ivvorumāvaraiyum kuṭuttēṉ
e[ppērp]paṭṭitum poṉ koṇṭu kāttuk kuṭuppōmāṉōm peruṅkuṟi

inscription.head:
No. 111.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parāntaka I, 30th year = A.D. 937.

A gift of 10 chey of land for a lamp by a Eṟumbiyūr-uḍaiyāṉ Nakka-nāgaṉ to the temple of
Teṉkayilāyattu-Mahādēva at Śrīkaṇṭha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam. The sabhā of the village made
the land tax-free after receiving the tax capital.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 166.)

iṟaikāval kaṭavā-

inscription.head:
No. 112.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [Gaṇḍarāditya], 7th year = A.D. 956.

This records assignment of some land as jīvitam for the maintenance of a watchman. The land
was purchased from the ūrār of the Tiruveṟumbiyūr by Vēḷān Vīranāraṇaṉ alias Sembiyaṉ
Vēdivēḷāṉ of Chiṟudavūr who had built the central shrine. The document was written by a
Kaṇḍaṉ Araiyaṉ a kiḻāṉ of Vaṟaḍāṟṟu-vallam in Veṇbi-nāḍu.

l.ta:
piyūr ūrar icainta ūr[o]m ittiruveṟumpiyūr āḷvār śrīvimāṉa
eḻuntaru-

eṉe[ḻu*]ttu ippaṭi oṭṭi i-

inscription.head:
No. 113.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman [Uttamachōḻa], 15th year = 985.

The end portion is not available. It records assignment of a piece of land for offerings to the de-
ity Āḻvār Tirubhuvanasundarar at Tiruveṟumbiyūr. The said land which forms part of a land that
had been purchased by Sembiyan Vēydivēḷāṉ from the sabhā of the village and it was made
cultivable by a saṅkarappāḍi member called Māṟandūtuvaṉ Sivapattaṉ.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIX, No. 372).

tittaval uccampōtai santiyil
tiruvamitu ceyya vaiytta arici irunā-

ḻiyum kaṟiyamitoṉṟukkum neyyamitaraippiṭikkum tayiramitu urikkum
aṭaikkāyamitu iraṇṭum veṟṟilai eṭṭukkumāka vaiytta ni-

perunāṉkel-

inscription.head:
No. 114.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 4th year = c. 10th Century.

A temple girl of Tiruvaraṅgam by name Chēndaṉ Cheyyavāymaṇi made a gift of 10 kaḻañju
of gold for burning a lamp and for bringing a pot of water from the river daily to the temple of
Teṉkayilāyattu-Mahādēva at Śrīkaṇṭha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 88).

l.ta: ṇi koḷḷik kuṭattāl nicati oru kuṭattukkum āka-
tta poṉ patiṉ kaḻaiñcu ippoṉ pati-

inscription.head:
No. 115.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parāntaka I, 26th year = A.D. 933.

This incomplete record refers to some provision for offerings to the Teṉkayilāyam temple at
Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 116.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja Rājakēsarivarman (Rājarāja I), 7th year = A.D. 992.

This records a gift of money for feeding on the sacred hill 6 Brāhmaṇas versed in the Vēdas, by
Ilāḍarāyar Pugaḻuyppavargaṇḍaṉ Vīrachōḻaṉ. The sabhā of Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam sold
in lieu of the money the paddy dues collected from the tax mākkuṟuṇi on the uḻukuḍis of their
northern hamlet Vīranārāyaṇa-nallūr. The portion of the tax that had been sold previously by
the sabhā for a tank is said to be excluded from the present sale. (See No. 165 above for the
previous sale).

l.ta:
rttiruveṟumpūr malaimēl uṭaiyār ilāṭarāyar pukaḻuyppavar kaṇṭaṉ
vīracōḻar ittirumalaiyil

inscription.head:
No. 117.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 5th year = c. 10th Century.

Gift of 6 mā of tax-free land by Tāyaṉ Kaṇamuḍaiyāṉ for a lamp to the temple of Mahādēva on
the hill at Śrīkaṇṭha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya on the southern bank.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 111).

l.ta:
varkku ivvūrt tāyaṉ kaṇamuṭaiyāṉ oru nontā viḷakku erippataṟkku
nicati uḻakku neykku

vaitta nilam ivvūrakavāy vaṉṉiccuṟṟil mūṉṟu māvum itiṉ mēlai
yiraṇṭumāvum
yāṟkkup poṉ kuṭuttu itiṉ palicaiyā liṟaiyum sā

inscription.head:
No. 118.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 19th year = c. 10th Century.

Gift of land, made tax-free, for a lamp and offerings, by a Brāhmaṇa lady of Timmikuḍi who
built the shrine of Ādittabhaṭṭārakar on the hill.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 287).

l.ta: vamutukku tiruviḷakkukkum neyamutukkumāka vaitta nilamāvatu ivvūrakavā-
yiṉ veṅkūr nālāṅkaṇṇāṟṟuk kīḻ catirattu ppattām pāṭakam vaṉpā
yāṟkku iṉṉilattukku iṟaikāval patiṉ kaḻaiñcu poṉṉum kuṭuttu

inscription.head:
No. 119.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 18th year = c. 10th Century.

One Nambaṉ Kēsuvaṉ purchased and gifted some land for lamp to the temple of Teṉkayilāyat-
tu-Mahādēva. He got the land freed from taxes by paying tax capital to the sabhā of the village.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 283).

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 120.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west and south walls of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra I, 7th year = A.D. 1019.

The sabhā of Śrīkaṇṭha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya in Mikōḻai a subdivision of Pāṇḍi-
kulāśani-vaḷanāḍu on the southern bank remitted all taxes (iṟai, echchōṟu, veṭṭi, vētiṉai, tiruk-
koṟṟavāchalil-pōnda-kuḍimai) on some dēvadāna land of the Tiruveṟumbiyūr temple in lieu of
the interest on the a sum of 202 kāsu they received from the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 121.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra II, 5th year = A.D. 1057.

Gift of a sum of 30 kāsu for a lamp by Muttaṉ Māḍattāḷ, a woman servant (peṇḍāṭṭi) belonging to
uyyakkoṇḍāṉ-terinda-tirumañjanattār-vēḷam (a palace establishment) of Gaṅgaikoṇḍachōḻapu-
ram.

ki] ••••• kaivaḻarā

l.ta:
āvatu pāṇṭi kulācaṉi vaḷanāṭṭu
śrīkaṇṭac caruppēti maṅkalattu[ttu] tiruveṟumpiyūr

inscription.head:
No. 122.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja Rājakēsarivarman (Rājarāja I), 16th year = A.D. 1001.

The sabhā of the village decided to remit all taxes on certain dēvadāna lands of the temple on
the hill in lieu of the interest on a sum of 50 kaḷañju of gold received from the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 123.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [Gaṇḍarāditya], 3rd year = A.D. 952.

This records a sale of land jointly by the ūrār of Eṟumbiyūr and the sabhā of Śrīkaṇṭha-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam to Sembiyaṉ Vēdivēḷār, who in turn gave it to the temple of Tiruveṟumbiyūr for the
maintenance of four tiruppadiyam reciters. It is said that the sold land was a disputed property
(sīmā vivīdam āy kiḍanda nilam) between the two seller bodies.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 50.)

inscription.head:
No. 124.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājēndra I, •••= A.D. 1012-44.
Fragmentary. Refers to a gift of 90 sheep for a lamp by Māṟaṉ Kāri.

l.ta:
•••••••••• vartēvi[yar*] ōṅkeḻil muṭiyum muṉṉavar pakkal teṉṉavar
vaitta cuntaramuṭiyum intiranāramum teṇṭirai īḻamaṇṭala muḻutum eṟipaṭaik

inscription.head:
No. 125.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Rājarāja I, 24th year = A.D. 1009.

Two sisters related to a vēṭkōvaṉ called Marudaṉ Paṭṭapirāṉ endowed a sum of 7.5 kaḻañju of
gold to the hill temple of Tiruveṟumbiyūr.

l.ta: teṉkarai nittaviṉōta vaḷanāṭṭu kiḷāṟ kūṟṟu perumila-

nampi ammaiyum vivaḷ [ḷōṭuṭap piṟanta] cāyā tēviyum ivviruvōm
vaicca

inscription.head:
No. 126.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 5th year = c. 10th Century.

A Veḷḷāḷa couple of the village of Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam made a gift of 45 sheep for a
lamp in the temple of Tiruveṟumbiyūr.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 112.)

inscription.head:
No. 127.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 6th year, Makara, Tuesday, Svasti = c. 10th Century.

Gift of land by a local Brāhmaṇa for feeding a Brāhmaṇa versed in the Vēdas in the chattiram
(choultry) on the sacred hill of Tiruveṟumbiyūr. The sabhā of the village which met in the hall
called Tiruchchittirakūṭam decided to remit all taxes on the land in lieu of the tax capital taken
from the donor.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 138.)

inscription.head:
No. 128.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman [Uttamachōḻa], 16th year = A.D. 986.

This records a gift of land for offerings to the temple of Āḻvār on the hill of Tiruveṟumbiyūr in
Kavira-nāḍu by the ūrār of the village.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIX, No. 395.)

inscription.head:
No. 129.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [Gaṇḍarāditya], 3rd year = A.D. 952.

Vēḷān Vīranāraṇaṉ alias Sembiyaṉ Vēdivēḷāṉ assigned some land after purchase from the local
sabhā for singing the tiruppadiyam in the temple, accompanied by the musical instruments
uḍukkai and tāḷam. This land was added to the land that the donor purchased sometime back
(mentioned in No. 123 above). Among the boundaries is mentioned a canal called Kiḷiyūr-vāyk-
kāl that was dug by the donor with the permission of the king (line 8).
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 51.)

inscription.head:
No. 130.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 6th year, Makara, Tuesday, Ārdrā = c. 10th Century.

A gift of tax-free land, by a local Brāhmaṇa, for feeding some Brāhmaṇas versed in the Vēdas
in the chattiram on the hill.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 139.)

l.ta: peṟṟa tiruvātiraināṉṟu pakal ivvūrt tiruci[t]tira kūṭattēy

inscription.head:
No. 131.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [Gaṇḍarāditya], 6th year = A.D. 955.

Sale of land to Vēdivēḷān by the sabhā of Śrīkaṇṭha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam for the purpose of
making the temple streets (maḍaviḷāgam) of the Tiruveṟumbiyūr-āḻvār temple. A piece of land
given to the tiruppadiyam reciters is mentioned among the boundaries.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 140.)

l.ta: ḻikku mukkōlnīkki itaṟku mē[ṟ*]kku teṉpāṟkellai tiṟuveṟumpiyūrārk-
kuḷattukku vaṭakku[m] mīypārkkellai tiruppatiyam pāṭum aṭikaḷmārkku

inscription.head:
No. 132.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 5th year = c. 10th Century.

Gift of 34 sheep for a sandhi lamp by a member of the āḷuṅgaṇam, who also gave a lamp-stand
made of tarā.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 113.)

inscription.head:
No. 133.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, 5th year, Mithuna, Wednesday, Svāti = c. 10th Century.

This interesting record gives the decision of the sabhā of Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam that
the temple of Tiruveṟumbiyūr-āḻvār is not obliged to pay any taxes (to the sabhā) and hence the
sabhā should not demand any tax from the temple or attach any property of the temple on that
pretext. Any transgressor of the decision would be considered as an enemy of the village in-
viting punishment with excommunication and demolition of his house (griha tāṭakas chaidu).
(Published in SII, Vol. XIII, No. 114.)

inscription.head:
No. 134.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 10th Century.

Gives in detail all the items of expenditure (maintenance for five Vēda-brāhmaṇas, musicians,
food offerings to deities) for which land had been given by Vēḷān Vīranāraṇaṉ alias Sembiyaṉ
Vēdivēḷāṉ after purchase from the sabhā of Peruveṅgūr. [The inscription is full of symbols for
fractions written in very cursive form difficult to decipher].

inscription.head:
No. 135.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III (?), 4th year = A.D. 1220.

Gift of a lamp-stand to the temple of Tiru-Eṟumbiyūr by an individual of Tirunelvēli in Pāṇḍi-
maṇḍalam. The gift was conveyed through an agent.

l.ta: ṭṭi paṇṭārattil loṭukkiṉa tirukkutti viḷakku oṉṟu §

inscription.head:
No. 136.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājarāja III, 3rd year, Kumbha, badi 12, Wednesday, Uttarāshāḍha =
A.D. 1219, February 13, Wednesday.

This fragmentary inscription records a gift of land by a local Brāhmaṇa. The village is called
Malari alias Śrīkaṇṭha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Mīkōḻai-nāḍu in Pāṇḍyakulāsani-vaḷanāḍu on
the south bank.

inscription.head:
No. 137.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the kitchen in the Piplikēśvara temple, right side.
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Vikramachōḻa, 4th year = A.D. 1122.

Records a sale of land, made tax-free, to the temple of Tiruveṟumbiyūr-Mahādēva by the sabhā,
who met in the temple of Tirumērkōyil-Āḻvār on the occasion. The river Arumoḻidēva-pērāṟu is
mentioned among the boundaries of the sold land. Some twelve members of the sabhā attested
to the document. The writer was the madhyastha called Kaṇattāṉ Chentāmaraikkaṇṇan alias
Aiyyāyirap-piriyan.

inscription.head:
No. 138.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the kitchen in the Piplikēśvara temple, right side.

Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [mistake for Parakēsarivarman] Vikramachōḻa,
7th year = A.D. 1125.

Uttama-piriyan, a madhyastha of Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam purchased land free of taxes
from the sabhā of the village and donated it for offerings and for feeding five strangers daily
in the temple of Tiruveṟumbiyūr-Āṇḍār. The land measuring 8 mā (of standard category) and
equivalent to 2 vēli of virivu category was priced at 13.5 kāsu and the tax capital for the same
was also 13.5 kāsu.

inscription.head:
No. 139.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the kitchen in the Piplikēśvara temple, left side.
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga II, 4th year = A.D. 1137

Besides the eulogy of the king, only the sabhā for Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam is mentioned
in the available text.

inscription.head:
No. 140.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the kitchen in the Piplikēśvara temple, left side.
Chōḻa, Vikramachōḻa, •••= A.D. 1118-35.

Much worn out. Seems to register a gift of land for offerings by some individuals for food of-
ferings to the deities of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 141.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the first prākāra of the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya II, 8th year, 1009th day (also 8+1+1) =
A.D. 1248.

This relates to the royal gift of the village of Erumaikkuḷam in Teṉ-Kaḷavaḻi-nāḍu for a festival
in the name of the Prince (piḷḷaiyār) Bagavadi-Āḻvār in the temple of Tiruveṟumbiyūr-uḍaiyār.
On the advice of Ayyaṉ Maḻavarāyaṉ the king gave away the village as dēvadāna-iṟaiyili inclu-
sive of the kārāṇmai and mīyāṭchi rights and with all the tax revenue. The previous leascholds
and jīvitam tenures in the village were exempted from the purview of the gift.

māṟṟu uḷḷiṭṭu vēṇṭum nimantaṅkaḷum teṉ kaḷavaḻināṭṭu erumaikkuḷam
nāṉkellaikkuṭ[paṭṭa*] iṭam nīr nilamum karuñcey puṉceyum nattamum
kuḷamum uṭpaṭṭa nilattup paḻaṉ tēvatānam paḷḷiccantam nīkki uḷḷa
nilamum muṉṉuṭaiyāru-

m uḻavum cīvitamum mutalu [tavittu] kārāṇmai mīyāṭciyum kaṭamaiyum
antarāyamu viṉiyōkamum taruvatāṉa accum kāriya ārāṭciyum pāṭi kāvalum

poṉvariyum maṟṟum eppēṟpaṭṭiṉavum uṭpaṭa eṭṭāvatiṉ etirāmāṇṭiṉ
etirāmāṇṭu

mutal tēvatāṉa iṟaiyili āka iṭapeṟavēṇumeṉṟu ayyaṉ maḻavarāyar
namakku coṉṉamaiyil ittēvarukkup piḷḷaikaḷil pakavati āḻvār pērāl
kaṇṭa cantikkut tiruppaṭimāṟṟu uḷḷiṭṭu vēṇṭum nimantaṅ-

kaḷukku teṉkaḷavaḻi nāṭṭu erumaikkuḷam nāṉkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa nīrnilamum
karuñcey puṉceyyum ēttamum tōṭṭamum kuḷamum uṭpaṭṭa nilattil
paḻantēvatāṉam paḷḷiccantam nīkki nīkki uḷḷa nilamum muṉṉuṭaiyārum
uḻavum

cīvitamum mutalum tavirntu kārāṇmai mīyāṭciyum kaṭamaiyum antarāyamum
viṉiyōkamum taruvatāṉa accum kāriya ārāṭciyum pāṭikāvalum poṉvariyum
maṟṟum eppērppaṭṭiṉavum uṭpaṭa ivvāṇṭu mutal tēvatāṉa i-

ṟaiyili āka variilār eḻuttiṭṭa uḷvariyum nam ōlaiyum taraccoṉṉōm
ippaṭi cantirātittavaṟ celvatāka kallilum cempilum veṭṭivittuk koḷka
eṉṟu tiruvāy moḻintaruḷiṉār ivai tirumalli nāṭṭut taṭaṅkaṇṇic
ciṟṟū-

ruṭaiyāṉum niṉṟāṭuvāṉ vīracōḻa tēvaṉāṉa kurukulattaraiyaṉeḻuttu
ivai cevvirukkai nāṭṭuc cakkarapāṇi nallūr araiyaṉ viratamuṭittāṉāṉa
pallavarāyaṉeḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 142.
Tiruveṟumbūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the first prākāra of the Piplikēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman [mistake for Rājakēsarivarman] Rājarāja III, 29th
year, sudi 14, Monday, Maghā = A.D. 1245, February 12, Sunday (not Monday).

The sabhā of Chōḻamahādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Mīkōḻai-nāḍu a unit of
Pāṇḍikulāsani-vaḷanāḍu made a gift of 4 vēli of land that was lying waste in the northern hamlet
of the village for making a flower garden (nandavanam).

inscription.head:
No. 143.
Puttūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the top of the Chōḷampāṟai rock near the village.
inscription.p: c. 17th-18th century.
This short label reading ‘pagōḍapi’ in mixed characters makes no sense.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 13 Century.

Mentions that Rāsiṅgadēvaṉ alias Choḻakōṉ paved three rows of stone to the east of the hall
called Maṇi-maṇḍapa.

inscription.head:
No. 145.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 12th year, Kārttigai, 8 = A.D. 1263.

Nāvalūr-uḍaiyāṉ Chōti Chiṟiyāṇḍān, a native of Śivapādaśēkharapuram made provision for
offerings to the god Tirumāṇikkamalai-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, while the deity is taken to the hunting
ceremony.

l.ta: tu nāvalūr uṭaiyāṉ cōti ciṟiyāṇṭāṟkku uṭaiyār tirumāṇikkamalai uṭai-

ya nāyaṉār tirunāḷ tōṟum tiruvēṭṭaikku ēṟi aruḷiṉa tirumaṇṭapattu
tūṇi patakku ari-

ciyum kaṟivañcaṉamuḷḷiṭṭavai kuṟaivaṟukkumiṭattu muṉ nāḷ policaikku
vāṅki ēṟṟi-

toṇūṟṟu kalamu koṇṭu cantirātitta varai naṭatta kaṭavōm
ātica-

inscription.head:
No. 146.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south and east walls of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya, Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Mithuna, badi 10,
Rēvatī, Friday = A.D. 1544, June 13, Friday.

This records a gift of land in Kuḻittaṇḍalai-sīrmai alias Karuṅgilāppaḷḷi in Mīkōḍu-nāḍu, of
Rājagambhīra-vaḷanāḍu, by Timmappa-Nāyaka, the executive agent (kāriyattukku kartar) of
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja-Chinna-Timmayadēva-Mahārāja, for the morning offerings in
the temple of Tirumāṇikkamalai-uḍaiya-Tambirāṉār. A string of titles and the genealogy of the
donor are given in the beginning. He belonged to Chembala gōtra. son of Siṅgama-nāyakkar
and grandson of Cherukku Koṇḍama-nāyakkar of Toṇḍai-maṇḍalam.

edition.l: rattu [ dēca] miyum rēvati nakṣattiramum peṟṟa cukkira vārattu

l.ta: ṭalam tummala tēva vacava caṅkara va •••[kara] kaṇṭakaṭa-
la kōttiramāṉa cerukku koṇṭama nāyakkar puttiranāna
ciṅkamanāyakkar puttiraṉ timmappa nāyakkar kuṭutta taṉmacā-
ṭṭu nāṭṭuk kuḻittaṇṭalaic cīrmai āṉa karuṅkilāppaḷḷi

inscription.head:
No. 147.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Vīra-Narasiṁha Bhujabalarāya-Mahārāya, Śaka 1428, Kshaya,
Karkaṭaka, badi 12, Friday = A.D. 1506, July 17, Friday.

The king is evidently Vīra-Narasimha, elder son of Narasā-nāyaka and first king of the Tuḷuva
dynasty. Gift of gold by a Deyvachchilai Iṟatinagirinātar for a lamp, for bringing water from
the Kāvēri river for the sacred bath of the deity of Tirumāṇikkamalai temple and for other of-
ferings.

l.ta: ṟa cukkiṟavārattu nāḷ uṭaiyār tirumāṇikkamalai uṭai-

inscription.head:
No. 148.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Śaka 1362, Plavanga, Tai, 13th Solar day, Rēvati. Irregular. =
probably c. 1547-48 taking the Cyclic year as correct.

Records a gift of land in Karuṅgilāppaḷḷi by Timmarāja the younger brother of Viṭṭalarāja of
Ashakkaiyil (?), for conducting worship to the god at Ratnagiri. [See No. 191 below for the
same brothers in the time of Sadāsivarāya]

l.ta: u rēpatiṉāḷ tampirāṉār iraṟṟiṉa kiri nāyanā-

inscription.head:
No. 149.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān (Kulōttuṅga III?), 2+1 year = A.D. 1181.

The king ordered, on Toṇḍaimāṉ's petition, assignment of some tax-free dēvadāna land in
Edirilichōḻa-nallūr to those bringing the Kaveri water for sacred ablution in the temple of
Tirumāṇikkamalai-Mādēva in Kurunagar-nāḍu in Rājagambhīra-vaḷanāḍu. The royal secretary
Mīṉavaṉ-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ wrote the order. It is said that the said land had been given sometime
back to the temple but it was without anybody's enjoyment.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravatti kOṉeriṉmai koṇṭāṉ rācakempīra
vaḷanāṭṭu kuṟunakar nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tiru-

ttēvarkku kāvēri tirumañcaṇam cumappārkku nivantatukkuṭalāka iṉṉāṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 150.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, •••= A.D. 1178-1218.
This is fragmentary and seems to register a gift of land.

l.ta:
vattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum pāṇṭiyan muṭittalaiyuṅ
karuvūrum koṇṭu vīra apiṣEkamum vijaya • • •

tillai nāyakaṉ mātēvaṉum ivvi ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 151.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja (II/III), 2nd year = c. 12th Century.

Partly built in. Mentions some gift (name lost) by a Veḷḷāḷa of Niṉmaṇam in Poṅgalūrkkā-nāḍu
in Koṅgamaṇḍalam.

edition.l:
svastiśi tiripuvaṉaccakkaravatti śiirājarāja tEvaṟku yāṇṭu 2- vatu uṭaiyān
tirumāṇikkamalai uṭaiya nāyanā • • •

l.ta:
cak koṅkamaṇṭalattup poṅkalūrk kāṉāṭṭu niṉmaṇa veḷḷāḷaṉ cēva • •
yāl kuruṭamāṇikkatēva • • •

inscription.head:
No. 152.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Tribhuvanavīradēva (Kulōttuṅga III), 34th year, Makara, badi, Monday,
Uttiram (Uttara-Phālguni) = A.D. 1211, December 26, Monday.

Built in the beginning. This registers a gift of land by purchase from the sabhā of a village
(name lost) by a Veḷḷāḷa called Cholāl-cheṭṭi Māṇikkaṉ, a native of Poṅgalūrkkā-nāḍu in Vaḍa-
Koṅgu.

l.ta: •••lil capaiyār tiripuvaṉap peru vilai koṇṭu paṇikka inni-

inscription.head:
No. 153.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th Century.

It refers to the seventh year of some king. But the beginning part of the inscription contain-
ing king's name not available. It records a unanimous decision (amaivu-kachcham) of the
Periyanāḍu assembly comprising the grāmas (that is the brahmadēya villages), the nāḍu (the
Veḷḷāḷa villages) and the nagarams of the locality (name lost) around Kuḻittaṇḍalai, which
seems to have met in the temple. The decision related to the punishments to be meted out to
those who collect unjust taxes (anniyāya-kaḍamai) and to the village accountants and others
who collude with those tax collectors. The intended punishments to the culprits were plucking
of eyes and cutting of the noses (in the case of Brāhmaṇas), death (for the village accountants),
ex-communication for artisans, etc. Finally, to mark the important occasion, the periyanāḍu
decided to collect from the kār season of the seventh year some fixed quantity of grain or labour
(kuḍimai kuṟuṇi) from each of the member villages towards the making and maintenance of
the compound wall of the temple called Periyanāṭṭān-tirumāḷigai (named after the assembly).

l.ta: •••••••••• mak koruvarkku uṭaṟpaṭṭu eymukamuṅ kuṭuttu kōṭ kuṟaḷai kē •••
kuṭikēṭu keṭuttā ruṇṭākilum aṉṉiyāyaṅkaḷukku uṭaṟ paṭṭāruṇ • • •
pirāmaṇarākil kaṇṇum paṟittu mūkkum aṟukka kaṭavatākavum i • • •
ḷiruvaraippiṇaittum kuṟṟam kuṟṟamaṉṟiyilē teṟṟa kuṟṟattukku taṇṭami • •

llātākavum nāṭupeṟṟuvanta mutal kavaḷamutal kāṇummiṭattu kirāmamum
nāṭum nakara ••••••-

ṇakkaṭavōmākavum ūṟkaṇakkar aṉṉiyāyak kaṭamaikku taraveḻutak
kaṭavatallavākavum

vīrakkoṭiyār kaiyyālē kulai uṇṇakkaṭavakaḷākavum ippaṭi tavira
oruvariruvar • • •

kil nāṭāka kuṭivāṅkipōkak kaṭavōmākavum kuṭivāṅka irukkacceytē nam

inscription.head:
No. 154.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Koṅgu-Chōḻa, Virachōḻadēva, 12th year = A.D. 1195.

Fragment. Seems to record a gift of land by the sabhā of Vīrachōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a
brahmadēya in Taṭṭaiyūr-nāḍu.

l.ta: • • nañ ceyyakkuṭutta nilam eṅkaḷūṟ kamuri nilattil

edition.l:
• • rppaṭi nilam oru māvum iṟaiyili tēvatāṉamāka
[rest damaṅed]

inscription.head:
No. 155.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III, 9+1st year = A.D. 1226.

This mentions a gift of 1000 kāsu for the expenses of the Śivarātri day in the Tirumāṇikkamalai
temple by a merchant of Śivapādaśekhara-puram.

l.ta: malai uṭaiyanāyanārkku civapāta cēkarapurattu viyāpāri ēkapōkaṅkoṇṭāṉ

inscription.head:
No. 156.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III
23rd year = A.D. 1201
This is much damaged. Somebody from Malaimaṇḍalam is referred to.

inscription.head:
No. 157.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
inscription.p:
At the entrance into the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine of the Ratnāchalēśvara
temple, left side.
c. 12th Century.

States that a certain Maṟaikalandāṉ alias Koṅgarai-koṇḍa-Pallavaraiyaṉ, one of the agam-
paḍi-mudalis of Chāttan Chēdirāyaṉ, built the entrance steps and fixed the door to this maṇḍa-
pa and built the tirumaḍai-viḷāgam (temple streets) at the foot of the hill and settled them with
people.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Sundarapāṇḍya, 15th year = c. 12th-13th Century.

The terrace-roof of this maṇḍapam was the joint charity of the nagaram of Śivapādaśēkhara-
puram and the cartmen of Pēṭṭai.

inscription.head:
No. 159.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 12th Century.

This is in verse form and records the construction of the temple on the hill with stone by a
Āḍaviḍaṅga-Pichchaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the Dakshiṇāmūrti shrine in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III, 22nd year = A.D. 1238.

Gift of some land as kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna for offerings to the image of Dakshiṇāmūrtidēva in
the temple by Chiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ Vikkaviṉāyakaṉ a merchant of Śivapādaśēkhara-puram
in Kuṟunagar-nāḍu a unit of Rājagembhīra-vaḷanāḍu. The donor purchased the land from the
nagarattār of his village.

l.ta: ka viṭṭa nilam iraṇṭumāvum kuṭi nīṅkā-

inscription.head:
No. 161.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the Dakshiṇāmūrti in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: [Chōḻa, Rājarāja III] = A.D. 1238.

Records that the merchant mentioned in the above inscription (No. 160) set up the image of
Dakshiṇāmūrtidēva and built the steps.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the Dakshiṇāmūrti in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: [Chōḻa, Rājarāja III] = A.D. 1238.

Gift of money by the merchant mentioned in 161 above for a lamp to be lit in the shrine of
Dakshiṇāmūrtidēva.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the kitchen in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple, right side.
Hoysaḷa, Vīra-Rāmanāthadēva, 13th year = A.D. 1267.
Only the king's name is available.

inscription.head:
No. 164.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the kitchen in the Ratnāchalēśvara temple, left side.
c. 13th Century.
Beginning lost. Somebody built the kitchen east of the Maṇi-maṇḍapa.

inscription.head:
No. 165.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock outside the south prākāra wall of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga I, 48th year = A.D. 1118.

Partly built in. It records that a Kiḻavan of Karuṅgālippaḷḷi purchased land from some individ-
uals.

inscription.head:
No. 166.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock outside the south prākāra wall of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga I, •••= A.D. 1070-1120.
Built in and damaged. Seems to register a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 167.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock outside the west prākāra wall of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra II, 7th year = A.D. 1059.

Built in and incomplete. Lines 1—30 give the meykkīrtti (eulogy) of the king with several or-
thographical mistakes. The last three lines refer to some transaction between the nagaram of
Sivapādasēkarapuram and a Viḻupparaiyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 168.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock outside the west prākāra wall of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra I, •••= A.D. 1012-44.

This records a sale of land for offerings to the temple of Kuṉṟam-uḍaiyār, by the sabhā of Val-
lada-maṅgalam a brahmadēya in Mīkōṭtu-nāḍu, a unit of Kēraḷāntaka-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 169.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock outside the west prākāra wall of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 4th year, Mithuna, Monday, Pūrvāshāḍha = A.D. 1181,
May 31.

This registers a sale of land to the temple of Tirumāṇikkamalai-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār by a native of
Kuḻittaṇḍalai. The sabhā of Kuḻittaṇḍalai made the land tax-free on receiving the tax capital
from another person called Ārūran Kūttan alias Kīḻāṟṟūr-nāḍāḻvār.

inscription.head:
No. 170.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock outside the west prākāra wall of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, viruppanna, s/o Ariyana, Śukla, Mīna, sudi 8, Thursday, Punarvasu =
A.D. 1390, March 24, Thursday.

This is an exceptional inscription recording sale, by a Brāhmaṇa, of his Veḷḷāḷa slaves (veḷḷāṉ
aḍimaigaḷ) most probably to the temple. The sale price of 270 irāsi-paṇam was received by
the seller in the presence of one Chilai-cheṭṭiyār of Rājēndirachōḻa-pēṭṭai as witness. It is said
that the seller, Vēlippiḷḷai Deyvanayana-bhaṭṭaṉ Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-biramādarāyaṉ of Pālarai-
kuḍi in Naḍuvil-maṇḍalam, had got the possession of the slaves by gift from his uterine sister
Koḍi-vayiraṅgaṭṭiṉa-sāṉi, who was the wife of Tiruvādavūr Poṉmēyndachōḻa-biramārāyar of
Uttamachōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Chūralūr-kūṟṟam in Rājagembīra-vaḷanāḍu. The slaves
included one Chūḍiyār, her four children, and grandchildren too, both male and female members.

l.ta:
rāyara kaṇṭan ariyaṇa uṭaiyar kumāraṉ viruppaṇa uṭaiyār irācciyam
paṇṇi aruḷā niṉṟa

inscription.head:
No. 171.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock outside the north prākāra wall of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān [Kulōttuṅga III], 16th year, 340th day = A.D. 1194

This is a king's order addressed to the temple trustees conveying his decision that he had pleased
to assign all the tax revenue from some thirty-two old dēvadāna villages, ranging from (the big
village) Ālavāy to (the small village) Āḍi, in Kuṟunagar-nāḍu for celebrating Pūram festival in
the month of Vaigāsi praying for his health in the temple of Tirumāṇikkamalai-uḍaiya-Nāyaṉār.
The road Ādaṉ-peruvaḻi is found among the boundaries. The tirumandira-ōlai officer Nēri-
ya-mūvēndavēḷaṉ wrote the royal order. The four boundaries of Kuṟunagar-nāḍu are mentioned
as the fortification wall called Kōṭṭaikkarai-madiḷ on the west, the highroad Ādaṉ-peruvaḻi on the
north, the boundary of Vaḍa-kōṉāḍu on the south, and the boundary of Rājagambhīra-vaḷanāḍu
on the east. The tirumandira-ōlai Nēriya-mūvēndavēḷān wrote the royal order.

l.ta:
ṇikkamalai uṭaiya nāyaṉār kōyil tēvar kaṉmikaḷukkum
māhē

ceyvārkaḷukkum śrīkāriyam ceyvāṉṉukkum tēvarkkuḷḷa paḻam
tēvatānamā-

y varukiṟa kōṭṭaikaraimatiḷḷukku kiḻakkum ātaṉ peru[vaḻi]kku teṟkkum
vaṭakōṉ
ālavāy talaiyāka kuṟunakar nāṭṭu pala ūr

eṭuppār
kum palapaṇi nimantattukkum uṭalāka iṟaiyiliyāka aṉaittāyaṅkaḷum tavintu

ippaṭikkuc cempilum cilaiyilum veṭṭikoḷvatāka variyillārum
varikkūṟu ce-

eḻut

inscription.head:
No. 172.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock outside the north prākāra wall of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya, 13th year, Tulā, badi 13, Monday,
Śravaṇa = A.D. 1264, January 28, Monday (taking the month as
Kumbha for Tulā).

This records the resolution of a dispute between the temples of Tirumāṇikkamalai-uḍai-
ya-Nāyaṉār, etc. in Kuṟunagar-nāḍu a unit of Rājagambhīra-vaḷanāḍu and the nagarattār of
the town Śivapādaśēkhara-puram as regards the payment of taxes by the people residing re-
spectively in the temple streets of some three temples and in the town. On hearing the dispute
he deputed a chief officer (mudali) accompanied with another mudali of Perumāḷ Vīrapāṇḍya
(perhaps the Prince) to enquire and settle the dispute. The two officers and the nāḍavar of
Kuṟunagar-nāḍu, Mīkōṭṭu-nāḍu, and Ādaṉūr-nāḍu met at Kuḻittaṇḍalai and resolved that the
occupants of the the temple streets, namely the Śivabrāhmaṇas, temple girls, kaikkōḷar, etc.,
should pay their taxes to the respective temples and the herding people living in the town but
rearing the livestock of the temples should pay to the nagaram body. The occupants of the two
sides should not migrate to the other side hereafter. A number of persons including several
Cheṭṭis have attested the document.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock outside the north prākāra wall of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 6th year, Mīna, sudi 13, Maghā, Sunday =
A.D. 1262, March 5.

The ūrāḷi-nāṭṭavar of Koḍukkayam, Kumaṭṭēri, Chātta-maṅgalam and Varagūr in Kuṟunagai-
nāḍu a unit of Rājagembīṟa-vaḷanāḍu decided to contribute a sum of 350 paṇam for meeting
the expenses of the Pūram festival in the month of Māsi in the temple of Tirumāṇikkamalai-
uḍaiya-Nāyaṉār.

inscription.head:
No. 174.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock outside the north prākāra wall of the Ratnāchalēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra I, 24th year = A.D. 1036.

This records that the sabhā of some brahmadēya village (name lost) in Mīkōṭṭu-nāḍu of
Kēraḷāntaka-vaḷanāḍu sold some land, freed from taxes, for a sum of 42 Rājarājaṉ-paḻaṅkāsu
to the temple of Tiruvāṭpōkki-Mahādēva. It is said 4 kāsu was the sale price and the rest the
tax capital.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya, 10th year, Makara, sudi 6, Friday, Ut-
tara-Bhādrapadā = A.D. 1261.

Dēvaṉ Uyyāṉ Arumpukalaḻitta-pāṇḍiya-dēvaṉ, a mudali belonging to the retinue called Agap-
parivāram of Chōḻapperumāḷ (the Chōḻa king ?) purchased land from the nagarattār of Sivapā-
dasēkarapuram and presented the same for the sacred bath and offerings on the day of Tiruvādi-
rai in the month of Mārgaḻi in the temple of Tirumāṇikkamalai-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār.

inscription.head:
No. 176.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Hoysaḷa, Vīra Sōmēśvara, 20st year, Mēsha, sudi 13, Sunday, Hasta =
A.D. 1253, April 13, Sunday.

This records a gift of 20,000 kāsu by Tiruññāsambanda-nambi, a merchant of Śivapādaśekha-
ra-puram for offerings to the image of Tirukkāmakkōṭṭam-uḍaiya-Nachchiyār that he set up
in the temple of Tirumāṇikkamalai and also for the expenses of providing meals to pilgrims
during midday service.

inscription.head:
No. 177.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the first prākāra of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, 36th year, Rh;shabha, sudi 3, Saturday,
Rōhiṇī = A.D. 1106, May 5, Saturday. (tithi is wrong).

Gift of land for the sacred bath of the deity in the temple Tiruvāḻpōkkimalai-Mahādēva in
Kuṟunāgaṉ-nāḍu, a unit of Rājamahēndra-vaḷanāḍu. The donor, a merchant of Śivapādaśēkha-
ra-puram, purchased it from the sabhā of Kuḻittaṇḍalai, a brahmadēya in Mīkōṭṭu-nāḍu. It is
said that the sold land had been lying fallow without owners and hence added to the village
common.

inscription.head:
No. 178.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the first prākāra of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, 47th Makara, sudi, Thursday, 13, Hasta
= A.D. 1117.

Badly worn out. Seems to record assignment of some temple land for feeding pilgrims (apūr-
vi), for which an individual deposited some money with the temple.

peruvari cilvari cennīrveṭṭi kaṭavatallavākavum innilattukku catiravaṟutik
kaṇṇāṟṟāl nīrpāñci ko[ḷ]vatāka sammatittu cempilu ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 179.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga I, 40th year, Dhanus, badi 10, Punarvasu, Friday =
A.D. 1109, December 10, Friday.

This records that a merchant of Rājarāja-puram in Vaḍa-Koṅgu, who had set up also the image
of Tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai-Nāchchiyār purchased land from the sabhā of Kuḻittaṇḍalai in Mīkōṭṭu-nāḍu
of Rājamahēndra-vaḷanāḍu and endowed it for feeding dēvaraḍiyār, śivayōgis and tapasvis in
the maṭha called Eḻunūṟṟuvaṉ-tirumaḍam near the western gate of Śivapādaśēkhara-puram in
Kuṟunāgai-nāḍu. The merchant and his wife conveyed the gift through a local agent.

inscription.head:
No. 180.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga I, 39th year, Kumbha, badi, Sunday, 14, Śravaṇa =
A.D. 1109, January 31, Sunday.

Registers a similar gift of land after purchase as in the above inscription (No. 179) by two kaik-
kōḷa members of Rājarājapuram in North Koṅgu. The same local agent figures in this record
also.

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 181.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, 38th year, Karkaṭaka, sudi, . . .,
Pūrva-Phālgunī Monday = A.D. 1108.

It is said that Rājādhirājaṉ Vīranārāyaṇaṉ hailing from North Koṅgu made a gift of land for
providing two potfuls of water for the sacred bath in the temple of Tiruvāṭpōkkimalai-Mahādē-
va. The donor purchased the land, freed from taxes, from the sabhā of Kuḻittaṇḍalai.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷp puṇara
urimaiyiṟ ciṟa

ṉavar riyaluṟṟiḻitara tikkaṉaittun taṉcakkara naṭātti vīrasiṅhāsaṉattu
avaṉimuḻutuṭaiyāḷōṭum vīṟṟiruntaruḷiya kōvirājakēcari pa

yaiyum pūramum peṟṟa tiṅkaḷ kiḻamai nāḷ ivvūr naṭuviṟ tirumuṟṟattu
rājē

cōḻaṉ tirumaṇṭapattē taṉmi ceytu kūṭṭa kuṟaivaṟak kūṭiyiruntu
iṉnāṭṭu kuṟunāka nāṭṭu[vā]-

inscription.head:
No. 182.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, 43rd year = A.D. 1133.

Registers a gift of money for sandhi lamps in the temple of Tiruvāḷpōkki-malai in Kuṟunā-
ga-nāḍu by a merchant of Śivapādśēkhara-puram in the same nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 183.
183, Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Vikramachōḻa, 6th year = A.D. 1124.
Records an endowment of money for a perpetual lamp in the temple.

poṉ uṭpaṭa muṉ
collappaṭṭa civappirāmaṇarōm ikkaṭappūruṭaiyāṉ caṭutiyaṉ po-

inscription.head:
No. 184.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājādhirāja II, 3rd year = A.D. 1166.
This records gift of a sum of 24 kāsu for sandhi lamps by a Vēḷāṉ of Kuḻittaṇḍalai.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the north wall of the Chōḷēśvara shrine in front of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 20th year, Vṛiśchika, badi 8, Monday, Pūrva-Pūrva-
Phālgunī = A.D. 1198, November 23, Monday.

Gift of an amount of 8 āṉai-achchu otherwise called paḻañjalākai-achchu for offerings
to the image of Āriya-Nāyaṉār. The donor was a merchant of Sivapādasēkarapuram, called
Nāvalūr-uḍaiyāṉ Kāḷi Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ alias Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-uṟandairāyan.

l.ta:

karuvūruṅkoṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulOttuṅka cOḻatEvaṟkku yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 186.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock below the Chōḷēśvara shrine in front of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, 11th year, Vṛiśchika, Monday =
A.D. 1081.

This records a sale of land by the ūrār of Kannamātūr to the temple on the hill Tiruvāṭpōk-
kimalai. An uḍaiyāṉ of Māyaṉ-marutūr in Mīkōṭtu-nāḍu in Sarvalōkāśraya-vaḷanāḍu paid the
sale price (2 kāsu) and also the tax capital (16 kāsu) to the ūrār.

naṭappa viḷaṅku jayamakaḷai iḷaṅkOparuvattu

inscription.head:
No. 187.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock below the Chōḷēśvara shrine in front of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, •••badi 6, Thursday = A.D. 1070-1120.

Much mutilated. It seems to record purchase of tax-free land by a merchant from the sabhā of
Kuḻittaṇḍalai for the temple of Tiruvāppōkkimalai.

inscription.head:
No. 188.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock below the Chōḷēśvara shrine in front of the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra I, 22nd year = A.D. 1034.

This records a sale of land to the temple on the Tiruvāṭpōkki-malai hill by the ūrār of Kāruṅgālip-
paḷḷi in Mīkōṭṭu-nāḍu. The sale deed was written by a local Veḷḷāḷa.

inscription.head:
No. 189.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock near a spring behind the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra I, 21st year = A.D. 1033.

Registers a sale of land, freed from taxes, by the sabhā of Ariñjikai-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a
brahmadēya in Uṟaiyūr-kūṟṟam in Kēraḷāntaka-vaḷanāḍu to the temple of Śrīkayilāsam alias
Madhurāntak-Īśvaram at Rājēndrachōḻa-pēṭṭai in Mīykōṭṭu-nāḍu. A part of the sold land was
lying fallow for some fifteen years past as the owner had absconded without paying taxes. Con-
sequently the the land was sold in government auction (Rājēndrachōḻa-peruvilai) for the tax
arrears; the sabhā purchased it and was keeping it as public property. [For some inexplicable
reasons this and the following inscription (190), belonging to different villages are inscribed
on the rock in Ratnagiri hill even though they have nothing to do with the local village or the
temple on the hill].

[35-40 siṅnatures much worn out].

inscription.head:
No. 190.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock near a spring behid the Harāḷakēśvari temple on the hill.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājēndra I, 21st year = A.D. 1033.

This is a record of sale of land, freed from taxes, by the sabhā of a village (name lost, but
most probably Ariñjikai-chaturvēdimaṅgalam) in Uṟaiyūr-kūṟṟam in Kēraḷāntaka-vaḷanāḍu to
the Vishṇu temple of Jananātha-viṇṇagar-Āḻvār in Mīkōṭṭu-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 191.
Ratnagiri, Kulittalai Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock near the Ponnaḍippāṟai on the hill.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya, Śaka 1467, Krōdhin, Śrāvaṇa, badi,
Kṛishṇashashthī = 1544 August 11.

Records that Timma made a grant to the god on the hill Ratnagiri under the orders of Rāmarā-
ja-Viṭṭalarāja. This gives a genealogical account of the Āravīṭi family commencing with Āravīṭi
Bukka. His son Rāma (Rāmarāja I), his three sons Timmarāja, Koṇḍōya (Peda-Koṇḍarāja) and
Raṅgarāja and the four sons of Timmarāja, namely Nalla-Timma, Viṭṭhala, China Timma and
Pāpa-Timma. These four are stated in the inscription to have been entitled Vīravasanta. Under
orders from king Sadāśiva and his minister Rāmarāja, Viṭṭhala and China-Timma started on a
victorious campaign right up to the shore of the southern ocean and made the whole country
free from oppressive rulers. In the śaka year 1467 the two brothers together with their relations
set up pillars of victory at Rāmasētu (Rāmēśvaram) and Kanya-kumarika in order to establish
their fame. Dharmarāja of Kākkaḷūr composed the inscription. The same donor and his elder
brother figure in another inscription in the same temple. (No. 148).

(Text not traceable) As both the estampage and transcript are not traceable in the archives of the Department due to some
misplacement, the detailed comments of the Epigraphist H. Krishna Sastry in part II of the Annual
Report for 1914—15 are reproduced here. Unfortunately a field visit by the present editor to the actual
site was also disappointing as the entire rock on the spot is engraved by the devotees with their foot-
prints, which are in hundreds (hence the name of the rock pon-aḍi-pāṟai ‘rock of golden feet’), thereby
making it difficult to recognize the old inscriptions, if any.

inscription.head:
No. 192.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the central shrine in the Vamsōddhārakanātha temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Vīrapāṇḍya, 14th year, Āni 1 = A.D. 1267.

This concerns a dispute between the ūrār of Peruṅgaḷūr and Maṅgalam regarding the right of
ownership of Ariyūr, a dēvadāna village of the Kāḷamāmuṉi-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār temple in Teṉ-
paṉāṅgāḍu-nāḍu in Jeyasiṅgakulakāla-vaḷanāḍu. The mudali who was in charge of the nāḍu
seems to have resolved the dispute. The end portion is missing.
[Published in IPS, No. 370]

inscription.head:
No. 193.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vamsōddhārakanātha temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulottuṅga I, 45th year = A.D. 1115.

The ūrār of Peruṅgāḷūr gifted some land as ūr-kīḻ-iṟaiyili to the temple of Kulōttuṅga-chōḻīś-
varam-uḍaiyār in the village. Some part containing signatures is missing in the end.
[Published in IPS, No. 228]

inscription.head:
No. 194.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Vamsōddhārakanātha temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 29th year = A.D. 1325.

This records a deed of asylum (āsaṟiya-pramāṇam) given to the ūrār of Peruṅgaḷūr in Teṉ-
Paṉaṅgāḍu-nāḍu a unit of Jayasiṅgakulakāla-vaḷanāḍu, by two araiyar members of Vat-
tāṇḍār-Kōṭṭai. In return for getting the protection, the two araiyas promised to give some
specified quantity of paddy to the ūrār.
[Published in IPS, No. 615]

l.ta: rāka icainta ūravaṟku teṉmalaināṭṭu vattaṇṭār kō-
ṭṭai araiyakaḷil vattaṇṭār tēvaṉāṉa naraiñat tēva-
ṉu mivaṉ tampi kaṟuppāṇṭāṉu miviruvarōm ācaṟiya-
piramāṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu iṉāḷ mutal ca-
ntirātittavarai eṅkaḷai kāttu nōkkip pōtak ka-

inscription.head:
No. 195.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vamsōddhārakanātha temple
inscription.p: c. 18th Century.

In modern characters. Records the Swing maṇḍapa and the images of Sundiraperumāḷ and
Sūrya were the gifts of an individual.

inscription.head:
No. 196.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vamsōddhārakanātha temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th-14th Century.
Gift of land by an individual out of his kāṇi in Chiṟukaḷūr for a lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 197.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vamsōddhārakanātha temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th-14th Century.

Gift of a lamp to the deity Kūttāḍundēvar in the temple of Kulōttuṅga-chōḻīśvaram-uḍaiyār at
Peruṅgaḷūr.

l.ta: muṭaiyār tirukōyilil kūttāṭum tēvar tirumuṉpu eriya tiruviḷakku

inscription.head:
No. 198.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vamsōddhārakanātha temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th-14th Century.

This seems to record a gift of land by a native of Peruṅgōḻiyūr as a compensation to an individ-
ual for falsely incriminating the latter before the royal officials (irājakarattilē kāṭṭinapaḍiyāle).

l.ta:
āyakuḷattāpaṭa vappipaṇṭi ivarai nāṉ irācakarattilē kāṭṭiṉapaṭiyālē
eṉṉai irācakarattilē kāṭṭi

kōṭṭaiyilk kuṭuppāṉēṉ eṉṟu eṉṉai paḻi nāṭiṉapaṭiyālē ivaṟku
tantu viṭṭa nilam
eṉkāṇiyāṉa putuvayal ceṅkāṭaṉ māvaṭi cey ivaṟku viṭṭatu inta nilam

inscription.head:
No. 199.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vamsōddhārakanātha temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th-14th Century.

Damaged badly. Seems to record an asylum deed given to the ūr-kaṇmāḷar of both the wings
of Peruṅgaḷūr in Teṉ-panaṅgāḍu-nāḍu by some kaṇmāḷar. The concluding details are lost.
[Published in IPS, No. 965.]

makaṉ cāttaṉ uḷḷiṭṭārrōm ippaṭi cāttaṉ uḷḷiṭṭār coṟpaṭi

inscription.head:
No. 200.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vamsōddhārakanātha temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Bukkaṇa-uḍaiyar, s/o Ariyaṇa-uḍaiyar, Chitrabhānu, Puraṭṭādi 7 =
A.D. 1402, September 5.

This records an āchiṟaya-pramāṇam. An araiya of Vārappūr in Kīḻchūdi-nāḍu donated some
of his kāṇi land for offerings to the temple of Kulōttuṅga-chōḻīśvaram-uḍaiyār in lieu of his
asylum with the ūravar of Peruṅgōḻiyūr in Teṉ-Paṉanaṅgāḍu-nāḍu, a unit of Jayasiṅgakulakā-
la-vaḷanāḍu.
[Published in IPS, No. 693.]

varāyara kaṇṭaṉ ariyaṇa uṭaiyār kumāra pokkaṇa uṭaiyāṟku cellā
niṉṟa

paṉaṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭup peruṅkōḷiyūr ūravaṟku kīḻ cūti nāṭṭu vārappūr
araiyarka-

inscription.head:
No. 201.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vamsōddhārakanātha temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Bhūpatirāya, Śarvadhāri, Puraṭṭādi 12 = A.D. 1408, September 10.

An asylum deed given to the ūravar of Peruṅgōḻiyūr by an araiya called Vāchchāṉ Chēmap-
piḷḷai of Muṉṉāttūr in Vaḍamalai-nāḍu in Vaḍa-panaṅgāḍu. The latter agreed in return to give
some land in Muṉṉāttūr as well as his pāḍikāval income from a village for offerings in the
Kulōttuṅga-chōḻīśvaram-uḍaiyār temple.
[Published in IPS, No. 696]

l.ta: varāyar kaṇṭaṉ pūpatirāyaṉukku cellāniṉṟa caṟuvatāri varuṣam puraṭṭā-
tiyati nāḷ jayaciṅka kulakālavaḷanāṭṭu teṉpaṉaṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu
pperuṅkōḷiyūr ūravaṟku vaṭapaṉaṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu vaṭamalaināṭṭu muṉ-
ṉāttūr araiyakaḷil vāccāṉ cēmappiḷḷaiyāṉnēṉ āciṟiyap pira-
māṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu iṉṉāḷ mutal cantirātitta varaikku āci-
[cura]muṭaiya nāyaṉārku ammutupaṭikku nāṉ kuṭutta nilamāvatu muṉṉāttūr

inscription.head:
No. 202.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
inscription.p:
At the entrance into the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vamsōddhārakanātha
temple.
Vijayanagara, •••rāyar, = c. 15th Century.

Beginning damaged. This records an asylum deed given to the ūravar of Peruṅgōḻiyūr (including
both the western and eastern sections) in Teṉ-Panaṅgāḍu-nāḍu in Jayasiṅgakulakāla-vaḷanāḍu
by two araiyas of Pūkkuḍi in Vaḍamalai-nāḍu of Vaḍa-Panaṅgāḍu. For getting the necessary
protection the araiyas made a gift of land for offerings in the temple of Aḻagiya-kulōttuṅgar.
[Published in IPS, No. 765.]

inscription.head:
No. 203.
Perungaḷūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On stones built into the Mangaḷa-Nāyaki shrine in the Vamsōddhārakanātha temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, •••= c. 10th Century.

These are fragments in the tenth-century characters. The first one mentions Chembiyaṉ
Indavadi-araiyaṉ making a gift of gold. The second dated in the reign of Rājakēsarivarman
refers to a mercenary soldier (kūlichchēvagaṉ). Other bits mention the temple of Mahādēva at
Choḻachūḷamaṇi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and the ūrār of Peruṅgōḷūr and Chāttaṉūr.

inscription.head:
No. 204.
Veḷḷanūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Agastyēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, [Māṟavarman] Sundarapāṇḍya 1, •••= A.D. 1216-38.

This fragment seems to provide for offerings to the temple of Tiruvagattīśvaram-uḍaiyār at
Veḷḷai-nallūr.

inscription.head:
No. 205.
Veḷḷanūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Agastyēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara, 22nd year, sudi 7 = A.D. 1290.

Records a gift of land for offerings to the temple of Śrīkailāsa at Veḷḷai-nallūr in Teṉ-Chiṟuvāy-
il-nāḍu in Jesiṅgakulakāla-vaḷanāḍu. The donor Aḻagaṉ Atiravīchi-āḍuvār of Mayindrapuram
in Mēl-Vēmba-nāḍu purchased two thirds of the land possessed by some individuals in Veḷḷain-
allūr in Teṉ-chiṟuvāyil-nāḍu. The latter themselves donated the remaining one third of their
possession. The local nāḍu body exempted the land from payment of taxes.
[Published in IPS, No. 540.]

inscription.head:
No. 206.
Veḷḷanūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Agastyēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Vīrarājēndra [Kulōttuṅga III], 7th year = A.D. 1185.

This is in verse form mentioning the construction of the ardha-maṇḍapa in the temple of
Agastīśvara at Veḷḷai-nallūr by a Muḍikoṇḍa-nāḍāḻvāṉ of Chiṟuvāyil-nāḍu.

cūḻāliyar kōṉmanila kaṟpakamēy §

inscription.head:
No. 207.
Veḷḷanūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south and east walls of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Agastyēśvara
temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 23rd year = A.D. 1239.

Records an agreement among the ūrār of Veḷḷai-nallūr to conduct the 10-day festival in the
month of Chittirai and mentions the names of groups (aḍaiviluḷḷār) of persons like the group
of Chembiyaṉ Teṉkarai-nāṭṭu-viḻupparaiyar, the group of Jeyaṅgoṇḍa-vēḷār, etc., responsible to
conduct the festival each day.
[Published in IPS, No. 320, lines 1—12 only.]

l.ta:
vatu ceyaciṅkakula kāla vaḷanāṭṭut teṉvāyil nāṭṭu [ve]ḷḷai
nallūr ūrāyicainta ūrOm

ra muṭai[ya] nāyaṉār cittirai

ril nīr vārttu nāṅkaḷ niccayittapaṭi piḷḷaiyār viḻā ik-
ḷivikkak kaṭavōmākavum mūṉṟān tirunāḷ kulōttuṅka vē[ḷā]

inscription.head:
No. 208.
Veḷḷanūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Agastyēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya, Śaka 1470, Saumya, Makara, Purva 12,
Monday = A.D. 1549, Dec. 3, Monday.

The first two lines, forming a Pāṇḍya inscription, refer to the Pāṇḍya king Sundara-pāṇḍya,
who took the Chōḻa country and it has apparently no relation to the Vijayanagara inscription
from the end of line 2. Gift of the tax revenue from the entire village of Kaiyiṟ-kuḍi by the
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Immaḍi Akkaladēva-chōḻa-mahārāja for the midday worship and for festi-
vals in the Tiruvagattīśvaram temple at Veḷḷainallūr in Teṉchiṟuvāyil-nāḍu in Jeyasiṅgakulakā-
la-vaḷanāḍu. The donor has the attributes Torai-vīmaṉ and sāmanta-nārāyaṇaṉ.
[Published in IPS, No. 754]

l.ta: cōnāṭu koṇṭaruḷiya cuntarapāṇṭiya tē[vaṟku yāṇ] •••• ceyaciñka kulakā-
la vaḷanāṭṭu teṉciṟuvayanāṭṭu veḷḷai na[l]lūr uṭaiyār tiruvakattī-
yīcuramuṭaiya nāyanāṟku catācivarāyaṉ piṟa[tivirāj]yam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa
ṉ mēl cellā niṉṟa cavumiya ca[m]vatccarattu makaranāyaṟṟu
pūṟuva pakkaṣattu tuvāteciyum cōma vāramum peṟṟa ciṟimaṉ makāmaṇṭalīcu[ra]
ṉ tōraivīmaṉ cāmanta nārāyaṇaṉ yimmaṭi akkala teyva cōḻa makā-
rācāviṉ taṉmamāka innāṭṭukku uḷpaṭṭa kaiyiṟkuṭi kiṟāmam oṉṟukku nat-
tam ceyttalaittiṭal nañcai puñcai māvaṭai maravaṭai cāraṭai calatta-
ram ma[ṟṟum] •••eṟpeyaṟpaṭṭaṉavum nāḷatu varaiyum patintum varum tāṉa-

tiruppaṇikkum āka viṭṭa paṭiyālē intapaṭikku cantirātityavaraiyum naṭan-

tuvarakkaṭavatākavum intappaṭikku naṭattāmal akutam paṇṇinavaṉ keṅkai
karai-

mātāpitāvaiyum

koṉṟa pāvattilē pōka kaṭavarākavum iṟpaṭikku akkala teyva
cōḻamakarācāvi-

nampiyār tekṣṇā-
mūrtti kayilāya nātar eḻuttu civanta ceṅkoṇṭa vēḷāṉ eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 209.
Satyamangalam, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west and south bases of the Chōḷēśvara temple.
inscription.p: . . ., 13th year Tai = c. 13th-14th Century.

A temple girl called Tāyagam Maṟakkavoṇṇātār of Veḷḷai-nallūr in Chiṟuvāy-nāḍu was per-
mitted to enjoy some temple land as kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna with the condition that she should
contribute to the temple a stipulated quantity of paddy as fixed by the temple trustees. It is said
that she got the right from purchase of the land from an earlier holder of the said land.

l.ta:
vatu tai mātam teṉ ciṟuvāyiṉāṭṭu veḷḷai nallūruṭai •••• muṭaiya
nāyaṉār kōyil tēvaraṭiyāril tāyakam maṟakkavoṇṇātārkku kuṭi nīṅkāt
tēvatāṉam piramāṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu perumpuñcai uṭaiyār
tiruperumāḷ nāyiṉār tēvatāṉamāṉa vīrapattiraṉ kuṭikkāṭ[ṭu cākka]-

edition.l:
vayakkalum kiṇaṟum [ka]lamati kalpakkālum ēttum ēttakkālum itu cūḻnta
maraṅkaḷum mōluḷḷa kuḷa [panā]ṉa muṭikoṇṭa cōḻanāṭāḻvāṉ pakka vilai
koṇṭuṭaiya cunti vayakkalukku vilai niṟceyittu kuṭutta orumā- 80 ippaṇam
eṇpatukkum vō[lai*] eḻuti tanta cātaṉappaṭiyē maṟakka oṇṇā[tār]

pātta nilattukku vīra parikalaṉ kālāl

[māttāl] aṟukalanellum āṭikkuṟuvai araivaricaiyum aṟpaci kuṟuvai
mukkāl varicaiyu māttāl antarāya mūṉṟu paṇamum tāṅ koṇṭu iṟukkavum

cōḻavēḷ eḻuttu mīṉavaṉ muttaraiyar taṟkuṟi aruntavaṉ virupparaiya
eḻuttu aḻakiyacōḻa nāṭāḻvār

ku(pa) ōmaḻakiya paṭṭar taṟkkuṟi tēvāṉai eḻuttu muṭipēraiyaṉ eḻuttu
muṭikoṇṭa koṇṭa kollaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku cāttan āṉaiyar eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 210.
Sembāṭṭūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Tiruvāruḍaiyār temple.
inscription.p: c. 12th-13th Century.

Records the construction of the stone temple by Aḻagiya-Chēmaṉ Arasagaṇḍa Rāmaṉ alias
Nāvilaṅgādāṉ.
[Published in IPS, No. 1054]

inscription.head:
No. 211.
Sembāṭṭūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Tiruvāruḍaiyār temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya, 4th year = c. 13th-14th Century.

The ūrār of three villages, namely Chembāḍu, Kōraikkurichchi and Puttāmbūr in Kuḷamaṅ-
gala-nāḍu a unit of Jayasiṅgakulakāla-vaḷanāḍu assigned some land in each of their villages
for the maintenance of a tank in Chembāḍu. The accountant of the nāḍu was to supervise the
measurements of the desilting pits in the tank.
[Published in IPS, No. 477]

l.ta: araiyaṉ vayalum āka nilam 1kāṇi m kōraik
tu mēl vāramum kuḷattillē kuḻi veṭṭi
nāṭṭu kaṇakku eṟikuḻi aḷantu kuṭuttu itukku

inscription.head:
No. 212.
Sembāṭṭūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruvāruḍaiyār temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Sundarapāṇḍya, •••= c. 13th-14th Century.

Some unnamed person made a gift of land for a lamp to the temple of Tiruvaiyaṟ-uḍaiya-Nāyaṉār
at Chembāḍu alias Chembiyaṉ Puttāmbūr.
[Published in IPS, No. 526.]

vaḷanāṭṭu kuḷamaṅkala ṉāṭṭu cempāṭṭūrāṉa cempiyaṉ puttā-

inscription.head:
No. 213.
Sembāṭṭūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruvāruḍaiyār temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Sundarapāṇḍya, 19th year = 13th Century.

Gift of land by purchase as kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna to the goddess shrine of the Tiruvaiyāṟuḍai-
ya-nāyaṉār temple at Chembāḍu by a kaikkōḷa of the same temple.

l.ta: kkōḷaril periyāḷ tēvaṉ koṇṭu nā •••nāṭṭil kaḻittu caṅkā-

inscription.head:
No. 214.
Sembāṭṭūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruvāruḍaiyār temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 27th year = A.D. 1205.

Records that the Śivabrāhmaṇas of Tiruvaiyāṟuḍaiya-nāyaṉār temple at Chembāḍu received
some temple land for offerings in a sandhi.
[Published in IPS, No. 224]

l.ta: tiripuvanac cakkarattikaḷ kulōttuṅka cōḻa tēvaṟku yāṇṭu
vatu ceyaciṅka kulakāla vaḷaṉāṭṭuk kuḷamaṅkala nāṭṭuc cempāṭāṉa cempiya-
ṉ puttāmpūr uṭaiyār tiruvaiyāṟuṭaiya nāyaṉār kōyil muppatu vaṭṭa-
ttu civappirāmaṇarōm uṭaiyār tiruvaiyāruṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku oru ca-
nti amutupaṭikku upaiyamākak kaikkoṇṭa nilam periyā vayakka-
