ttavarai oru canti nāṉāḻi arici celuttakkaṭavōmāka kaikkoṇṭōm §

inscription.head:
No. 215.
Sembāṭṭūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruvāruḍaiyār temple.
inscription.p: Hoysaḷa, Vīra Sōmēśvara, 23rd year, Tai 11, Thursday = A.D. 1258.

This records that the mahāpradhāni Siṅgaṇṇa-deṇḍanāyakkar, who was the son of mahāpradhāni
Siṅgaradēva-daṇḍanāyaka set up the image of goddess in the temple.
[Published in IPS, No. 667.]

makaṉār śrī maṉumahā pe-

tiruvaiyāṟuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku tiruppaḷḷi-

inscription.head:
No. 216.
Sembāṭṭūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruvāruḍaiyār temple.
inscription.p: [Pāṇḍya], Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān, 17th year, 360th day = c. 13th Century.

The king granted one vēli of land, on the request of his son-in-law Aḻagapperumāḷ, as tax-free
dēvadāna for offerings to the gods Tiruvaiyāṟuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār, Kūttāḍundēvar and Tambirāṭṭiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 217.
Sembāṭṭūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruvāruḍaiyār temple.
inscription.p: [Pāṇḍya], Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān, 10th year = c. 13th Century.

Badly damaged. This records a grant of half a vēli of land by the king as tax-free dēvadāna for
offerings to the deity Tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai-Nāchchiyār in the temple. The same son-in-law mentioned
in the above record (No. 216) seems to have requested the king for this grant too.
[Published in IPS, No. 661.]

inscription.head:
No. 218.
Sembāṭṭūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruvāruḍaiyār temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Sundarapāṇḍya, 16th year = 13th Century.

The Śivabrāhmaṇas of Tiruvaiyāṟuḍaiya-nāyaṉār temple at Chembāḍu received some temple.
land for offerings in a sandhi. This may be related to the above record.
[Published in IPS, No. 527.]

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī cuntarapāṇṭiya tEvaṟku yāṇṭu
patiṉāṟā-
vatu jayaciṅka kulakāla vaḷanāṭṭu kuḷamaṅkala ṉāṭṭuc cempāṭāṉa cempiyaṉ
puttāmpūr uṭaiyār tiruvaiyāṟuṭaiya ṉāyiṉār kōyil muppatu vaṭṭa-
ttu kāṇi uṭaiya civappirāmaṇarrōm ikkōyil āvuṭaiyār āṇṭa
kūttaṟkum tampirāṭṭiyāṟkum upaiyamāka aṟuṉāḻi aricikkum kaṟi amutum

tiyuṅ kār vayakkalum kaikkoṇṭu ippaṭi cantirātittavarai upaiyamāka

inscription.head:
No. 219.
Sembāṭṭūr, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruvāruḍaiyār temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Sundarapāṇḍya, 6th year = c. 13th Century.

Records a gift of land by Vīrasiṅgarāyaṉ, an araiya of Kōraikkuruchchi in Kuḷamaṅgala-nāḍu
as kuḍi-niṅgā-dēvadāna.
[Published in IPS, No. 509.]

l.ta: vaṟkku yāṇṭu āṟāvatu ce[ya]ciṅka kulakālavaḷa nāṭṭu
ṉēṉ cempāṭṭu ūravar pakkal yiṟaiyili nilamāka nān vi-
lai ko[ṇṭa] iṭai nīkkiyum komaṟaṉ vayakkalum muṟka •••in-
ta taṭi mūṉṟum yiṉṉāyaṉāṟku kuṭinīṅkāt tēvatāṉamāka tiruccū-
kku nāṭṭukkālālē panniru kala ne[llu vi]ḷañca nilattilē aḷa-

inscription.head:
No. 220.
Ammankurichchi, Tirumayam Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a slab set up in the maṇḍapa in front of the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Nayaka, Vīra-Pūchchaya-nāyaka, Śaka 1613, Prajōtpatti, Chittirai 2 = A.D. 1691.

The first part (line 5—12) of the record gives a eulogy of the family of the donor chief Vīra-Pūch-
chaya-Nayakkar, who was the son of Ōvaḷa-Pūchchaya-nāyakkar, who himself was a brother
of Tirumalai-Pūchchaya-nāyakkar. He remitted all taxes on the peasants, merchants and other
citizens (kuḍi-paḍai) living in the tiruviḷaiyāṭṭam village Namaṉakuṟichchi and its six hamlets
in favour of the temple of Chokkar in Namaṉakuṟichchi. However the existing tax-free cate-
gories like dēvadāya, brahmadāya, maḍappuṟam, and umbiḷigai-jīvitam grants were exempted
from the present grant. It is said the order will apply to the immigrant kuḍis from outside too.
The existing rates of fines were also reduced.
[Published in IPS, No. 875.]

l.ta: caṉ rāca kempīraṉ rācamāttāṇṭaṉ racci • • maṇi
kuṟicci il itiṉuṭaṉ cēnta cāṅkaṉ maṉayaṭi mātaṉ
lē inta vakaiyilē oru paṇam orukācākilum

l.note:
This is the name of the Chief and written in Telugu characters. It seems to be placed in a symbolic way
in the centre of the inscription.

l.ta: ṣa parumpiṭi ñāya tappu naṭappikkiṟatillai yeṉṟu
kku ceṭṭi vattakar kuṭiyāṉavar palarum nammai

inscription.head:
No. 221.
Ammankurichchi, Tirumayam Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a slab set up in front of a maṇḍapa in the village.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Rāmadēva-Mahārāya, Śaka 15[4]2, Raudri, Uttarāyaṇa, Mithuna
= A.D. 1630.

A woman called Aṅgāḷammaṉ dug a tank, constructed a maṇḍapa with the Vināyaka image
consecrated in it and also made provision for offerings to the temple of Chokkanāthasvāmi
at Namaṉkurichchi alias Vaḍaveḷi-Ambalavāṇatuṟai, all for the merit of Vīrappa-Pūchchai-
ya-Nāyakkar.
[Published in IPS, No. 760.]

inscription.head:
No. 222.
Ammankurichchi, Tirumayam Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a slab set up in a field in the village.
inscription.p: Nāyaka Chief, Vīrappa Pūchchaya Nāyakkar, Ānanda, Chittirai 17 = A.D. 1614.

Records the construction of the maṭha of Tiruvārūr Ñāṉappiṟakāsa-paṇḍāram by Vīrappa-Pu-
chchaya-Naāyakkar.
[Published in IPS, No. 955.]

inscription.head:
No. 223.
Ambukōvil, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bhaktalalitēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Kulaśēkharadēva, 32nd year = A.D. 1300.

This records that the nagarattār of Kōdaṇḍarāma-puram gave (the revenue of) the entire vil-
lage to the temple of Vīrājēndra-chōḻēśvaram at Aḻimbul.
[Published in IPS, No. 580.]

l.ta:
ṭarāmapurattu nakarattārōm eṅkaḷ kāṇi āṉa nāṉkellaiyum inta
nāyaṉāṟ-

inscription.head:
No. 224.
Ambukōvil, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bhaktalalitēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān Vīrapāṇḍya, 14th year Mēsha, sudi 5, Monday,
Tiruvōṇam = c. 13th Century.

Records that the king remitted the taxes in the village of Mañjiyūr in Puṉṟi-kūṟṟam, for offer-
ings and repairs to Vīrājēndra-chōḻēśvaram temple at Aḻumbil in Paṉṟiyūr-nāḍu.
[Published in IPS, No. 369.]

l.ta: yum tiṅkaḷ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa tiruvōṇattu nāḷ paṉṟi-
yū[r*]ṉāṭṭu aḻimpil ṉāyaṉār vīra rājēntira cōḷī
yaṉāṟkku puṉṟi kūṟṟattu mañciūr iṉṉāyaṉāṟku amutupaṭi cāt-
[ṭ]ṭi koḷka ippaṭikku tiruvāy malantaruḷiṉa paṭikku iḷāṉku-
ṭi uṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu •••ṟu pallavarāyaṉ eḻuttu varuṣam

inscription.head:
No. 225.
Ambukōvil, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bhaktalalitēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Kulaśēkhara, ? = c. 13 Century
Fragment. Only the king's name is available.

inscription.head:
No. 226.
Ambukōvil, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bhaktalalitēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Mallikārjunarāya, Śaka 1499, Pramādicha, Vaigāsi, sudi. =
A.D. 1577 (?)
Stops with the date.

l.ta: lc cellāniṉṟa piṟamātica varuṣam ṣattu vaikāci mātattu pū

inscription.head:
No. 227.
Ambukōvil, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bhaktalalitēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Śrīvallabha, 17+2nd year, Siṁha, sudi 12, Monday, Uttarāshāḍha =
A.D. 1275, August 5, Monday (probably).

Incomplete. The nāṭṭār of Kuḷattūr-nāḍu in Poyyiṟ-kūṟṟam decided to give 30 kalam of paddy
annually to the temple of Vīrarājēndra-chōḻēśvaram at Aḻimbil in Paṉṟiyūr-nāḍu a unit of Rā-
jarāja-vaḷanāḍu.
[Published in IPS, No. 458.]

l.ta:
uttirāṭattu nāḷ rācarāca vaḷanāṭṭup paṉṟiyūr nāṭṭu aḻimpil nāyaṉār
uṭai-
innāyaṉārkku āṇṭoṉṟukku muppatiṉ kala nellu aḷakkak kaṭavōmā-

inscription.head:
No. 228.
Ambukōvil, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bhaktalalitēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Kulaśēkhara, 24th year = c. 13 Century.
Fragmentary. It seems to refer to some transaction by the ūrār of Aḻimbil.

inscription.head:
No. 229.
Ambukōvil, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bhaktalalitēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Sundarapāṇḍya, 14th year = c. 13th-14th Century.

This records that the araiyakaḷ of the ‘five families’ (añjukuḍi) unanimously decided to gift a
half of all the taxes collected on shops and tolls for repairs to the temple at Aḻimbil.
[Published in IPS, No. 522.]

inscription.head:
No. 230.
Ambukōvil, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bhaktalalitēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Sōbhakrhtu = A.D. 1363/1423?

Incomplete. A tirunāmattukkāṇi gift of land to the temple of Aḻumbil in Paṉṟichūḻ-nāḍu in Rā-
jarāja-vaḷanāḍu by the three sons of Pāṇḍya-perumāḷ alias Māvuli-Vāṇādarāyar, chief (kaṟuttā)
of Neḍuvāsal-sīmai in Kāṉava-nāḍu.

l.ta: ḷa nāṭṭup paṉṟi cūḻ nāṭṭu aḻimpinā-
ya nāyaṉāṟkut tiruṉāmattukkāṇi āka viṭṭa nilam

inscription.head:
No. 231.
Ambukōvil, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Bhaktalalitēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Kshaya = c. 14th-15th Century.

This says that the temple trustees of Aḻimbil-nayiṉār and the ten perumpar (?) decided that
the stone (or wooden pole) planted to mark the chēttaḷavu (probably the height of water level)
should not be removed for ever. A low-relief sculpture of Liṅga as protected by a cow is just
below the inscription.
[Published in IPS, No. 975.]

l.ta: rum pattu perumparum pērum iṉi cētta-

inscription.head:
No. 232.
Ambukōvil, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the gōpura, right of entrance, in the Bhaktalalitēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Kshaya, Chittirai 25 = c. 14th-15th Century.

The temple trustees of Aḻimbil-nayiṉār and the ten perumpar (mentioned in the above inscrip-
tion) decided to contribute a sum of 45 poṉ annually to the temple for offerings and repairs. The
money is to be collected from the temple trustees, from the tolls and fairs and from the Sūdra
members among the temple trustees.

l.ta: lē aḻimpil kōyil cūttirat tāṉattār pērilē
patiṉañcu poṉṉum āka nāṟpattu añcu po-

inscription.head:
No. 233.
Ambukōvil, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the gōpura, left of entrance, in the Bhaktalalitēśvara temple.
inscription.p: . . ., Kālayutta, Tai 18 = c. 14th-15th Century.

Some provision for offerings in the early morning service was made by the palanquin bearers
and some others.

l.ta:
ḷiṭṭār taṉmamāka kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭu ciṟukālai canti[k*]ku kuṟuṇi aruciyum
kaṭṭaḷai yiṭṭapaṭiyā-

inscription.head:
No. 234.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Rājēndra I, 26th year = A.D. 1038.

Gift of land, freed from taxes, by the sabhā of Tiruvēṅgaivāyil, a dēvadāna in Peruvāyil-nāḍu
in Jayasiṅgakulakāla-vaḷanāḍu for offerings to the image of Nittachūḷāmaṇi-Viḍaṅgar. A local
Brāhmaṇa called Māḍalaṉ Nārāyaṇaṉ Mahādēvaṉ alias Dharmishṭhayōgi got the land tax-free
by paying the tax capital to the sabhā. The ūrār of Vallakkūṟṟuvar was present on the occasion
of the gift.

inscription.head:
No. 235.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, •••= A.D. 1227.

Piḷḷaiyār Aḻagapperumāḷ who was the holder of the nāḍu endowed some land for offerings to the
image of Chandraśēcharadēva set up by him when his superior officer of the (nāḍu-piḍit-
ta) Chēra-Pāṇḍyadēva made the revenue settlement of the locality (nāḍukaḷukku ichaiya aṟudi
paṇṇi).
[Published in IPS, No. 327.]

inscription.head:
No. 236.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Rājādhirāja I, 30th year = A.D. 1048.

Seems to make an inventory of all the dēvadāna lands belonging to the temple of Tiruvēṅgaivāy-
il. The lands were located in different villages like Mayilāppūr, Chuṉaiyūr, Tiruveṭpūr and others.
[Published in IPS, No. 108.]

inscription.head:
No. 237.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Vīrapāṇḍya, 23rd year, Karkaṭaka, sudi, Thursday =
A.D. 1276 (?)
Fragment. Seems to record a sale of land by the trustees of the Perumāḷ temple at Vēṅgaivāchal.
[Published in IPS, No. 599.]

inscription.head:
No. 238.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman, 28th year = A.D. 935.

Records gift of a lamp to the temple of Tirumēṟṟaḷi-Mahādēva at Tiruvēṅgaivāyil by an indi-
vidual of Kalaiyamaṅgalam in the kavirappāl of Valla-nāḍu.
[Published in SII, XIX, No. 432.]

• • vāyil tirumēṟṟaḷi mah[ā*]tē[va*]rkku vallanāṭṭu kavirappāṟ

inscription.head:
No. 239.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman, 5th year = c. 10th Century.

This verse inscription refers to a gift of lamp to the temple of Tiruvēṅgaivāyil-Śiva by a Vēḷ of
Maḻa-nāḍu.
[Published in SII, XIX, No. 136.]

l.ta:
parakEcari ceṅkOl paṟṟiya vāṇṭañcil tiruvEṅkaivāyil

inscription.head:
No. 240.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the door-posts of the entrance into the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the
Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja Rājakēsarivarman Rājarāja I, 26th year = A.D. 1011.

Gift of money for a lamp by a local merchant called Karuvūr Chūṟṟi, to the temple of
Tirumēṟṟaḷi-Mahādēva at Tiruvēṅgaivāyil, a dēvadāna in Peruvāyi-nāḍu, a sub-division of Pāṇ-
dikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu.
[Published in IPS, No. 88.]

inscription.head:
No. 241.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the Brhhannāyaki shrine in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Śrīvallabha, 34th year = c. 14th Century.

Badly mutilated. The trustees of the Tiruvēṅgaivāyil temple seem to have assigned some tem-
ple land to an individual with the condition that he should pay some specified quantity of paddy
annually after reclaiming it for cultivation.
[Published in IPS, No. 640.]

inscription.head:
No. 242.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the Brhhannāyaki shrine in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya, 13th year = c. 13th Century.

Much damaged. Seems to record a sale of temple land by the trustees of Tiruvēṅgai-vāchal
temple to a Pāṇḍiyadēvar who endowed the same in the temple for a perpetual lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 243.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the Brhhannāyaki shrine in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Śrīvallabha, 34th year = 14th Century.

The trustees of the temple of Tiruvēṅgavāchal comprising the dēvakaṉmi, accountant, the
śrīkāriyam, and the māṭāpattiyam fixed the dues to be paid on some dēvadāna land in posses-
sion of a maṟava individual of Maṭa-mayilāppūr in Peruvā-nāḍu.
[Published in IPS, No. 639.]

inscription.head:
No. 244.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the Brhhannāyaki shrine in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Kampaṇa-Uḍaiyar, Ānanda = A.D. 1374.

The trustees of Tiruvēṅgaivāyil temple and the ūravar assigned the right to collect the pāḍikāval
tax to somebody (name lost) who saved the village by repairing the village tank which had
breached laying the lands waste.
[Published in IPS, No. 681.]

l.ta:
ṉattārum ūravarum pāṭikkāval cuvantaram paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu
tiruvēṅkaivācal kuḷam

inscription.head:
No. 245.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the Bṛihannāyaki shrine in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, . . ., 7th year = c. 13th Century.

Built in partly. This seems to record that the nāṭṭār of peruvāyi-nāḍu reduced the rates of
taxes paid on the lands in the village which were lying waste due to some reason (details lost).

inscription.head:
No. 246.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the first prākāra of the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja II(?), 12th year = A.D. 1158.

The inscription is mutilated and seems to record a gift of land for offerings to the images
Chadira-viḍaṅgadēva and his consort set up in the Tiruvēṅgaivāyil temple by the chief Kulōt-
tuṅgachōḻa-Kiḍārattaraiyar.

ka kulakāla vaḷanāṭṭup peruvāyil nāṭṭut tiruvēṅkaivāyi lāṇ • • •

inscription.head:
No. 247.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the first prākāra of the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III, 20th year = A.D. 1236.

This records the decision of two individuals, perhaps brothers, to convert their kuḍippaṟṟu
lands, measuring 18 mā, into kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna and pay a half of the taxes (kaḍamai, an-
tarāyam, veṭṭi and muṭṭāvāḷ) from the land to the temple of Tiruvēṅgaivāyil-uḍaiya-Nāyaṉār
and use the other half for festivals in the shrines of Chadiraviḍaṅga-Nāyakar and Nāchchiyār
in the same temple.
[Published in IPS, No. 195.]

l.ta:
vaḷanāṭṭup peruvāyiṉāṭṭut tiruvēṅkaivāyil akavayaliṟ kuṭi vayaliṟ
kuṭippaṟṟāy iṟuttu varukiṟa nilam patiṉ

eṭṭu māvāl vanta kaṭamai antarāyam veṭṭi muṭṭāvāḷ maṟṟum eppēṟpaṭṭa
āyaṅkaḷum kaṭamai

uṭaiyār tiruvēṅkaivāyiluṭaiya nāyaṉāṟkuc cempātiyum
ittirukkōyiṟ catiraviṭaṅka nāyaka-

ṟkum nācciyāṟkum tirunāṭpaṭikkuc cempātiyuñ cantirātittavaṟ kuṭinīṅkāt

kiṭārattaraiyaṉ eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 248.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the first prākāra of the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja II, 7th year = A.D. 1153.
Fragment. Seems to record a gift of land by one Araiyan Chadiraṉ Irājan.

l.ta: ṭāṟku tiruvattacāmattukku nittam nānāḻi arici ci ••••••••••••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 249.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the first prākāra of the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 10th year = A.D. 1226.

The trustees of the temple of Tiruvēngaivāyil-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār in Peruvāyil-nāḍu assigned some
temple land as kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna to a dancer (śāndikkūttaṉ) Araiyaṉ Oṉṟāyiram and an-
other person with the stipulation that the latter should measure out to the temple a specified
quantity of paddy annually.
[Published in IPS, No. 275.]

l.ta:
kOmāṟapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ cOṇāṭu vaḻaṅkiyaruḷiya

inscription.head:
No. 250.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the first prākāra of the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja II, 8th year = A.D. 1154.

This states that the dēvadāna lands of the temples were lying waste for want of tenants and
that a Śivabrāhmaṇa came forward to purchase some piece of land for cultivating it under kuḍi-
nīṅgā-dēvadāna tenure and measure out some specified quantity of paddy to the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 251.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the kitchen in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya, 7th year = A.D. 1258.

This records that the trustees of the temple of Tiruvēṅgaivāyil sold some dēvadāna land to a
Mayilāppūr-uḍaiyāṉ Īsānadēvan to enjoy it under the kuḍinīṅgā-dēvadāna tenure.
[Published in IPS, No. 480.]

inscription.head:
No. 252.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the outer west wall of the first prākāra in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Vikramachōḻa, 14th year = A.D. 1132.
Fragment containing just king's name.

inscription.head:
No. 253.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the outer west wall of the first prākāra in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Vikramachōḻa, 14th year = A.D. 1132.

The nāṭṭār of Peruvayal-nāḍu assigned some temple land to a woman called Ēḻunāṭṭu-naṅgai
for performing the dance sāntikkūttu in front of the deity Tiruvēṅgaivāyal-Āṇḍār, during the
Chittirai festival.
[Published in IPS, No. 128.]

l.ta: kku viṭṭa nilamāvatu tēvartānam tippaiyā vayalu-
m kuḷamum vaḷaiyil cuṟṟum inta ēḻunā-
yāta āṇṭu āṭiṉa kūttukku koṟṟu viṭuvatā-

inscription.head:
No. 254.
Tiruvēngavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the outer west wall of the first prākāra in the Vyāghrapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājādhirāja II, 5th year = A.D. 1168.

Records an endowment of land by purchase by Chadiraṉ Irājaṉ alias Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-Kiḍārat-
taraiyaṉ of Peruvāy-nāḍu for performing sāntikkūttu six times during the Tiruvādirai festival in
the month of Vaigāsi in front of the deity Chadiraviḍaṅga-Nāyaṉār set up by him. The land was
assigned to a woman dancer Umaiyāḻvi alias Chadiraviḍaṅga-naṅgai.
[Published in IPS, No. 139.]

inscription.head:
No. 255.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Rājarāja Sundarapāṇḍya, 14th year, Vaigāsi 22 =
A.D. 1265.

This is a sale deed by the nagarattār of Arumoḻidēva-puram in Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu
alias Kāṉa-nāḍu for the land sold to an Ambalavaṉ Nāyagan who gifted it to the temple of
Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍaiya-Nāyaṉār, for lamp service. The nagarattār made the land free of all
taxes including kaḍamai, nāṭṭu-viniyōgam, ūr-viniyōgam, and paṇimakkaḷ-pēṟu. The sale price
is mentioned in terms of the coin vāḷāl-vaḻitiṟandāṉ-paṇam. Water rights to the sold land are
clearly specified.
[Published in IPS, No. 487.]

l.ta:
kara vaḷanāṭṭu arumoḻitēvapurattu nakarattukkuc camaiñca nakarattārōm
kāṇivilaipramāṇam paṇṇi-

kkuṭutta paricāvatu ivūr uṭaiyār tiruvaraṉkuḷamuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku vaitta
tirunantāviḷakkukkut tiruviḷakku-

pārkka nāṭṭiṉa tiruccūlakkallukku vaṭakkum

inscription.head:
No. 256.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Rājarāja Sundarapāṇḍya, 14th year, Āni = A.D. 1265.

Records that the trustees of Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār temple agreed to supply oil to
lamps on receiving a land endowment from the nagarattār of Arumoḻidēva-puram.
[Published in IPS, No. 488.]

āṉi mātam • • •
caṉamuṭaiyāṉ ṉāyaṉāṟku uṭaiyā[r*] tiru-

iraṇṭukku arumoḻitēvapurattu nakarat-

tārōm koṇṭu tirucūlattāppaṉam [paṇ]ṇiṉa ceṅka ••••••••••••muḻutum tām

inscription.head:
No. 257.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
c. 13th Century.
inscription.p:
Gift of a silver vessel (koḷgai) by a Pāvaiyūr-uḍaiyāṉ Sīrāmaṉ Paṭṭappiḷḷai from Kulaśēkha-
ra-paṭṭaṇam.
[Published in IPS, No. 1060.]

inscription.head:
No. 258.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 9th year = c. 13 Century.

This records a gift of land to the temple of Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, perhaps by the
same person mentioned in the above inscription (No. 257).
[Published in IPS, No. 605.]

l.ta: kāṇiyāka koṇṭa cōmaṉ kuḻi uḷḷa ū-
m puṉcai maraṅkaḷu kālukkum perunaṟki[ḷ*]ḷic cō-
ḻa nāṭāḻvār kuṭikkāṭu nāṉkellaikkuḷ paṭṭa ni-

inscription.head:
No. 259.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Kulaśēkharadēva, 2nd year = 12th Century.

Gift of taxes on some land to the temple of Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār by the ūrār of Pe-
ruṅgaraikkuḍi alias Tiruvaraṅguḷanallūr in Kāṉa-nāḍu. The land came to the possession of the
ūrār by purchase and mortgage from the maṟava araiyakaḷ of Peruṅguḍi.
[Published in IPS, No. 504.]

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī kulaicEkaratEvaṟku yāṇṭu iraṇṭāvatu
kāṉa ṉāṭṭup peruṅ-

karaik kuṭiyāṉa tiruvaraṉkuḷa nallūr ūrāyicainta ūrōm nāṅkaḷ
peruṅkuṭi maṟava-

raiyakaḷ pakkal vilaiyum oṟṟiyuṅkoṇṭuṭaiya peruṅkaraikkuṭi vayalu

peruṅ-

inscription.head:
No. 260.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 5th year = A.D. 1301.

The taxes due from the nagarattār of Vallanāḍaṉ-perunderu as a part of the nāḍu were gifted
to the Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār temple for burning a sandhyā lamp for the merit of Chē-
mappiḷḷaiyār by one of the latter's mudalis (officers).
[Published in IPS, No. 431.]

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvīrapāṇṭiya tEvaṟku yāṇṭu aiñcāvatu
uṭaiyār

tiruvaraṉkuḷamuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku nāyanār cēmappiḷḷaiyār tirumēṉikku
naṉṟāka caṉtiyātīpat ti-

ru viḷakkikku valla nāṭaṉ perunteruvil nakarattārai nāṭṭu [vi]
ḻukkāṭṭuppaṭi accu uṭaiyār tiruvarankuḷa-

muṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku nāyaṉār tiruvuḷamāy aruḷa tirumalaiyilē kalveṭṭik
kuṭuttōm nāyaṉār mutalikaḷil vaṉputē-

mican tirunaṭṭaperumāḷāṉa vīratēvaṉēṉ ivai eṉ eḻuttu nāyaṉār
kaṇkāṇik kaṇakkum aḻakiya cēmavēḷā-

inscription.head:
No. 261.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Śaka 1378, Dhātu = A.D. 1456-57.

It says that a native of Rājarājēndrachōḻa-puram in Kōnāḍu set up the image of Kaṇṇappa-Nay-
iṉār in the temple.
[Published in IPS, No. 797.]

l.ta: ṉ mēl cellā niṉṟa tātu varuṣa
uṭaiyār tiruvaraṉ kuḷamuṭaiya nāyaṉār tirukkōyilil kaṇṇappa nāyiṉā-
kōṉāṭṭu irācarācēntiracōḻa purattu cempāvuṭaiyāṉ aḻakiya tirucciṟṟa-

inscription.head:
No. 262.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
c. 13th-14th Century.
inscription.p: Gift of a silver pitcher for bathing the god, by a native of Sundarapāṇḍya-puram.
[Published in IPS, No. 1059.]

inscription.head:
No. 263.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
c. 13th-14th Century.
inscription.p: Records the digging of a well by a Tōṉṟippiḷḷai.
[Published in IPS, No. 1061.]

inscription.head:
No. 264.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 4th year = A.D. 1300.

This records a gift of land by purchase for offering puddings to the goddess in the temple of
Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, by Udaiyañcheydāṉ Bhuvanasiṅgadēvaṉ a mudali of Chē-
mappiḷḷai. The same person also gave two lamp-stands, a girdle, and a gold fillet. He made
provision for burning perpetual lamps from the tax dues collected from the two workshops of
blacksmiths (kollar ūdukiṟa ulai) in the village. Besides, he also arranged for lighting some
more lamps by assigning a sum of 6000 kāsu (equivalent to 5 achchu) being levied by the ara-
iyas of Valla-nāḍu from the blacksmiths (of the Valla-nāḍu) and a sum of 300 kāsu being paid
by the latter for the parisaṭṭam. Reference is also made to the sheep gifted to the temple by
Chēmappiḷḷaiyār and the sheep that were presented through pīḍavāri.
[Published in IPS, No. 427.]

l.ta:
ḷaiyār mu[ta*]likaḷil utaiyañceytāṉ puvaṉa ciṅkatēvaṉēṉ uṭaiyār
tiruvaraṉkuḷamuṭaiya

nāyaṉār [tampirāṭṭiyāṟku] piṭṭamutu ceytaruḷa kaḷiyaṉ naṅkaṉ uḷḷiṭṭār
pakkal ācāriyaṉ va-

yalil nīr nilattil •••tteru •••paṟṟil puṉceykaḷilum maraṅkaḷilum
nālattonṟu ivva-

edition.l:

inscription.head:
No. 265.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 9th year = A.D. 1305.

This records the sale of their land by two groups of brothers to the Tiruvaraṅguḷam temple. The
sellers undertook to pay themselves all the tax dues on the sold land and also take responsibility
for any future disputes on that land. It is said that the land comprised wet land, dry land, and
the fields with tamarind and mango trees.

l.ta:
vaṟku iṉāṭṭu • • kuṭi •••kuḷavaṉ koṟṟaṉum
tampimārum namaṉ ciṟai eṉavalla mutta-

raiyaṉun tampimārum ivvaṉaivōmum iṉāyaṉār tirumalaiyil kalveṭṭik

llai inṉāyaṉār tēvatāṉam teṉkavināṭṭu kuḷattu uḷ vayal nāṅkaḷ
taṭi pāṟtu viṭṭa nilattukkum nāyaṉārkku paḻantēva-

muttaraiyantal ellaikkum vaṭakkum mēlpāṟkku ellai

kuṟucci nāṭṭelaikku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟku ellai periyanattu vaṭavēlikku
mēṟkku mēlvēlikku kiḻakkum iṟai-

kuḷattukkaṭai cella teṟkku āka icainta perunānkellai uṭpaṭa nīr(nīr)
nilamum puṉcekaḷum kaliṅkataraiyaṉukku

tenkarai nāṭāḻvaṉ viṟṟa vilaippaṭi ivan puṉceyu vallapan piḷḷi

kuṭuttōm

araṉkuḻavaṉ koṟṟaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 266.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 10th year = A.D. 1306.

Incomplete. Seems to record a gift of land by Bhuvansiṅgadēvaṉ an agambaḍi-mudali of Chē-
mappiḷḷaiyār.
[Published in IPS, No. 433.]

l.ta:
vatiyaṉ puvaṉa ciṅkatēvaṉēṉ uṭaiyār tiruvaraṉkuḷamuṭaiya ṉāyaṉārkku
villavaṉ muttaraiyaṉ uḷḷuṭṭār viṟṟa cēmaṉ kuḻi

paṟṟil tiruccūla tēvarai eḻuntaruḷivitta ellaikkuḷ •••vikki[ra*]macōḻa
nāṭāḻvākkum puṉṟavataraiyar • • lukku nikaṉevil-

inscription.head:
No. 267.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 4th year = A.D. 1300.

This records that the naṭṭār of Teṉ-Kavi-nāḍu decided to give the village of Kuḷattūr with all
its tax dues, including kaḍamai and achchuvari for celebrating a festival and a service in the
name of the chief Arasagaṇḍarāmaṉ, a sāmanta, to be celebrated on the day of his natal star
Śatabhishaj in the month of Puraṭṭādi. Several vēḷāṉs are found among the signatories of the
document, obviously the members of the nāṭṭār body.
[Published in IPS, No. 428.]

ka kaṇṭa puraṭṭātit tirunāḷāṉa aracakaṇṭarāmaṉ tirunāḷukkum

inscription.head:
No. 268.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 4th year = A.D. 1300.

A kaikkōḷa, Kuraṅgan Uḍaiyān alias Rājagambhīra-mārāyan, living in the temple street sold
part of his land to the temple for 6000 kāsu to pay off the fine imposed on him (for some of-
fence) by Bhuvanasiṅgadēvaṉ a mudali of Nāyaṉār Ēmappiḷḷaiyār (or Chēmappiḷḷaiyār) and de-
posited the amount in the treasury of the latter. The amount was used by the chief for making a
sacred grove named after himself. Actually the temple also permitted the chief to use the above
land of the kaikkōḷa for making the grove.
[Published in IPS, No. 429.]

edition.l:
svastiśrī tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrīvīrapāṇṭiya tEvaṟkku yāṇṭu 4- vatu
irājarāja vaḷanāṭṭu vallai nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tiruvaraṉkuḷamuṭai-

l.ta:
tēvaṟkku innāyaṉār tirumaṭaivi[ḷāka]ttu
kaikkōḷaril kuraṅkaṉ uṭaiyāṉāṉa irāja

ṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu nāyaṉā ēmappiḷḷaiyār mutalikaḷil
puvaṉaciṅka tēvar eṉṉaik kuṟṟa teṇṭattukku niccayitta kācu eṉ kāṇi[yā]-

ṉa ācāriyaṉ vayalil eṉ kūṟu cempātiyum mēṟkkuḷattu uḷ •••nāṉ
vilaikoṇṭu uṭaiya nilamum inta ācāriyaṉ vayalukku mēṟkuḷḷa [puṉce]-

mmaramum nāyaṉār tēvatāṉam teṉkaviranāṭaṉ kīḻvayalil
tiruvuṇāḻikaikkōṉ pakkal nāṉvilai koṇṭu uṭaiya nilamum inta
ceyyil kiṇaṟum in-

ta ōlai mēlppaṭṭa nīr nilattukkum kiṇaṟum puṉceyyum kiṇ[ō*]kkiya
kiṇaṟum mēṉōkkiya
maramum vilaikkuṟa viṟṟu koḷvatāṉa emmilicainta vilaipporuḷ nalkācu

vilaikkuṟa viṟṟup poruḷaṟak koṇṭu vilaippiramāṇam paṇṇik
kuṭuttēṉ uṭaiyār tiruvaraṉkuḷamuṭaiya nāyaṉār āticaṇṭēc

mpīra mārāyaṉēṉ inta ōlai
mēṟppaṭṭa nīr nilaṅkaḷukkum pun ceykaḷum kīṇōkkiya kiṇaṟum mē-

ṉōkkiya maramum eppēṟpaṭṭatukkum innāyaṉār cīpaṇṭārattilē koṇṭu
emmillicainta vilaipporuḷukku vilaikkuṟa viṟṟa kācu āṟāyiramum
vilai-

kkuṟa viṟṟu poruḷaṟakkoṇṭu (vilaikkuṟa viṟṟup poruḷaṟakkoṇṭu)
viṟṟu vilaippiramāṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttēṉ āticaṇṭēcura tēvaṟkku irā-

jakempīramārāyaṉēṉ innilattukku cempilum kallilum ve[ṭ*]ṭikkoṇṭu
tiruccūlatēvaraiyum eḻuntaruḷiviccuk koḷvarkaḷāka piramāṇam pa-

ṇṇikkuṭuttēṉ irājakempīra mārāyaṉēṉ ippaṭikku ivai irācakempīra
mārāyaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku uṭaiyāṉ kuraṅkaṉ cayiñaiyum iṭṭu

ivarkaḷ colla ippiramāṇam eḻutiṉēṉ tiruvaraṉkuḷa vēḷāṉ eḻuttu
ippiramāṇattiṉ paṭiyilē nāyaṉār cēmapiḷḷaiyār paṇṭārattilē ikkā-

cu āṟāyiramum cīpaṇṭāramāka kaikkoṇṭu nāyaṉār cēmappiḷḷaiy[ā]
r ceyvikkiṟa aracakaṇṭarāmaṉ tiruttōppukku ittiruttōppu ceykiṟa
aṭṭa • •

perumāḷāṉa aracakaṇṭarāmap piccaṟ tōppup puṟamākap piramāṇappaṭi
tiriviṭṭuk kuṭuttōm āticaṇṭēcura tēvakaṉmikaḷōm ippaṭikku ivai
p[o]-

ṉmāḷikaip piḷḷaiyaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai tirumāḷikaippiḷḷai
eḻuttu ippaṭikku [kiḷi]ppayiṟṟu nāṭṭup paṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai
tirumāḷikaipiḷḷai [e]-

ḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai tirumāḷikaippiḷḷai cayiññaiyum iṭṭu irā ••••••
•••ṭaṉ[ca]yilē miṭṭu •••eḻutiṉeṉ tiruvarankuḷavēḷāṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku
ivai cun[tara]-

pperumāḷ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai nāṟpatteṇṇāyiravaṉ eḻuttu
ippaṭikku ivai tirunāvukkara[cu āṇṭār] eḻuttu ippaṭi tiruveḻucci
aḻakava[r eḻu]ttu ••••••

inscription.head:
No. 269.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
This brief inscription is a continuation of the above one (No. 268) and records the assignment
of a piece of land for the gardeners who look after coconut plantation in the new grove.

l.ta:
aracakaṇṭarāman tiruttōppu ceyumavarkaḷ añnūṟṟuvaṉukku vaṭakkum
aññūṟṟuvaṉukku mēṟkku-

m tirumaccaṇapperuvaḻikku mēṟkkum aracakaṇṭa rāmaṉ tōppu uṭpaṭat
tirunantavāṉamakkuvār taḷuvu

vaitta teṅkam piḷḷai nōkkuvāḷḷukku mēppaṭi nōkkumavakaḷukku kīḻil
kalveṭṭi paṭi nīr-

nilam puñceppuḷi okka koṇṭu arakaṇṭarāmaṉ tōppu ceyum avarkaḷ
aṉupōkippākaḷākak kalveṭṭikkuṭut-

inscription.head:
No. 270.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 4th year = A.D. 1300.
This is a duplicate of No. 264. [Published in IPS, No. 430.]

l.ta:
kampaṭi mutalikaḷil utaiyaṉ puvanaciṅkatēvanē uṭaiyār tiruvaraṉkuḷamuṭaiya

tampir[ā]ṭṭiyāṟku puva-

kiraṇṭiṉā liṭai aṟupattoṉpataṉ pa-

lam uṭaiyāṟkkuc cāttiyaruḷiṉa tirumēkalaipoṉ patineḻukaḻañcu
tiruppaṭṭa patinkaḻañcu nāyaṉār cēmap-

piḷḷaiyār civanāmattāl aracakaṇṭarāmaṉ tirinuntāviḷakkuñ cantiyā
tīpattukkum niccayittapaṭi tiri-

nuntāviḷakkiraṇṭukkum kollar ūtukiṟa ulaiyil iraṇṭu ulaikkuk
kācāṟukku kaṭaiyil nāḻiyā-

l uḻakku neykku iraṇṭu tirinuntāviḷakku iṭavum valla nāṭṭaraiyarkaḷ
kollar pēril ā-

ṭṭaikkup piṟikkum accu aiñcukkuk kācu accu aiñcukku kācu āṟāyiramun
tiruppaṅkuṉit tirunāḷukkut tiruppariyaṭṭattu

kkut tarakkaṭava kācu muṉṉūṟum ākakkācu āṟāyirattu muṉṉūṟukku
nāḷoṉṟukku viḻukkāṭu kācu

patinēḻaraikkuc ceyyu nāḻiyāl ney nāḻi uḻakkukku eḻupattañcu tiruviḷakku

inscription.head:
No. 271.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Kṛishṇarāya, Śaka 1445, Śubhānu, Uttarāyaṇa, badi 11, Friday,
Mūla = A.D. 1524, January 31, Sunday (not Friday).

It was decided by the nāṭṭār of Valla-naḍu that all the taxes on the Veḷḷāḷa and other tenants
(kuḍigaḷ) of Pālaikkuḍi, Kaḷaṅguḍi and Kiḷinallūr in Kiḷippayiṟṟūr-nāḍu a part of Valla-nāḍu
should be given to the temple of Araṅguḷanāthar at Tiruvaraṅguḷam in Valla-nāḍu from now
on seeking divine intervention to protect the nāḍu from the evil effects (dōsham) of some bad
planetary configuration (aṭṭatti in Kumba-rāsi). The villages had been a kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna
of the temple.
[Published in IPS, No. 737.]

inscription.head:
No. 272.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Kṛishṇarāya, Śaka 1442, Pramādi, Siṁha, sudi 10, Śiddhayōga,
Friday, Ārdra =
A.D, 1519, August 19, Friday (probably).

The nāṭṭavar of Valla-nāḍu had sold as tirunāmattukkāṇi of the temple of Tiruvaṅguḷam-uḍai-
ya-nāyaṉār all the land in three villages in Kiḷippayiṟṟūr-nāḍu, namely Pālaikkuḍi, Kaḷaṅguḍi
and Kiḷinallūr, for clearing the loan they had taken from the temple. Now they decided to pay
themselves all the government taxes on those villages by distributing the responsibility over the
entire nāḍu. The loan was taken earlier to pay some heavy taxes imposed on the nāḍu by Svāmi
Narasā-nāyaka in the year Siddhārti (which is mentioned in No. 313 below).
[Published in IPS, No. 733.]

inscription.head:
No. 273.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Tribhuvanavīradēva (Kulōttuṅga III), 40th year, Karkaṭaka, 3 =
A.D. 1218.

The nāṭṭār of Valla-nāḍu met in the temple of Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār and decided
unanimously that they should protect all the kuḍimakkaḷ in the temple (dēvadāna) villages,
should not harass them by forcefully taking their cattle, etc. All the communities there—Śiv-
abrāhmaṇas, Cheṭṭi, Veḷḷāḷa, kaikkōḷa, dēvaraḍiyār, kaṇmāḷa, herders—were permitted to enjoy
the land they had purchased from the nāṭṭār as kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna and pay the stipulated dues
in grain and money to the temple.
[Published in IPS, No. 176.]

inscription.head:
No. 274.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Kulaśēkhara, 11th year = 13th Century.

The trustees of the temple received an endowment of 34 paṇam from Aḻagaṉ Kaliyugameyyaṉ
alias Kalikaḍinda-Pāṇḍyadēva of Viḷattūr in Miḻalai-kūṟṟam for the maintenance of the garden-
ers engaged for growing cheṅgaḻunīr (red lily) flowers.
[Published in IPS, No. 567.]

inscription.head:
No. 275.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th Century.
Construction of the maṇḍapam by a Chērapāṇḍya-dēvar.

inscription.head:
No. 276.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the south verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, Dhātu = A.D. 1336(?)

Records a peace settlement entered between Iṉbaṉ Āriyachakravarti and Sūriyaṉ, and others
on one side and Aḍiyārkkunallāṉ Aṅgarāyaṉ and Kuppaṉ on the other. There was a long-stand-
ing feud between the two groups as Teṟkil-araiyaṉ killed Irāhuttaṉ, the younger brother of
Aṅgarāyaṉ. By the present settlement both the groups swore to each other to stand united in all
circumstances and help each other in adverse circumstances.
[Published in IPS, No. 619, lines 1-13.]

inscription.head:
No. 277.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the south verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: . . ., Pramādi, Piraṭṭāsi = A.D. 1339(?)

Seems to record that the araiyas of Pūvarasakuḻi in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu had been in feud as one
Araṅguḷattāṉ Īḻattaraiyaṉ had harmed another araiya called Ulagamāṇikkap-paraiyaṉ and that
it was now brought to an end by the former agreeing to give some land as compensation for the
sear he made to the latter. It was also agreed that they and their progeny should not think ill of
each other but remain friendly. If anybody violated this arrangement his land will be confiscated
to the temple as a kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna. The settlement was arrived at in the presence of sev-
eral araiya dignitaries of the locality.

l.ta: araiya makkaḷil mōkaṉ vāḻvittāṉāṉa ulaka māṇi-

inscription.head:
No. 278.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the south verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Vīrapāṇḍya, 17th year, Karkaṭaka 25, badi 7,
Sunday, Aśvini = c. 13th Century.
Badly damaged. Mentions the arasu of Aṟandāṅgi in Pāṇḍya-vaḷanāḍu and some land gift.

inscription.head:
No. 279.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the south verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Mallikārjunarāya, Śaka 1384, Chitrabhānu, Āvani 12 = A.D.
1462, August 11.

Vīrapāṇḍya-Vijayālayadēva, the arasu of Chūraikkuḍi in Niyamappaṟṟu of Kēraḷasiṅga-
vaḷanāḍu gifted some land as dēvadāna excluding the kārāṅkiḻamai right. The old dēvadāna,
paḷḷichchandam, and the tax-free land being enjoyed by the ‘patiṉeṇ kuḍimakkaḷ’ was exempted
from this gift.
[Published in IPS, No. 712, lines 1—6.]

inscription.head:
No. 280.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the north verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Kulaśēkhara, 24th year, Chittirai = c. 13th Century.

The temple trustees of the Tiruvaraṅguḷam temple assigned some dēvadāna land as a vaidya-vṛit-
ti and jīvita to a Ātrēyaṉ Tiruchchendār-mudaliyār Nimbavana Vayichchiyādarāyaṉ, a saravaṇan
(physician) of Tirumarugal.
[Published in IPS, No. 575.]

inscription.head:
No. 281.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the north verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Mallikārjunarāya, Śaka 1380 = A.D. 1458.

This records a gift of land for offerings by Tirumēṉi-Aḻagaṉ alias Vīra-Pāṇḍya Vijaiyālaiyadēva.
It seems that the nāḍu undertook to pay themselves the taxes on that land.

inscription.head:
No. 282.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the north verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Bhūpati-Uḍaiyar, Hēviḷambi, Tai 8 = A.D. 1418, January 3.

Much worn out. This registers a peace settlement between two groups of kaṇmāḷar who were
quarrelling with each other resulting in some murder.
[Published in IPS, No. 697, lines 1—6.]
I

inscription.head:
No. 283.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the north verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān (Sundara-pāṇḍya), •••= 14th Century.

The king granted some land as kārāṇmai-iṟaiyili for offerings at a special service called Sund-
ara-paṇḍyaṉ-sandhi in his name in the temple of Tiruvaraṅguḷam.

inscription.head:
No. 284.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the north verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Bhūpati-Uḍaiyar (son of Bukkaṇa) = c. A.D. 1418.

Daṉmarāyaṉ the arasu of Chēndavaṉ-maṅgalam made a gift of land as tirunāmattukkāṇi in
the village of Sāmanta-nallūr for offerings in the Tiruvaraṅguḷam temple. A second gift by the
māṭāpattiyam of the temple is mentioned at the end.

inscription.head:
No. 285.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the north verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Kampaṇa-Uḍaiyar, Kshaya, Vaigāsi = A.D. 1386.

Seems to record a sale of land, freed from taxes, at Ādavattūr by the nāṭṭār of the locality
(name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 286.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the north verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Dēvarāya-Mahārāya, Śaka 1373, Prajāpati, Tai 2 = A.D. 1451.
December 29.

This is badly worn out; records a gift of land as tirunāmattukkāṇi by Sīraṅganāthar alias Palla-
varāyar, the arasu of Peruṅgōḷiyūr in Teṉ-Panaṅgāḍu-nāḍu to the temple of Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍai-
ya-nāyaṉār. The land was in Teṉ-Kavi-nāḍu, whose nāṭṭār agreed to take upon themselves the
tax responsibility and agreed to permit water from the Kavināḍu tank as before.

inscription.head:
No. 287.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the north verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Kālayukta, Vaigāsi = c. 14th Century.

One Perumāṇḍār purchased some temple land and endowed it to the same temple as tirunāmat-
tukkāṇi for a service in his name.

l.ta:
perumāṇṭā[r] caṇṭēcurapperuvilaiyāka uṭaiyār tiruvaran[kuḷamuṭai]ya
nāyinār •••yil iṟpaṭikku kuḷa •••••••••

yār tiruvaraṉkuḷamuṭaiya nāyināṟku perumāṇ[ṭār] cantikku can[tirāti]
ttavaraikku tirunāmattukkāṇiyākak koṇṭu ka • • •

inscription.head:
No. 288.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the west verandah of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 39th year = A.D. 1335.

This refers to the decision to end the enmity that was existing between two groups of araiyas,
Ayilarāyaṉ and his brothers of a paḍaippaṟṟu village in Kavi-nāḍu on the one hand and Piḷḷāṉ
Aḻagiyachōḻa-nāḍāḻvāṉ and Aḍaikkalavaṉ of Pudukkōṭṭai on the other. The feuding groups
agreed mutually not to continue the enmity that had existed from the time of their fathers. If the
decision is not adhered to, the defaulting member should give some land to the temple and also
pay a heavy fine to the rulers (irāja-daṇḍam). They also decided to meet the expenses of the
meeting of the devotees (mayēśuras) who would gather to enquire the defaulters.
[Published in IPS, No. 618, lines 1—12.]

inscription.head:
No. 289.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the Sabhāpati-maṇḍapa in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 7th year, Kumbha, sudi, Wednesday, Rōhiṇi =
c. 13th Century.

This is an order of the king warning the Śvabrahmaṇas of the temple of Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍai-
ya-nāyaṉār not to take (misappropriate?) themselves certain specified taxes on the tirunāmat-
tukkāṇi lands of the temple and asking them to use it for the proper performance of temple
services.

l.ta: llar uḷḷiṭṭār pēril taṇṭum [ac]cuvari kaṭamaikaḷum uḷ ••••
uṭalāka iṟaiyiliyākat tantōm ippaṭi •••nāyaṉār kōyililē kal[lu]-
m veṭṭi ippaṭiyāl vanta mutalkaḷ tirukkōyil paṇṭārattaṇṭi muta-
paṭikku tirumantira ōlai eḻutum ciṟukuṭaiyāṉ aḻakiyāṉ e-

inscription.head:
No. 290.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the Sabhāpati-maṇḍapa in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 15th year = c. 13th Century.

The nāṭṭār of Valla-nāḍu agreed to pay themselves all the dues which the priests of the temple at
Tiruvaraṅguḷam had to pay towards the chūṟai-vari (war levy?) imposed on them by an officer
called Aḷiyakētaṉ in the 10th year of Sundarapāṇḍyadēva.
[Published in IPS, No. 373.]

inscription.head:
No. 291.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the Sabhāpati-maṇḍapa in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 14th year = A.D. 1310.

Built in the middle. Seems to record that Bhuvansiṅgadēvan, the agambaḍi-mudali of Chē-
mappiḷḷaiyār, presented an elephant vehicle (kaḷiṟṟuppaḍi) for the deity to ride upon.

inscription.head:
No. 292.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the Bhairava-maṇḍapa in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 35th year = A.D. 1331.

This much worn out inscription refers to a peace compact between some araiya members of
Pūvarasakuḻi and some others agreeing to be friendly towards one another. Some of the araiyas
were Īḻattaraiyaṉ, Ulagamāṇikkapparaiyaṉ, Māvalivāṇādarāyaṉ, and Iḍaiyaṉ Iruṅgōḷan.

irācarācavaḷanāṭṭu valla nāṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 293.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the Bhairava-maṇḍapa in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: . ., Kālayukta, Puraṭṭāsi, 18th day = A.D. 1318(?)

This records a peace settlement between the kin of Koppaṇaṉ Nārasiṅgadēvaṉ and those of
Tuṇḍarāyar, which was brought about by the efforts of Vāṇādarāyar of Teṟkāṭṭūr. The two groups
were fighting for long since the murder of Koppaṇaṉ Nārasiṅgadēvaṉ committed by the other
group, thereby involving much destruction of property and loss of lives.

inscription.head:
No. 294.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the Bhairava-maṇḍapa in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
C. 13th Century.
inscription.p: Records the construction of a tank by a native of Teṉ-Kavira-nāḍu.
[Published in IPS, No. 1065.]

inscription.head:
No. 295.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the Bhairava-maṇḍapa in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Achyutadēva-Mahārāya, Śaka 1461, Plavanka, Dakshināyana,
sudi 5, Rēvatī, Thursday = A.D. 1541.

The arasu Tirumalarāsa-pallavarāyar made a gift of land with all the tax revenue from that
for offerings. The land was irrigated by the tank of the nāḍu (nāṭṭu-kuḷam) in Valla-nāḍu in
Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu. The locality had been bestowed on the donor chief as umbiḷikkai-sīrmai by
Rāmappa-nāyakkar. The chief boasts of a long string of achievements and titles.
[Published in IPS, No. 752.]

inscription.head:
No. 296.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the old kitchen in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vibhava, Puraṭṭāsi 1 = 14th Century.

Records the assignment of the pāḍikāval dues by the ūrār of some three villages, namely Irum-
bāḻi, Marudāntanilai, and Vaḍa-Mayilāppūr, to the temple of Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍaiya-Nāyaṉār
in appreciation of the protection they got from the nāṭṭār of Valla-nāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu.
The three villages are said to belong a geographical unit called Irumbāḻi-añjunilai.
[Published in IPS, No. 898.]

tiyati irācarāca vaḷaṉāṭṭu valla nāṭṭu
uṭaiyār tiruvarankuḷamuṭaiya nāyiṉārkku irumpāḻi añcu nilaikku

irumpāḻi ūravarum marutānta nilai ūravarum vaṭamayilāppūr ūravarumōm
pāṭikāval cuvantaram paṇṇik koṭutta paricāvatu eṅkaḷukku

(ḷukku) iṉṉāḷ mutal cantirātitavaraikku vallanāṭṭu nāṭṭavarāl
orupollāppaṟa kāttu nōkki pōtakkaṭavatākavum nōkkippōtu-

miṭattu āṇ[ṭu] oṉṟukku nāṟpatiṅkala nellu nāyiṉār paṇṭārattā[ṟ]
kēṟa oru mutalākac celavaṟuttup pōtak kaṭavōmākavum ippaṭik-

ku cantirātitavarai paṟṟi aṉupavittup pōtakkaṭavatākavum ippaṭi cammatittu
cukantaram paṇṇikkuṭuttōm uṭaiyār tiruvara[ṅ]

ku[ḷa*]muṭaiya nāyiṉāṟku irumpāḻi ūravarum marutāntaṉilai ūravarum
vaṭamayilāppūravarum ippaṭikku ūravar coṟpaṭikku •••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 297.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the old kitchen in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 9th year = 13th-14th Century.

The nāṭṭār of Valla-nāḍu sold a silver vessel of the temple to get a sum of 11,000 kāsu for
clearing the tax arrears on a common land within the tank of the nāḍu. Instead they decided to
use in future the tax revenue of the above land for celebrating a festival called Valla-nāḍan-
tirunāḷ during the Tiruvādirai day in the month of Mārgaḻi. For that they made the land as tax-
free tirunāmattukkāṇi of the temple.
[Published in IPS, No. 607.]

inscription.head:
No. 298.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the old kitchen in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 10th year = A.D. 1306.

Udaiyaṉ Bhuvanasiṅgadēvaṉ an agambaḍi-mudali of Nāyaṉār Chēmappiḷḷaiyār fixed the
rates of vāram, perhaps on concessional rates, on dry crops raised in the temple lands held by
all classes of tenants—Śivabrāhmaṇar, kaikkōḷar, iḍaiyar, kollar—within the temple village.
[Published in IPS, No. 434.]

muṉ nāḻi eṇ-

eḻuttaṟivā[ṉ*] eḻuttu•

inscription.head:
No. 299.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north and west walls of the old kitchen in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara
temple.
inscription.p: •••, Śaka 1305, Rudhirōdgāri, Kārttigai 19 = A.D. 1383.

This registers the gift of a village as tirunāmattukkāṇi and kuḍi-nīṅgā-kārāṅkiḻamai by Kuḍumi-
yār Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa-perumāḷ Toṇḍaimāṉār, Sundarapāṇḍiya-toṇḍaimāṉār and Sūriyadēvar
Toṇḍaimāṉār, who were the arasu chiefs of Aṟantāṅgi in Iḷaṅgō-nāḍu a sub-division of Vaḍa-
Veḷḷāṟu alias Sundara-pāṇḍya-vaḷanāḍu, for conducting the service called Malvallāṉ-sandhi.
The donors also decided to give up in favour of the temple the dues collected towards their
arasu-sutandaram in the locality.

l.ta:
••••••sakāttam āyirattu mu[ṉṉū]ṟṟu añcil mēl cellāniṉṟa utirōṟkāri
varuṣam kārttikai mātam pattoṉpatāntiyati nāḷ irācarācavaḷa nāṭṭu
valla nāṭṭut tiruvaraṉ kuḷattu u •••

cuntarapāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu iḷaṅkō nāṭṭu aṟantāṅki aracu kuṭumiyār
aḻakiyamaṇavāḷapperumāḷ toṇṭaimāṉārum cūriya tēvar cuntarapāṇṭi •••

malvallāṉ cantikkut tirunāmattukkāṇi ākak kuṭinīṅkāk kārāṅ[ki]ḻamai

aṇṇāḻvi malvalla perumāḷ toṇṭaimāṉā ••••••••••••••••••••••

tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam ayyaṉ pātti piṭari viḷākam utirap-

uṭaiyār aḻakiya maṇavāḷapperumāḷ toṇṭaimāṉārum cūriya tēvar
cuntarapāṇṭiyat toṇṭaimāṉārum mēṉṉiṉṟa perumāḷ kulacēkarat
toṇṭaimāṉārum aruḷic ceyal pa •••••••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 300.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the old kitchen in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Vīrapāṇḍya, 29th year, Kārttigai 19 = A.D. 1325.

This is similar to the peace compact mentioned in No. 292 above between the araiya members
of Pūvarasakuḻi and others. Perhaps this may represent the earlier stage of the conflict and set-
tlement.
[Published in IPS, No. 595.]

l.ta:
ḷ irācarāca vaḷa nāṭṭu valla nāṭṭu
uṭaiyā-
r tiruvarakuḷamuṭaiya nāyanār tirukkōyil tāṉattārkkum ūrarkku-
m pūvaracakuḻi araiyamakkaḷil cūriyaṉ toṇṭaimāṉu makkaḷ marumakkaḷum cōḻi-
yaṉ ulaka māṇikkapparaiyaṉum makkaḷum pāṇit tēvarāṉa īḻattarai-
yar makaṉ vīramūvaṉ vicaiyar tēvaṉum vīramūvaṉ īḻattaraiyaṉum tampi[ye]-
kkaṉum kalveṭṭi kuṭutta paricāvatu aṭiyārkku nallāṉ tuṇṭarāyarai cu[n]-
taṉ nāraciṅka tēvan kuttukaiyil inta pakaiyai nōkki iṉpaṉ ulakamā-
īḻattaraiyaṉum cuntan nāraciṅka tēvaraiyum cōḻaṉ ulakamā-
ṭaimānārum ivar makkaḷum toṇṭaimānārum marumakkaḷāṉa cēra-
cōḻa kōṉum araṅkuttāṉum puttaṉum cōḻaṉ araṅkuḷava nāṭṭarai-
yaṉum uṭappāṇṭāṉum uḷḷiṭṭārum āka ivvanaivarum intapa-
kai eṉṟu collakkaṭavōmalla eṉṟu pakai oruṅka tiru-

inscription.head:
No. 301.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the old kitchen in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 9th year = A.D. 1305.

Records the building of the kitchen by Udayaṉ Bhuvanasiṅgadēva for the merit of his overlord
Chēmappiḷḷaiyār between the 17th day of Māsi of the 9th year and Āṉi of the 10th year, that is
within a period of five months.

l.ta:
yaṉār cēmappiḷḷaiyār tirumēṉikku naṉṟāka ittirumaṭaippaḷḷi cēvittār
iṉ
ṉāyaṉār akampaṭi mutalikaḷil utaiyaṉ puvaṉaciṅkatēvar taṉmam harahara

inscription.head:
No. 302.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the old kitchen in the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 10th year = A.D. 1306.

The agambaḍi-mudali Bhuvanasiṅgadēvaṉ fixed the rates to be collected towards the levy taḍi-
kuṟuṇi (literally one kuṟuṇi per field) in all the tirunāmattukkāṇi and dēvadāna villages of the
temple. The proceeds had to be utilized towards offerings and ten lamps for the merit of his
overlord Chēmappiḷḷaiyār.
[Published in IPS, No. 435.]

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉa cakka[ra*]vattikaḷ ciri vīrapāṇṭiya tēvaṟkku yāṇṭu pattāvatu
uṭaiyār tiru[va*]rankuḷamuṭaiya nāyanārkku nāyanār cēma-

ppiḷḷaiyar akampaṭi mutalikaḷḷilil utaiyan puvaṉa ciṅka tēvaṉnēn
innāyanār tēvatāna āna tirunāmattukkāṇi āna nīrnilaṅkaḷūm

puncekaḷum kuṭinīṅkā tēvatānamāṉa nīrnilaṅkaḷum puṉcekaḷum nāyanār
cīpaṇṭārattil vilaikoṇṭa cēman kuḻi paṟṟāṉa nīr ni-

laṅkaḷum puncekaḷḷum perunelkiḷicōḻa nāṭāḻvān kuṭikāṭum nāyanār
tiruvi[ḷa]mmāy aruḷiya aracakaṇṭarāman [ca]nti nāḷaikku kavira

nāṭṭu nāṭṭārai iṭuvittu nān kalveṭṭivittu kuṭutta kuḷattūrrāṉa
aḻakiya cēma nallūr nīr nilaṅkaḷḷum innāyanār tirunāmattu

kkāṇiyāna kūṭallūrum kumpaṭakku nallūrum ka • • va nāṭṭu ū • • tēvatāṉaṅkaḷum
ippaṭiyil vaṉta tēvatānaṅkaḷ in

nāyanār kōil cīkāriyam ceyvārkaḷ koḷḷum taṭi kuṟuṇi koḷum
miṭattu nīr nilaṅkaḷukku taṭi onṟukku aṟukkum naṟukkuṟuṇiyum aṭi

kkum naṟukuṟuṇiyum ninṟa nāḷḷil vantatu koḷḷa kaṭavatākavum
puñceyyirukkum eḷḷum koḷḷuttuvarai uḷḷiṭṭa payiṟṟu-

kku ninṟa nāḷtōṟum kuṟuṇi peṟa kaṭavatākavum puñceyil [tuvarai] varaku
pullu keṇṭi uḷḷiṭṭa payaṟṟukku ninṟa nāḷ onṟukku patak-

kāka va[nta]tu peṟa kaṭavatākavum ippaṭiyāl cīkāriyañ ceyvarkaḷ koḷḷa[vu]
m kaṅkāṇi va[rai] mutal koṇṭu nāyanā[r*] cē-

mappiḷḷaiyār tirumēnikku nanṟāka tirup[paḷḷi] eḻucci cannatikku amutupaṭi
nāḷonṟu(ṟu)kku

ceppu nāḻiyāl kuṟuṇi arucikkum viñcaṉattukku muppatu tiruviḷakkum
cantirā-

titavaṟ cella kaṭavat[ā*]ka kalveṭṭikkuṭuttēn puvanci[ṅ*]ka tēvannēn
ippaṭikku
puvanaciṅka tēvar kaṇakku ātiṉūr uṭaiyāṉ ulaku uyyakoṇṭān aḻakan

inscription.head:
No. 303.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a slab built into the south wall of the first prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
c. 13th Century.
inscription.p:
This brief incomplete inscription refers to the contribution of the wall to an extent of 2 cubits
by an individual.

inscription.head:
No. 304.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a slab built into the south wall of the second prākāra in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
c. 13th-14th Century.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to the construction of the south wall by the ‘Nine Nagarattār’ and a land
gift to a maṭha.
[Published in IPS, No. 1064.]

l.ta: rum vallanāṭu nāṭṭu maṉuṣarumōm oṉpatu nakarat-
tār taṉmam teṟku tirumatiḷ ivarkaḷ maṭattukku maṭa-

inscription.head:
No. 305.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a the south wall of the Gaṇapati shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 14th year = A.D. 1310.

Records the construction of the shrine by Udayaṉ Bhuvanasingaṉ for the health of Nāyaṉār
Chēmapiḷḷaiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 306.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a the south wall of the Gaṇapati shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Śrīvallabha, 28th year = c. 14th Century.

Records the consecration ceremony of the shrine of Mūtta-Piḷḷaiyār (i.e. Vighnēśvara) by a
Brāhmaṇa of Nāṭṭāṟṟuppōkku.
[Published in IPS, No. 643.]

inscription.head:
No. 307.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a the west wall of the Chaṇḍēśvara shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Vīrapāṇḍya, 17th year = 13th Century.

It says that Vaḻittuṇai-perumāḷ alias Vilāḍarāyar provided for torches to be lit during the fes-
tival days for the merit of his overlord (sāmantaṉār). He did the arrangement with the concur-
rence of the temple trustees, the ūrār, the nagarattār and other kuḍimakkaḷ of Tiruvaraṅguḷam.

l.ta: ku vācal pan[tammu ni]laipantam ēttiṉa [tām] vācalilē tiṅkaḷ
ḷeṇṇai vā[r*]ttu nālu tiruṉāḷum piṭikka kaṭavarāka ippaṭikku ka-
llilum cempilum veṭṭi kuṭuttōm uṭaiyār ti-
ṉār kōyil tāṉattārum ūrōmum nakarattōmum iv-
vūṟp pala kuṭimakkaḷum ippaṭikku poṉmāḷikaippiḷḷai eḻu-
ttu ippaṭikku tirumāḷikaippiḷḷai eḻuttu irārāpaṭṭaṉ eḻu-
[yaṉ] eḻuttu irācarācaṉ piramārāyaṉ eḻuttu cētirāyaṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 308.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a the north wall of the Chaṇḍēśvara shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya, 12th year, Mēsha, sudi 13, Monday,
Rōhiṇī = c. 13th Century.
Mutilated. Some priest called Aḍiyārkkunallār is referred to.

inscription.head:
No. 309.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a slab set up near the Gaṇapati shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
c. 13th Century.
inscription.p:
The slab containing the inscription bears the figure of a horse-rider with two attendants on foot.
The inscription seems to refer to the hero depicted on the slab as the loyal soldier (muḻu-chēva-
gaṉ) of Vijayarāyar Miṇḍar alias Kaṇḍiyadēvar of Irājēndiram.
[Published in IPS, No. 1062.]

inscription.head:
No. 310.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the second gōpura of the Haratīrthēśvara temple, right of entrance.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya, 9th year = 13th Century.

This records the building of the compound wall with stones and named after Gāṅgēyarāyar. The
nagaram of Vallanāḍaṉ-perunderu seems to have contributed something to the construction.
[Published in IPS, No. 468.]

inscription.head:
No. 311.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the second gōpura of the Haratīrthēśvara temple, right of entrance.
c. 15th-16th Century.
inscription.p: This is a Sanskrit verse in praise of the god Haratīrthēśvara.
[Published in IPS, No. 1068.]

inscription.head:
No. 312.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the second gōpura of the Haratīrthēśvara temple, right of entrance.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, •••s/o Mallikārjunarāya, s/o Dēvarāya, Śaka 1436, Bhāva, Ut-
tarāyaṇa, Makara, sudi 11, Friday = A.D. 1515, January 26, Friday.

Incomplete. Gift of land for offerings by Poṉṉambala Toṇḍaimāṉ, the arasu of Aṟantāṅgi at
the service called Alaivilañjādāṉ. instituted in his name in the temple of Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍai-
ya-tambirāṉār. The land measuring 12 mā and odd was lying in Kuṇḍarkuḍi in Pālaiyūr-nāḍu.
which was his sīrmai.
[Published in IPS, No. 728.]

l.ta: nāḷ irācairāca vaḷanāṭṭu vallanāṭṭu uṭaiyār tiruvaraṉkuḷam uṭai-
ṭu cuntarapāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu iḷaṅkōnāṭṭu aṟantāṅki aracu accam

nam pērālē alaivil añcātāṉ canti āṭiyaruḷiya vamutu ceyyavum
tiruppaṇi-

kkum āka viṭṭa nammuṭaiya cīrmaiyāṉa [pālai]yūr nāṭṭup pālai[yū]ṟ
kuḷattuppe-
riya maṭai veppa vāykkālālum ciṟunaṅkaikkuḷattu maṭaiyālum pala ūṟaṇi en-

[ta]liṉālum nīrpāyntu viḷaiyuṅ [ku]ṇṭarkuṭi vayalil muṉṉāḷ tēvatāṉam
tiruvi-
•••nilam ā[ṟṟu] nalla piḷḷaippeṟṟāḷ nilam nālumā itaṉ teṟkuṭ po-

edition.l: itaṉ vaṭakku cuḷakumukki taṭi 1- l nilam 1 mā itaṉ teṉmēṟku taṭi 1- l nilam • •

l.ta: perunāṉku ellaiyāvatu kīḻpāṟku ellai palāṟṟukku mēṟkum ten • • •

inscription.head:
No. 313.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the second gōpura of the Haratīrthēśvara temple, left of entrance.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, •••s/o Mallikārjunarāya, Śaka 1421, Siddhārti, Vṛiśchika, sudi
11, Saturday, Rēvati = A.D. 1498, November 24, Saturday (Cyclic year should
be Kālayukta the previous year).
[Published in IPS, No. 729.]

This records the sale of three villages, Pālaikkuḍi, Kaḷaṅguḍi, and Kiḷinallūr in Kiḷippay-
iṟṟūr-nāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu to the temple of Tiruvaraṅguḷam by the ūravar of those villages
along with the araiyas of Valla-nāḍu who had the pāḍikāval rights in them and those who were
leaseholders of land in the same village. The sale was made to pay off the heavy kaḍamai dues
demanded on these villages by Svāmi Narasā-Nāyakkar. As the villagers could not pay the tax
they had to sell the villages. The araiyars of Valla-nāḍu who are the mortgagees of some land in
those villages were also a party to this transaction. [See No. 272 which is related to this record].

l.ta: mēṟcellā niṉṟa cittā[rtti va]ruṣattu viṟucciya nāya[ṟṟu]
nāḷ irāca irāca vaḷa[nāṭṭu val]la nā[ṭṭu uṭaiyār]
āṉavarkaḷum ivvūr nilaṅkaḷil koṇṭum oṟṟi piṭittu[m]
uṭaiōm āṉa valla nāṭṭu araiyarkaḷum ōm kalveṭṭi
inta mūṉṟu ūṟkum piṟivu vanta kaṭamaip paṇṭārattukku eṅ-
kaḷukku iṟai āṟṟātapaṭiyālē iṟaiyē vilaiyāka iṉṉāya-
mēlnōkkiya maramum nattamum nattappāḻum kuḷamum vayalu
pēṟpaṭṭaṉavum muṉṉāḷ uḷḷūr nayiṉār tēvatāṉa kuṭi-
aṉupavittum pōta inta mūṉṟu ūrum iṉṉāyiṉār

inscription.head:
No. 314.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the second gōpura of the Haratīrthēśvara temple, left of entrance.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya, Śaka 1447, Pārthiva, Dakshiṇāyana, Tulā,
sudi 5, Friday, Mṛigaśīsha = A.D. 1525, October 6, Friday.

A major part in the middle is worn out. This seems to record a gift of land by the nāṭṭavar
of Valla-nāḍu to the temple for the health of some nāyakkar (most probably Vīranarasiṅ-
ga-rāya-nāyakkar).
[Published in IPS, No. 738.]

l.ta: pāttiva camvaṟcarattu tekṣaṇāyanattu tulā nāyaṟṟu pū
ṣattu pañcamiyum cukkiṟavāramum peṟṟa miṟukacīrattuṉāḷ irāca

edition.l: poṉ [2]1 ippoṉ irupattu[ oṉṟu svāmi vīranara-]

inscription.head:
No. 315.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the second gōpura of the Haratīrthēśvara temple, left of entrance.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara = c. 13th Century.
Gift of four golden fillets (paṭṭam) weighing ten kaḻañju.

inscription.head:
No. 316.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the second gōpura of the Haratīrthēśvara temple, left of entrance.
inscription.p: c. 13th Century.
Records that a native of Peruñjuṉaiyūr constructed the gōpura and named it after Gāṅgēyarāyar.
[Published in IPS, No. 1066.]

inscription.head:
No. 317.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the Bṛihadamba shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Achyutadēva-Mahārāya, Śaka 1453, Nandana, Dakshiṇāyana,
Dhanus, sudi 11, Friday, Rēvati = Probably A.D. 1532, November 8, Friday.

Gift of tax-free land by the nāṭṭavar of Valla-nāḍu for the rituals and worship in the maṭha of
Tiruppuṉaivāsa-mudaliyār situated in the bazaar called Valla-nāḍaṉ-perunderu in the village
Araṅgulanādar-tiruppaḍaivīḍu. The nāṭṭavar undertook the responsibility of paying all the tax-
es on the gift land.
[Published in IPS, No. 746.]

pṛthvi •••yam paṇṇi

inscription.head:
No. 318.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north and west walls of the Bṛihadamba shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Achyutadēva-Mahārāya, Śaka 1452, Vikṛiti, Arpisi 1 = A.D. 1530,
October 1.

The nāṭṭavar of Valla-nāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu assigned some dēvadāna lands as kāṇiyāṭ-
chi to Chokkaṉār Pallavarāyar of Viraiyāchchilai, a paḍaipaṟṟu village in Kāṉa-nāḍu alias
Virudarāja-bhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu. The nāṭṭavar confirmed the kāṇiyāṭchi right in the land to the
latter's son too, with the stipulation that he should measure out the agreed share (vāram) of the
produce to the temple of Araṅguḷanāthar.
[Published in IPS, No. 745.]

l.ta:
ṉ makāmaṇṭalīcuraṉ ariaravipāṭaṉ pāṣaikku tappa
mūvarāyirakaṇṭan śrī accanta tEvamakārāyar tiruvirācci-

nāṭṭu kāṉaṉāṭṭu paṭaippaṟṟu viraiyāccilai cokkaṉār pallavarāyaṟku
irāca irācavaḷanāṭṭu valla nāṭṭu nāṭāka amainta nāṭṭa-

āṇmai āka kuṭuttapaṭiyālē inta ṉilattukku kīḻnōkkiya kiṇaṟum mēl

eḻuttu ||—

inscription.head:
No. 319.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the Bṛihadamba shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: [Vijayanagara]. Śaka 1439, Īśvara, Māsi 3 = A.D. 1517.

Moṇṇaiyappaṉ Vīranarasiṅgarāya-nāyakka-viḻupparaiyaṉ of Tiruvārūr remitted in favour of
the temple of Tiruvarankuḷanāthar the tax amount of 20 poṉ that was being paid by Sōmaku-
la-vīravarada on the temple land as part of the entire tax of the nāḍu. The remission was made
for celebrating the car festival of the temple for the merit of his overlord (svāmi) Vīra-Naras-
ingarāya-Nāyakkar.
[Published in IPS, No. 842.]

l.ta: yilum ka[ḻittu] tampirāṉār araṅkuḷa nātaṟku tirunāḷum tirut-
tērum na[ṭa]ttuñ celavukku poṉ irupatum kuṭuttu

inscription.head:
No. 320.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the Bṛihadamba shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 29th year = A.D. 1207.

Construction of the goddess' shrine by Kaṇṇuḍaiya-Perumāḷ alias Pirāṭṭi-Āḻvār the wife of
Koḍuṅguṉṟamuḍaiyār alias Nishadharājar and daughter of Kōnāṇḍār alias Nishadharāya of
Poṉṉamarāpati in Puṟamalai-nāḍu, a unit of Rājēndrachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Rājarāja-Pāṇḍināḍu.
[Published in IPS, No. 174.]

inscription.head:
No. 321.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the Bṛihadamba shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Śaka 1452, Vikṛiti = A.D. 1530.
Fragment. Seems to record some gift to the goddess by a Poṉṉa-muttaraiya.

inscription.head:
No. 322.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the Bṛihadamba shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 15th year = A.D. 1231.

Āḍavallānambi alias Magadarāyaṉ who had worked as the agent of Chērapāṇḍiyadēvar the
administrator of the locality and succeeded him as the nāḍu settlement officer (nāḍu kēṭṭaruḷi)
made some new arrangement for offerings to the goddess in the Tiruvaraṅguḷam-uḍai-
ya-nāyaṉar temple.

l.ta: tēvaruk[kuppiṉām] inta nāṭu kēṭṭaruḷi inta •••ccīkāriyap pē-

inscription.head:
No. 323.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the Bṛihadamba shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya, 9th year, Vṛiśchika, 22, Wednesday,
Svāti = A.D. 1260 (?).

Araiyaṉ Tiruvaiyāvūr-udāiyāṉ alias Tāyilunallāṉ of Maram in Kiḻār-kūṟṟam a unit of
Nittavinōda-vaḷanāḍu purchased land from the temple of Tiruvarankuḷam and gifted the same
as tirumaḍaippaḷḷi-puṟam of the goddess.

l.ta: putaṉ kiḻamai peṟṟa cōti nāḷ irāca irācavaḷanāṭṭu vallanāṭṭu

edition.l: tēvarkaṉmikaḷum śrī[ māhēśvarakaṇ] kāṇi cēvārkaḷum kōyil ka[ṇakku]

marattukki[ḻavaṉ]

ṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu uṭaiyār tiruvaraṉkuḷamuṭaiyaṉāyaṉār
tirukkā-
makkōṭṭa nācciy[āṟku]t tirumaṭaippaḷḷipuṟam ivar taṉmamāka koṇṭu vi-
