•••••••••• paṇi paṇṭārattil oṭukki i vayalum kuḷamum

inscription.head:
No. 324.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the verandah of the Bṛihadamba shrine in the Haratīrthēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Śaka 1466, Pramādīcha, Āni 3. [Irregular] = c. 16th Century.

The trustees of the temple and the nāḍu decided to honour the Nine nagarattār with a golden
fillet on the day of the swinging festival in appreciation of the latter's renovation work of the
tiruvūñjal-maṇḍapam.
[Published in IPS, No. 855.]

edition.l: • • ṉa cakāttam 1466- ṉ mēl cellā[ninṟa] piṟamātīca varuṣam

l.ta:
irācairāca vaḷanāṭṭu valla nāṭum tāṉamum āka oṉpatu
nakarattāṟku
ni[r]ṇayam paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi tiruvūñcal maṇṭapam nakarattār tirup-
paṇi ceykaiyil ivarkaḷukku tiruvātirai tiruvūñcal tirunāḷ [nāyakar]

inscription.head:
No. 325.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the first gōpura of the Haratīrthēśvara temple, left of entrance.
inscription.p:
Śaka 1789, K i 4968, Prabhava, Mithuna (Āni) 2, Utta-Bhādrapadā, Monday =
Probably A.D. 1867, June 24.

This bilingual inscription in Sanskrit and Telugu written in Telugu script records that King
Rāmachandra performed the kumbhābhishēka ceremony of the god Haratīrthēśvara and of the
goddess Bṛihadāmbā.

l.tetr: ca hita valaiḥ | prabhavaśaradi midhunērka bhā-
dra nakshatraṃ yī śubha dinamaṃdu mahā-

inscription.head:
No. 326.
Tiruvaranguḷam, Alangudi Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a pillar in the Iḍaiya-maṇḍapa in the village.
inscription.p: c. 12th-13th Century.
Records that a vayirāgi set up the pillar.

inscription.head:
No. 327.
Vīraikkuḍi, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south base of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruvēḍanātha temple.
inscription.p: Sādhāraṇa, Vaigāsi 10 = c. 16th Century.

Gift of land to the deity Tiruvōḍaippandal-uḍaiya-tambirāṉār at Vīraikkuḍi by Immuḍi Akka-
larāsar.
[Published in IPS, No. 951.]

l.ta:
immuṭi akkalarācar vīraikkuṭi
uṭaiyavar tiruppālaippantal uṭaiyār tampirāṉāṟku vīraikkuṭi nañcai
nilattil kaṭampavayal oḻukiṉpaṭi nilam uḷḷatu ulakuṭaiyāṉ vāykkālukku
teṟku cariyā-

kkuṭi ellaikku vaṭakku tiruppaiyūr ellaikku kiḻakku nañcai puñcai uḷpaṭa
tēvamāṉiyam āka cantirā

ttavaraiyum aṉupavikka koḷḷakkaṭavār ākavu
inta tammattukku iyātām oruttar akutam coṉṉavaṉ keṅkaikkaraiyilē
kārāmpacuvaiyum pirāma[ṇa] ••••

inscription.head:
No. 328.
Vīraikkuḍi, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa, left of entrance, in front of the central shrine in the Tiru-
vēḍanātha temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara, 7th year = c. 13th-14th Century.

Incomplete. This seems to record some agreement deed (piḍipāḍu) given to the temple of
Arumoḻi-Īśvaram-uḍaiya-Nāyanār at Chuṉaiyakkuḍi in Vaḍa-vāyil-nāḍu, a unit of Jayasinga-
kulakāla-vaḷanāḍu by the araiyakaḷ of Chērakkuṟichchi in Kuḷamaṅgala-nāḍu relating to some
land gift.
[Published in IPS, No. 537.]

l.ta: vāyil nāṭṭu cuṉaiykkuṭi uṭaiyār arumoḻi īcuramuṭaiya nā-
rakkuṟicci araiyakaḷḷōm piṭipāṭu kuṭutta paricāvatu iṉṉāyanār tēvatā-
• • naṭuvupaṭṭa nilamum kiṇaṟum ūr māvīnta kaṭamai

inscription.head:
No. 329.
Vīraikkuḍi, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruvēḍanātha temple.
inscription.p: Pramōda, Āvaṇi 30, Friday = c. 16th Century.
Gift of the pillar by a Viṉaitīttāpiḷḷai.

inscription.head:
No. 330.
Vīraikkuḍi, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a pillar in the nandi-maṇḍapa in the Tiruvēḍanātha temple.
inscription.p: Bahudhānya = c. 16th Century.
Records that a Mutteñmappiḷḷai of Vīrakkuḍi built the Nandi-maṇḍapam.

inscription.head:
No. 331.
Vīraikkuḍi, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the Oppilānāyaki shrine in the Tiruvēḍanātha temple.
inscription.p: c. 16th Century.

The ūravar of Vīraikkuḍi in Vaḍachiṟuvāyil-nāḍu gave some dēvadāna land to a mason as his
tax-free kāṇiyāṭchi for building the shrine and for making the image of the goddess.
[Published in IPS, No. 900.]

l.ta: kaittaccaril perumāḷācāri makaṉ cīraṅkācārikku kāṇi āḻci-

paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi nācciyārai tirumēṉikkoṇṭu nācciyār kōyilum
kaṭṭi muki-

ttapaṭiyālē vīraikkuṭi vayalil tēvatāṉamāṉa yiluppaiccey iṟaiyili
āka viṭ-

ṭōm cīraṅka ācārikku vīraikkuṭi ūrōm yinta yiluppaiccey iṟaiyilikku
piṉṉal

pētakam paṇṇi[ṉa*]vaṉ keṅkaikkaraiyil kārāmpacuvaik koṉṟa
pāvattilē pōvāṉ kōyil ti-

ruppaṇi ellām niṟaivēṟṟikkuṭukka kaṭavār ākavum mēṟpaṭi ūrkaṇakku
kāṅkaiyar piṟavikku nallār eḻuttu•

inscription.head:
No. 332.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the entrance, right side, to the first prākāra of the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 13th year, Mēsha, sudi 14, day 7, Sat-
urday, Uttara-Phālguni = A.D. 1229, April 7, Saturday.

Gift of money (1000 kāsu) by a temple girl called Aḻakiyanāchchi Piḷḷāṇḍār alias Tiruveṇāḻi-
gai-māṇikkam of the temple of Tiruvagattīśvaram-uḍaiya-Nāyaṉār in Vaḍa-Kōṉāḍu in Uṟat-
tūr-kūṟṟam in Iraṭṭapāḍikoṇḍa-chōḻa-vaḷanāḍu for the tirukāppunāṇ wearing ceremony of the
deities.

l.ta:
[tiyu] cattamiyum canikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttirattu nāḷ iraṭṭapāṭikoṇṭa
cō[ḷa] vaḷa-

ṉāṭṭu uṟatūr kūṟṟattu [va]ṭakō nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirupperumāṉāṇṭa
nāyanār cittirai

curamuṭaiya nāyaṉār kōyil tēvaraṭiyāril aḻakiyanācci piḷḷāṇṭārāṉa
tiruveṇāḻikai

māṇikka kūttan ittanmam iṉakku vēṇumenṟu ceyakaṭavapaṭikku
āticaṇṭēcuratēvar kan-

mikaḷ kaiyil nīrvāttu tirukkāppu nāṇ cātti eḻuntaruḷa vēṇṭumeṉṟu
cantirātittavaṟ cellak ka-

ikkācu āyiramum cī
paṇṭārattē oṭukku koṇṭu i-
tirukāppu nāṇukku pon eṭṭu māvum tirumantirakoṭiyum

ka pākku veṟṟilaiyum tirukkāppu nāṇ cāttavum parappavum puṇṇiyāka
arici uḷḷiṭṭa ari-

cik kuṟuṇi nānāḻi āka aṭikkīḻparappum nel kuṟuṇiyum amutupaṭi arici
tūṇi nāḻiyum kaṟiya-

mutu uḷḷiṭṭa viñcaṉaṅkaḷum cāttiyaruḷat tirumēl pūccum ceṅkaḻinīr
tiruppaḷittāmamum i[p*]paṭiyāl vantaṉa

cīpaṇṭārattē iṭṭu ittanmam ivaṟkku cantirātittavaṟ cellakkaṭavatāka
ippaṭi

cammatittu ikkācu āiramum cīpaṇṭārattu oṭukku koṇṭu
kalveṭṭikkuṭuttōm aḻakiya-

nācci piḷḷāṇṭāḷāṉa tiruveṇāḻikai māṇikkattukku
āticaṇṭēcuvaratē[va]r kanmikaḷōm §

inscription.head:
No. 333.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the entrance, right side, to the first prākāra of the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga II, 14th year = A.D. 1147.

Gift of a sum of 160 kāsu by a Pāṟkuḍaiyāṉ for the celebration of festival on new-moon days
in the Tirupperumāṉāṇḍār temple in Vaḍa-kōṉāḍu in Uṟattūr-kūṟṟam in Iraṭṭapāḍikoṇḍa-chōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu. The temple trustees and the ūrār received the gift.

inscription.head:
No. 334.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the entrance, right side, to the first prākāra of the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman [mistake for Rājakēsarivarman] Kulōttuṅga II, 20th
year, Kanyā, sudi 11, Thursday, Śravaṇa = A.D. 1152, September 11.

Gift of 100 sheep for a lamp by Araiyan Tannaṉ Edirilāppēraraiyaṉ alias Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-kaḍam-
barāyaṉ of Chār in Uṟattūr-kūṟṟam.

inscription.head:
No. 335.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the entrance, right side, to the first prākāra of the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga I, 13th year, Kanyā, 7th day, Monday, Uttara-Bhādrapada =
Probably A.D. 1084, September 2, Monday.

Lines 1-3 in verse in praise of Paiyūr, the patron's native place. Marudattūr-uḍaiyāṉ Kuṉṟaṉ Tiruch-
chiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ of Marudattūr in Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam gifted
some land to the temple of Tirupperumāṉāṇḍār. The land was received by the donor as a present
for composing verses in praise of a Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-udaiyāṉ Vēdavaṉamuḍaiyāṉ, a uḍai-
yāṉ of Paiyyūr in Uṟattūr-kūṟṟam in Vaḍa-kōnāḍu. End portion built in.
[Published in IPS, No. 129.]

l.ta: lēy nāṉ nīrvārtta kuṭikkāṭu iraṭṭapāṭi koṇṭa
yān vētavaṉamuṭaiyāṉai nān kavipāṭi pāṭiṉa kavikku eṉak-
ku paricil tanta tankāṇiyāṉa kuṭikkāṭu irumpūtikku vaṭapāṟkellai

inscription.head:
No. 336.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the entrance, left side, to the first prākāra of the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājādhirāja II, 12th year = A.D. 1175.

Incomplete. Gift of land for offerings to the goddess Periya-Nāchchiyār of the temple, by a
merchant of Chārappaṭṭaṇam alias Vikaramachōḻap-perunderu in Uṟattūr-kūṟṟam.
[Published in IPS, No. 140.]

l.ta: yārkku tēvaṟ nitta niyantam irunāḻi arici

inscription.head:
No. 337.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the entrance, left side, to the first prākāra of the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Rājādhirāja II, 2nd year, Makara, 7th day, Monday,
Svāti = A.D. 1165.

Edirilāpperumāḷ alias Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-Kaḍambarāyaṉ, an araiyan of Chār in Uṟattūr-kūṟṟam
in Iraṭṭapāḍikoṇḍa-chōḻa-vaḷanāḍu gave a village called Neḍuṅgīraikkuḍi in the same kūṟṟam
as kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna of the temple of Tirupperumāṉāṇḍār.
[Published in IPS, No. 138.]

l.ta: malaril nān nīrvārtta kuṭikkāṭu iṉ nāṭṭik kū-

inscription.head:
No. 338.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the first prākāra of the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th Century.

Records the construction of the parapet (eḍuttukkaṭṭi) by Vīrasiṅgapaṉmaṉ, an agambaḍi of
Uḍaiyār Kāṅgaiyarāyar.
[Published in IPS, No. 1107.]

inscription.head:
No. 339.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west wall of the first prākāra of the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p:
Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman Tribhuvanavīradēva (Kulōttuṅga III), 35th year =
A.D. 1213.

Gift of the village Puṉṉaṅguḍi to the temple of Tirupperumāṉāṇḍa-Nāyaṉār for worship and re-
pairs, by an araiyan of Kōṉāḍu called Ūruḍaipperumāḷ alias Edirilichōḻa-Kaḍambarāyaṉ pray-
ing for the safety of the king during his visit (perhaps some military expedition) to Madurai.
The village was subsequently renamed Udaiyapperumāḷ-nallūr after the king.
[Published in IPS, No. 169.]

l.ta: ṣēkamum paṇṇi aruḷiṉa tiripuvaṉavīra tēvaṟku yāṇṭu
koṉāṭāṉa kaṭalaṭaiyātilaṅkai koṇṭa cōḻa vaḷanāṭṭu iṉṉā-
parāyaṉēṉ ulakuṭaiya nāyinār maturaikku eḻuntaruḷiṉāl tirumēṉikku na-
ṉṟāka uṭaiyār tirupperumāṉāṇṭa nāyaṉārṟku pūcaikkum tiruppa-

ṇikkum uṭalāka nāṉ pirātticcu viṭakkaṭavatāna nilattukku piḷḷaiyār
tirukka-
ymalaril nīrvārttu kuṭutta puṉṉaṅkuṭiyum puṉṉaṅkuṭi uḷḷi-
ṭṭa kuṭikkāṭukaḷum tirukkaimalarilē nīrvāttuk kuṭuttu cōḻakōṉār pē-
lu nāṭṭikkuṭutta puṉṉaṅkuṭik kicaiñca kīḻpāṟkellai ā-
vatu tiripuvaṉavīran peruvaḻikkum māṅkuṭi vayalukku mēṟkum

inscription.head:
No. 340.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the four sides of a pillar of the west verandah of the first prākāra of the Maḍattukkōyil
temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājādhirāja II, 13th year = A.D. 1176.
Records the building of the Nṛitta-maṇḍapa by one Mālaipōttināṉ.
[Published in IPS, No. 105.]

inscription.head:
No. 341.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the north verandah of the first prākāra of the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman, 13th year = c. 10th Century.

Fragment. Seems to be some gift to the temple of Tirumāṉ-Mahādēvar by Vīrachōḻaṉ Ut-
tamaśīlaṉ.
[Published in IPS, No. 56 and SII. Vol XIX, 324.]

inscription.head:
No. 342.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the wall of the north verandah of the first prākāra of the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 17th year, Mithuna, sudi, Friday, 10,
Svāti = A.D. 1267, July 1, Friday.

The nāṭṭār of Vaḍa-Kōṉāḍu gave some land at Pāttēri as tirunāmattukkāṇi of the temple of Tir-
upperumāṉāṇḍa-Nāyaṉār for festivals and repairs in the temple for the well-being of the king
and the country.
[Published in IPS, No. 358.]

l.ta:
ṇṭalamuṅ
koṇṭaruḷiya śrīcuntarapāṇṭiya tEvaṟku yāṇṭu patinEḻāvatu

yu peṟṟa cOtiṉāḷ
uṟattūṟ kūṟṟattu [vaṭa]kOṉāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirupperumāṉāṇṭaṉāyaṉā-

māṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu
perumāḷ tirumēnikkum nāṭṭukkum naṉṟākat tiru[vātirai] tiruṉāḷ
eḻuntaruḷavum tiruppaṇikkum iṉnā-

yaṉāṟku iṟaiyili tiruṉāmattukkāṇiyākak kuṭutta iṉṉāṭṭup
pāttēriyāṉa periya ṉāṭṭu nallūr kīḻaivayaluṅ kuḷamum mēlai va •••kuḷamum

cōḷi
muṭaiya nāyaṉār tēva[tāna]mu nīkka nīkki uḷḷa nilamum puñce-

inscription.head:
No. 343.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the niches of the central shrine in the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p: c. 15th Century.

Records the names of the Ṛishis Jamadagni, Parāśara, Agastya and Viśvāmitra who worshipped
the god in this temple.
[Published in IPS, No. 1108.]

inscription.head:
No. 344.
Nāṅgupaṭṭi (Maḍattukkōyil), Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the west base of the Amman shrine in the Maḍattukkōyil temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Rāyaṇṇa-Uḍaiyar, Vikāri, Āni, 25 = A.D. 1419, June 21.

Registers a peace compact between the ūrār of Pērāmbūr and the ūrār of Kīḻaikkurichchi by
which they agreed to cease their long-standing feud, become friends hereafter, and act in unison
against their common enemies. This was made in the presence of the nāṭṭavar of Teṉmalai
and the ūrār of Puduvayal. An earlier compact engraved in the temple of Araṅguḷavar at Tiru-
varaṅguḷam is referred to, saying that the present one is a continuation of that.
[Published in IPS, No. 698.]

l.ta:
makāmaṇṭalīcuraṉ emmaṇṭalamum koṇṭaruḷiya śrī vīra
irāyaṇṇa uṭaiyar tiruirācciyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa vikāri varuṣam
āṉi mātam

pErāmpūr [varan]taru[tEva]ṉ aḻakiyār āṉa naraciñka tEvar
uḷḷiṭṭārOm

kīḻaikkuṟicci ūravaṟkup pakai orukkam tīntu kalveṭṭik kuṭuttapaṭi muṉpē
eṅkaḷ pēra[ṉār] cēmanaraciñkat tēvar nāḷ mutal aṭaikkalaṅkāttār
naraciñkatēvar nāḷ aḷavāka uṟṟu nilai aṟṟa viṉōtap pakaiyāy aṅkum
iñkum nālpatu nūṟu-

maṉittarum paṭṭu ciṟaimāṭum piṭittu āṟāp pakaiyāka kiṭakkaiyil
aṭaikkalaṅkāttār naraciñkattēvar nāḷilē ivarum mēṟpaṭi cēmarum
mēṟpaṭi kumu[ta]ya nāyanārum nāṅkaḷum eṅkaḷil porunti kīḻaikkuṟicci

ūravarai aḻaittu viṭṭu uṅkaḷukkum eṅkaḷukkum oru uṟṟu nilai aṟṟa
pakaikku viparītamāy irukka vēṇṭuvatillai eṉṟum aṅkuppaṭṭatu
aṟutiyāka mēlpaṭṭu pērampūr kīḻaikkuṟicciyum kīḻaikuṟicci pērāmpūrumāka
oṉṟupaṭa

naṭattip pōtakkaṭavōm eṉṟu nāṅkaḷ araṉkuḷavar caṉṉatiyilē aṟuti
paṇṇikkuṭuttu anta aṟutipaṭiyilē iṟṟai varaiyum naṭatti pōtukaiyil
iṟṟai nāḷ teṉmalai nāṭṭārum •••ṉūr putuvayal ūravarum mutucoṟkuṭi
nāṭṭārum

muṉpāka kalveṭṭik kuṭukkaiyil iṉṉāḷ mutal cantiraātittavaraiyum
pērampūr aracu veṟutta iṭam kīḻaikuṟicci veṟuttum kīḻai kuṟicci veṟutta
iṭam pērampūr aracu veṟuttum iraṇṭu niṉaiyāmal naṭattap pōtak
kaṭavōmākavum

iraṇṭu niṉaittōmākil eṅkaḷ vaḻipaṭu teyvamāṉa nāyaṉār
tirupperumāṉāṇṭār cīpātam tappiṉōm āvatākavum ippaṭikku naraciñka
tēvar eḻuttu ippaṭi aṟivōm teṉ malaināṭṭavarōm ippaṭi aṟivōm
kīraṉūr putuvayal ūravarōm

immariyāti oḻintu kīḻai kuṟicci ūravar iraṇṭu niṉaittōm ākil
nāyaṉār uṭaiya nāyaṉār cīpātam tappiṉōm āvatākavum ippaṭikku ūṟku
camaintu ivvūr kaṇakku uṭaiyāṉ oṭuvāy nāṭṭu vēḷāṉ eḻuttu•

inscription.head:
No. 345.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur, Pudukkottai District.
On the rock south of the rock-cut central shrine in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya, 12th year = c. 14th Century.

A Pāṇḍiyarāyaṉ seems to have sought protection (āsariyam) from the god of Tirukkuṉṟāk-
kuḍi-uḍaiya-nāyiṉār and (the nāḍu of) Iraṇḍumalai-nāḍu, for which he gifted his dues (rēkai)
from arasu-sutandiram of some locality to the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 346.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On a pillar of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 8th Century.

Kōdai Mayindaṉ gifted 220 nāḻi of rice for feeding 110 Brāhmaṇas on the day of Tiruvādirai
festival of the god Kuṉṟākkuḍittēvar. This he did for the merit of Mayindaṇ Vīrakāḍayaṉ.
[Published in IPS, No. 8.]

inscription.head:
No. 347.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south end of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pallava, Nandivarman II, 3rd year = A.D. 734.

Gift of 200 nāḻi of rice for feeding 100 persons on the day of Tiruvādirai, by Gaṇavatimāṉ alias
Pagaichchandira-Vichai-araiyaṉ, a native of Vaḍuvūr in Mīpuḻai-nāḍu.
[Published in IPS, No. 15.]

inscription.head:
No. 348.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north end of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pallava, Dantipōttaraiyar, 5th year = A.D. 801.

Records the digging of the tank Vāli-ēri by Vāli-Vaḍugaṉ alias Kalimūrkka-Iḷavaraiyaṉ, a ser-
vant (aḍiyāṉ) of Māṟappiḍuviṉār alias Pēradi-araiyar.
[Published in IPS, No. 17.]

inscription.head:
No. 349.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Mallikārjna-Virūpāksharāya, Śaka 1415, Ānanda, Vṛiśchika, sudi,
. . ., Monday = A.D. 1494, November.

This records the sale of a village to the temple of Kuṉṟākkuḍi-uḍaiya-Nāyiṉār in Vaḍa-
Paṉaṅgāḍu-nāḍu a part of Kāṭṭu-nāḍu alias Jayasiṅgakulakāla-vaḷanāḍu. The sellers were
Tirumalaiyirāsa-āriyachchakkiṟavatti and others, who seemed to have got the village as part of
their amara-nāyakattaṉam from the present king. The end part is completely worn out.
[Published in IPS, No. 716.]

l.ta:
cciṉa yirāyar viruppākṣayirāyar piṟitivirācciyam paṇṇiyaruḷā niṉṟa
cakāttam

mēlcellā niṉṟa āṉanta caṅvarcarattu viṟuccika nāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu • • •

cōmavārattu nāḷ kāṭṭu nāṭu āṉa ceyaciṅka kulakālavaḷa nāṭṭu
vaṭapaṉaṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tiru
kkuṉ ṟākkuṭi uṭaiyanayiṉāṟkku mīyceṅkiḷi nāṭṭu mullaivā[yiluṭaiya] ••••••

tirumalaiyirāca āriyaccakkiṟavatti uḷḷiṭṭāru maṇivāṇar āṉa
āriyacakkiṟavaṟtti uḷḷiṭṭārum

inscription.head:
No. 350.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: [Pāṇḍya], Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān [Sundara-pāṇḍya], 13+1st = c. 13th Century.

The king donated a sum of 100 poṉ for offerings at the service called Sundarapāṇḍyan-sandi
in his name.
[Published in IPS, No. 523.]

l.ta: yār tirukkuṉṟākkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉār kōyil tāṉattāṟku
taṅkaḷ kōyil celitti mikiti āka iṟuttu pōtum
poṉ nūṟum patiṉmūṉṟāvatu etirāmāṇṭu paṅkuṉi mā-
tam mutal nāyaṉār tirukkuṉṟākkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku
nampērāl kaṭṭiṉa cuṉtara pāṇṭiyaṉ cantikku amu-
tupaṭi cāttuppaṭi uḷḷiṭṭa paṭi viyañcaṉattukkum uṭalā-
ka ippoṉ nūṟum iṟaiyili ākat tantōm ippaṭikku va
lilum cempilum veṭṭikkoṇṭu pūcai tāḻvaṟa naṭatta

inscription.head:
No. 351.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Piṅgaḷa = c. 16th Century.

This records a deed of asylum given by Kōṉērirāya-pallavarāyar and others of Peruṅgōḻiyūr to
the temple of Tirukkuṉṟākkuḍi-uḍaiya-nāyiṉār temple and the nāḍu of Iraṇḍumalai-nāḍu seek-
ing protection for their lives and property.

l.ta: kal veṭṭiṉa namakku uṇṭu āṉa viṉaippāṭṭu-

inscription.head:
No. 352.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Parākrama-pāṇḍya, 9th year, Āvaṇi, Kanyā, badi, Satur-
day, 11, Punarvasu = A.D. 1323, August 27, Saturday.

This records a sale of land to Vyāpakaśiva, a disciple of the Naḍuvil-maḍam at Tiruvāṉaikkā
and of the Lakshādhyāya lineage, by Aṅgarāyaṉ a uḍaiyāṉ of Perumbuliyūr, in Teṉmalai-nāḍu a
sub-division of Vaḍa-Paṉaṅgaḍu-nāḍu in Jayasingākulakāla-vaḷanāḍu. The sale price was 100
varāhaṉ paṇam, that was then in currency. The annual kaḍamai on the land was fixed at 100
paṇam and the āyam at 15 paṇam.

inscription.head:
No. 353.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Viruppaṇṇa-Uḍaiyar, Āngirasa, Āni = A.D. 1392.

Later part not available. This seems to refer to some loan taken from the temple by Poṟkōy-
il-mārāyaṉ with the promise to return it in double the amount.
[Published in IPS, No. 688.]

l.ta: āṉi mātam nāyaṉār poṉ etiṟaṭaikku poṟ-

inscription.head:
No. 354.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Viruppaṇṇa-Uḍaiyar, s/o Ariyaṇa, Vibhava, Āni = A.D. 1388.
States that a temple girl repaired the northern wall of the temple which had fallen down.
[Published in IPS, No. 686.]

l.ta: śrī ariyaṇa uṭaiyār kumārar viruppaṇa uṭaiyāṟkkuc
kuṉṟākkuṭi uṭaiyanār kōil vaṭakkil tirumatiḷ carit-

inscription.head:
No. 355.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān Kulaśēkhara, 2+1st year = c. 12th-13th Century.

The king remitted all taxes on the village of Nāṅgūr in Puṟakkiḷiyūr-nāḍu in favour of the
temple of Tirukkuṉṟākkuḍi-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār for the expenses of a service instituted by Vaḷattu-
vāḻvitta-Perumāḷ alias Toṇḍaimāṉār. A detailed enumeration of the taxes is given. It is said the
inscription was engraved by the sculptor Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-āchāriyar as per the dictation of the
temple accountant from the copy of the royal order.
[Published in IPS, No. 559.]

inscription.head:
No. 356.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya, 18th year = c. 13th Century.

An Ādittadēvar Mullaikkuḍaiyāṉ seems to have gifted the pāḍikāval right on his land to the
temple of Tirukkunṟākkuḍi-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār in Vaḍa-panaṅgāḍu-nāḍu in Jeyasiṅgakulakā-
la-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 357.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th-14th Century.
Records the building of the Nṛitta-maṇḍapa and another maṇḍapa by a Aḻagiyachōḻa-nāḍāḻvāṉ.
[Published in IPS, No. 1097.]

inscription.head:
No. 358.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the rock-cut cave in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Parākrama-pāṇḍya, 9th year, badi 6, Wednesday, Rōhiṇi =
A.D. 1323.

This records the sale of land to Vyāpakaśiva referred to in 352 above, by a Paṉaṅgāṭṭu-nāḍāḻvāṉ
a uḍaiyāṉ of Perumpuliyūr in Teṉmalai-nāḍu of Vaḍa-Paṉaṅgāḍu-nāḍu a unit of Jayasiṅgaku-
lakāla-vaḷanāḍu.
[Published in IPS, No. 446.]

l.ta: n kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa rōcaṇi nāḷ ceyaciṅkakulakā-
la vaḷanāṭṭu vaṭapaṉaṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu teṉmalai nāṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 359.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the cross wall of the east prākāra in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya, 12th year = c. 13th-14th Century.

This is an undertaking given to the trustees of the Kuṉṟāṇḍār temple by the araiyars of Iraṇḍum-
alai-nāḍu that during the times of enmity and fighting among themselves they will in no way
harass and injure the tenants and other inhabitants in the village and also the travellers on the
roads, which they have been guarding. If they do not keep this promise, they were bound to pay
a fine of hundred paṇam individually and 500 paṇam for the whole village.
[Published in IPS, No. 484.]

l.ta: vatu kāttikai mātam jeyaciṅka kulakāla vaḷanāṭṭu vaṭapaṉa-

ṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirukkuṉṟākkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉār kōil
tāṉattu muta-
likaḷukku iṉṉāṭṭu iraṇṭu malai nāṭṭu araiyakaḷōm eṅkaḷil icaintu

eṅkaḷ kāval

ippaṭi ceyyum iṭattu veṭṭiyum kuttiyum cettu nōkka kaṭavarkaḷā[ka*]vum §

inscription.head:
No. 360.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the cross wall of the east prākāra in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān [Sundara-pāṇḍya], 13+1st year = c. 1266.

This is a king's communication addressed to the nāṭṭār of the temple villages (kōyil-paṟṟu) that
they should pay to the temple itself the sum of 100 poṉ they are paying towards the government
tax. The money was to be used for offerings during the service Sundara-pāṇḍyaṉ-sandhi named
after the king himself. The king may be the same as the one in No. 363 below].
[Published in IPS, No. 524.]

l.ta: ṭār kōyilppaṟṟu nāṭṭāṟku taṅkaḷ nāṭṭu uṭaiyār
[mi]kutiyāka iṟukkum poṉ nūṟum patiṉ mūṉṟāvatiṉ e-
ṟākkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku nam pērāl kaṭṭiṉa
nūṟum iṟaiyili āka co[ṉṉō]m ippaṭikku variyi-

inscription.head:
No. 361.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the cross wall of the east prākāra in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Vijayanagara, Dēvarāya-Mahārāya, Viśvāvasu, Māsi 10 = A.D. 1426,
February 4.

End portion mutilated. Seems to record gift of a vēli of land to the temple by Āvuḍaiya-nāyaṉār
Āṇḍapiḷḷai Villavarāyar, the arasu of Peruṅgōḷiyūr, in connection with the asylum (āśrayam) he
entered with the nāḍu of Iraṇḍumalai-nāḍu.
[Published in IPS, No. 701.]

inscription.head:
No. 362.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the cross wall of the east prākāra in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Dēvarāya-Mahārāya, Śaka 1356, Rākshasa = A.D. 1435.

This is, as in the above record, a deed of asylum (āsiṟaiyam) given by Vaḷandāṉ Vattataraiyar's
son Tambikku-nallār, the arasu of Peruṅgōḻiyūr to the temple and the nāḍu of Iraṇḍumalai-nāḍu.

edition.l: ṉṟa rākṣasa varuṣa āvaṇimācam 2- l vaṭapaṉai-

l.ta: ṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirukkuṉṟākkuṭi u-

inscription.head:
No. 363.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the cross wall of the east prākāra in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān [Rāarājan Sundarapāṇḍya], 12th year, Mithuna,
sudi, Friday, 1, Pushya = A.D. 1263.

The king ordered the trustees of the Tirukkuṉṟākkuḍi temple and the nāṭṭār of Tenmalai that the
temple need not pay a sum of 101 pon towards the usual government dues (iṟai) and instead use
it for offerings at the service Rājarājaṉ Sundara-Pāṇḍyaṉ-sandi, instituted in his name.
[Published in IPS, No. 353.]

l.ta: ciṅkakulakāla vaḷanāṭṭu vaṭapanaṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirukkuṉṟākkuṭi

inscription.head:
No. 364.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On three pillars of the vāhana-maṇḍapa in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 12th-13th Century.
Gives the names of the persons who donated the pillars (kāl).
[Published in IPS, Nos. 1102, 1106.]
I

inscription.head:
No. 365.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the second gōpura, right of entrance, in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Bokkaṇa-uḍaiyar, Subhānu, Āvaṇi 22 = A.D. 1403, August 20.

This records that Narasiṅgadēva, a chief of Pērāmbūr in Vaḍa-Kōṉāḍu gifted his kāṇi village
Kaṟṟalēri-piḍāgai with all its tax revenue to the temple of Tirukkuṉṟākkuḍi-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār in
view of his getting asylum from Piḷḷai Narasiṅgadēvar of Iraṇḍumalai-nāḍu. The latter would
also get the first honour in the temple.
[Published in IPS, No. 695.]

l.ta: kā[la] vaḷa nāṭṭu vaṭapaṉaiñ kāṭṭuṉāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirukku-
kkuṭpaṭṭa nattam nañcai puñcai kuḷaṟ pācippāṭṭamu-

inscription.head:
No. 366.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the second gōpura, right of entrance, in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Rāyaṇṇa-Uḍaiya, s/o Ariyaṇa-uḍaiyar, Śaka 13 . . , •••= c. 1412-19.
Fragment with the king's name only. [Published in IPS, No. 719.]

l.ta: ariyaṇa uṭaiyar kumāraṉ irācātirācaṉ irāca paramEcuraṉ

inscription.head:
No. 367.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the second gōpura, right of entrance, in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya (?), Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān, 5+2nd year = c. 13th-14th Century.

This is in two but related bits, with the middle portion missing. Seems to record gift of all the
taxes by the king on some village for the expenses of some service instituted in his name by the
nāṭṭār of some locality (name lost). A number of taxes is mentioned.
[Published in IPS, No. 659.]
I

l.ta: āṟuṅkālun turavum maramum uṭpaṭṭa maṟṟum ep-
kāriya ārāyci nelvari poṉ vari kācukaṭamai mā-
vaṭai puṉpayir taṟi iṟai cekkiṟai taṭṭoli taṭ-
ṭārp pāṭṭam ērvari iṉavari iṭaivari maṉaivari • • •

yiliyāka tantu ippaṭikku variyilār kaṇakkilum ka

inscription.head:
No. 368.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the second gōpura, left of entrance, in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Viruppaṇa-Uḍaiyar, Bhāva, Vaigasi 2 = A.D. 1394, February 25.

This records the decision of some groups (uḍaṉkūṭṭam) of the rathakāras assembled from a big
area including Rājagembīra-vaḷanāḍu, Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu and Nitta-
vinōda-vaḷanāḍu, to give some fees in money from each of their households in each year and
on the occasion of marriages to the temple of Tirukkuṉṟākkuḍi-nāyaṉār in view of their seeking
asylum and protection from the god of the temple bearing the title of ‘kaḷḷa-vēḷaikkāṟaṉ’.
[Published in IPS, No. 689.]

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 369.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the second gōpura, left of entrance, in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Bukkaṇa-uḍaiyar, Nala, Vaigāsi 6 = A.D. 1376, May 1.

Records that a Vikramachōḻa-Muttaraiyaṉ of Kīḻaippuduvayal made an assignment of a fifth
of his arasu-sugandaram estate to the temple and stipulated that after his death, in the absence
of any male issue, the remaining four-fifths also should go to the temple. He made this gift re-
ciprocating the help rendered by the nāṭṭār of Iraṇḍumalai-nāḍu in restoring his kāṇiyāṭchi and
arasu. [There is a fragmentary inscription following this, which seems to be related to No. 368
above].
I

l.ta: māḷāṉa iraṇṭumalai nāṭṭu vikkiṟama cōḻa mutta-
raiyaṉēṉ kallu veṭṭik kuṭutta paricāvatu eṉ kā-
m maṟṟum eṟpeyaṟpaṭṭa eṉ cukantarattilum nāyaṉā-
ṟku nāṉ kuṭutta añcilē oṉṟum nāyaṉār aṉupō-
añcu kūṟum cantirātittavarai nāyaṉār āḷak kaṭavarā-

edition.p: This is a fragment of a different inscription which seems to be to No. 368.

inscription.head:
No. 370.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the second gōpura, left of entrance, in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga III, 39th year = A.D. 1217.

It says that a Chōṟaṉ Aṅgarāyaṉ of Perumpuli-ūr gifted 4 mā of land to a pāraśivaṉ called
Mūṉṟukūṟṟu-vājya-mārāyaṉ.

l.ta: •••• ṭu nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirukkuṉṟākkuṭi nāyaṉār kōyilil
paṭṭa enmakan •••ḻa • • kikku ivarkaḷ kīḻ ceṅkiḷiṉāṭṭu araṅkuḻavaya •

utirappaṭṭi viṭṭa nilattil inṉāyiṉār kōyiliṟ pāracivaṉ • • •
pāṅkālakkuṭaiyān

mārāyaṉukku inta vā •••ttukku nimantam
ṉāḷ onṟu-

kku nel patiṉāḻikku upaiyamāka oṉpatiṟṟaṭi kōlāl iruṉūṟṟaimpattāṟu

viṭṭa nilam ṉālumā araiyum cantirātivaṟ celvatāka viṭṭēn
aṅkarāyannēṉ

inscription.head:
No. 371.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the second gōpura, left of entrance, in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th-14th Century.
Refers to the assignment of a washerman couple to the temple.
[Published in IPS, No. 1098.]

inscription.head:
No. 372.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the north side of second gōpura in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III(?), 2nd year = A.D. 1218.

The nagaram of Peruṅguḍi alias Virudarajabhaṅkara-puram, who were the tenants of the tem-
ple, agreed to pay a sum of 1,000 kāsu every year as fixed tax (vāḍā-kaḍamai) to the temple of
Kuṉṟapperumāḷ. The araiyakaḷ of Vaḍa-panaṅgāḍu-nāḍu were invoked to protect the arrange-
ment.
[Published in IPS, No. 184.]

l.ta: kkuṭimakkaḷāy āṭṭaikku vāṭāk kaṭamaiyāka āyiraṅkācu iṟu-

inscription.head:
No. 373.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south side of second gōpura in the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājarāja III (?), 3rd year = A.D. 1219.

This is a resolution by the nāṭṭār of Panaṅgāḍu-nāḍu that they would not harm anybody in the
fields or on the highways in Ambaṉavar-Nalvayalūr and if they did so they would incur a pen-
alty of one mā of land to the temple.
[Published in IPS, No. 186.]

inscription.head:
No. 374.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
inscription.p: In the hundred-pillared maṇḍapa of the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
c. 15th Century.
This gives the name Kuṉṟaṉ Kōppili Siṅgadēva, son of Peruṅgaḷūr Nāḍaḻvāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 375.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On sixteen pillars in the hundred-pillared maṇḍapa of the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th to 15th Century.

Mentions the persons who gifted the pillars. Among them are found merchants and araiyar
from different places like Perumpuliyūr, Tuvaraṅkōṭṭai, etc.
[Published in IPS, Nos. 1099, 1100, 1102-05.]
I

inscription.head:
No. 376.
Kunnāṇḍārkōyil, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the door-post of the unfinished gōpura of the Parvatagirīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pārthiva, Arpasi = c. 18th century (1705?).

This records the assignment of tolls (at the rate of 1/16 paṇam on every package) on all
the merchandise imported and exported (pōkkuvarattu-poti) in the sīmais of Tañjāvūr and
Tiruchchirāppaḷḷi, for midday offerings. The donors are mentioned as the palar, most probably
some big merchant group.
[Published in IPS, No. 911.]

inscription.head:
No. 377.
Kuḷattūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the ruined Siva temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 9th year = A.D. 1225.

Gift of land as kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna to the temple of Sundara-Chōḻīśvaram at Kūḻai-kuḷattūr in
Uṟattūr-kūṟṟam of Iraṭṭapāḍikoṇḍachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu by a temple girl called Ammai Āḻvi.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrīcOnāṭu koṇṭaruḷiya cuntirapāṇṭiya

kaḷḷapaṟṟilē villi nallaṉum villi vīraṉum pakkalē kuḷattūr vayalil
nīrnilam paḷ-

ḷakkuḻicceyyai vilaikoṇṭu nāyaṉāṟku kuṭi nīṅkātēvatāṉamāka

i-

eḷḷuk kuṟuṇi-

kkum varaku irutūṇiyum tiṉaikkut tūṇip patakkum ippaṭiyāl kaṭamai
nāyaṉāṟku aḷa

inscription.head:
No. 378.
Kuḷattūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the ruined Siva temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 17th year = A.D. 1233.

This records that three kaikkōḷas of the temple of Sundara-Chōḻīśvara at Kūḻai-kuḷattūr in
Uṟattūr-kūṟṟam of Iraṭṭapāḍikoṇḍachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu purchased some land in the village and gifted
it to the temple as kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna.
[Published in IPS, No. 274.]

l.ta: ya nāyaṉāṟku ikkōyiṟ kaikkōḷaṉ kaṇṇaṉ oḷiyaṉum
kaṇṇaṉ ariyāṉum kaṇṇaṉ aracaṉum immūvōm nāṅkaḷ
ivvūr vayalil vilai koṇṭa tāḻañceyyum iḷaiyar vayalil vilaiko-
ṇṭa kāñcirañ ceyyum itaṉ paṟikālum cilaiyōṭu ceyyum ātti-

tiruccūlakkallum nāṭṭik kalveṭṭik kuṭuttu iṉ nilaṅkaḷ nāṅkaḷē kuṭi nīṅ-
kāt tēvatāṉamāka uḻutu āṭṭaikku vāṭākkaṭamai cūlakkālāl pacāṉa-

tēvaraṭiyāḷ

inscription.head:
No. 379.
Kuḷattūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the ruined Siva temple.
inscription.p: •••, 33rd year = c. 13th Century.

Fragmentary. Somebody (king ?) ordered that the temple lands that were lying waste ow-
ing to breach in the tank be given on permanent tenancy (kuḍinīṅgā-dēvadāna) for reclamation
and cultivation.

l.ta:
vayalum kuḷamum neṭunāḷ paṭa pāḻāy kuḷamu maṭaittu vayalum varam piṟai •••
••••••
pāḻumāy parumarakkāṭumāy pāḻkiṭakkaiyil iṉṉāḷ mutal kuṭi nīṅ-
kā tēvatāṉamāka kuḷamum aṭaittu kāṭum veṭṭi varampum kaṭṭi payirc
nāṭṭuk kuṭinīṅkāt tēvatāṉap paṟṟukkum inta •••ṭṭil māttāl
tirumuṟṟap paṇṭārattē eṭuttu aḷakka kaṭavarkaḷākavum kācukaṭa-
maiyum aṉṉāṭṭi leṟṟa maricāti iṟukka kaṭavarkaḷākavum ivva ••••

inscription.head:
No. 380.
Kuḷattūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the ruined Siva temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 5+2nd year, Panguni 20 = A.D. 1223?.

The nāṭṭavar of Kuḷattūr in Kōṉāḍu together with the temple trustees resolved a dispute be-
tween the temple and a Vikkiramachōḻa-nāḍaḻvāṉ as to the ownership of a canal. It was agreed
that water might be baled out from the canal within some specified limits to irrigate the fields of
the latter and that one half of the income on fishing (pāsippāṭṭam) in the canal should be shared
equally between the two contending parties.
[Published in IPS, No. 512.]

l.ta:
tiyati kOṉāṭṭuk kuḷattūr
nāṭṭavarum ivvūruṭaiyar cuntaracOḷīccuramuṭaiya nāyaṉār kOyil
tāṉattārum kārai ūr araiyakaḷil maṅkāmaik kāttāṉāṉa vikkirama cOḻa
nāṭāḻvā-

rkku icaivu piṭipāṭu iṭṭa paricāvatu kōpuṟattukku mēṟkum calliya

nāṭāḻvārkku [oṉ]ṟu pātiyum āka canta ••••

inscription.head:
No. 381.
Kuḷattūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the ruined Siva temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Koyil-Kulaśēkharadēva, 2nd year = c. 13th-14th Century.

Miḻichchār alias Piḷḷai Pallavarāyar purchased land from the ūrār of Kūḻaikuḷattūr in Kīḷ-
Koṉāḍu and gave it as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the temple of Sundirachōḻīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār
in the same village for conducting a service called Pallavan-sandi in his name.
[Published in IPS, No. 555.]

l.ta: kūḻaikuḷattūr [ū]rāka icainta ūravarōm ivvūr nāyiṉār cu-
ṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu iṟṟaināḷ peruṅkaḷūr miḻiccārāṉa
kku oru cantināṉāḻi ney •••vēṇum meṉṟu eṅkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 382.
Kuḷattūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the east wall of the ruined Siva temple.
inscription.p: c. 13th Century.

This is a strange inscription recording the order of somebody (not specified) that no inscriptions
should be engraved in the central shrine and in the agra-maṇḍapa. It is warned that any
transgressor would be considered as a traitor to the king, to god Śiva and to the community.

inscription.head:
No. 383.
Kuḷattūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the ruined Siva temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Tribhuvanavīradēva (Kulōttuṅga III), 30th year = A.D. 1208.

This records the routine fixed by the body consisting of Vallavaraiyar, the trustees of the temple.
of Sundarachōḻīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyiṉār and the ūravar for the temple girls to perform their dai-
ly duties of singing and the carrying of the tiru-ālatti according to their seniority (tiruchchūlam
iṭṭa muṟaimai). Some six names of the incumbents are given. It is to be noted that their names
end all in the suffix component māṇikkam.
[Published in IPS, No. 162.]

l.ta:
latti eṭukkavum •••tirupāṭṭaṭaivum tiruccūlam iṭṭa muṟaimaiyē
vallavaraiyarum

ṇikkamum tiruvampalam piriyātāṉ catturukāla māṇikkamum iraṇṭāmuṟai

inscription.head:
No. 384.
Kuḷattūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the ruined Siva temple.
inscription.p: Vijayanagara, Bokkaṇa-uḍaiyar, Svabhānu, Panguni = A.D. 1402, February 27.

End portion damaged. Seems to record the grant of some privileges (pēṟṟu suvandiṟam) given
to the local goldsmiths, by the nāṭṭavar of Kīḻai-Kuḷattūr and the ūravar of Kīḻaippuduvayal
along with Vikkiramachōḻa-Muttaraiyar.

edition.l: ṣam paṅkuṉi mātam 3- tiṉ kīḻ kōṉāṭṭu kīḻaikkuḷattūr nāṭṭavarum kīḻai-

inscription.head:
No. 385.
Kuḷattūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the ruined Siva temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Kulaśēkhara, 39th year = 13th-14th Century.

Gift of land, after purchase, for a lamp in the temple by Aḻagiya-maṇavāḷapperumāḷ praying
for the health of his father Udaiyār alias Veḷḷōṭṭu-kaṇḍa-kāṅgēyaṉ, a kaikkōḷa-mudali of Tirup-
paṉaṅgaḍu in Chōḻa-maṇḍalam.
[Published in IPS, No. 551.]

l.ta:
ttu oru nāḷ cōḻamaṇṭalattu tiruppaṉaṅkāṭṭuk kaikkō[ḷa] mutalikaḷil
uṭaiyārāṉa veḷḷōṭ-

inscription.head:
No. 386.
Kuḷattūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the ruined Siva temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Koyil-Kulaśēkharadēva, 20+1st year = c. 14th Century.

The ūrār of Kuḷattūr reconfirmed the tax-free status of some land they had given earlier to a
Tiruvaṉ Pallavarāya-pallavaraiyar.
[Published in IPS, No. 558, lines 1—10 and 20—30].

l.ta: vayal tiruvaṉ pallavarāyap pallavaraiyaṟku nāṅkaḷ mu-

kak kaṭavatu ākavum itukku aḻivucoṉṉār uṇṭāki-

inscription.head:
No. 387.
Kuḷattūr, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.
On the south wall of the ruined Siva temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Tribhuvanavīradēva (Kulōttuṅga III), 32nd year = A.D. 1210.
Fragment. Stops with the king's name.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉa cakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum karuvūrum pāṇṭiyaṉ
muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇ-

inscription.head: No. 388.
inscription.p:
(I)
Sittannavāsal, Kulattur Taluk, Pudukkottai District.

Engraved round the top and left sides of one of the seventeen beds in the cavern on the hill
one kilometre east of the village.
In Tamiḻ-Brāhmī characters of about the first century BC.

This inscription records the making of the cave residence (atiTanam) by the iḷayar or warriors
of Chiṟupochil village for the (Jaina ascetic) KAvuTi Īteṉ who was a native of KumuḻUr in
Erumi-nāTu. AtiTaṉam is the Prākrit/Tamil form of the Sanskrit AdhishThāna. Several scholars
have read and published the text, each differing a little from the other. Since the palaeography
and orthography of the script are in the initial stages of adopting it for the Tamil language, there
are different versions of reading and interpretation of this inscription. Taking into consideration
the majority of readings the following text is found to be the most plausible one. For all the citations see Iravatham Mahadevan, Early Tamil Epigraphy: From the Earliest Times to the Sixth
Century A.D., Chennai: Cre-A & Harvard University, USA, 2003, p. 385.

p.ta: erumi nāṭu kumuḻūr piṟanta kāvuṭi īteṉku ciṟupocil iḷayar ceyta atiṭaṉam.

inscription.p: Transliteration and translation.

erumi nāṭu kumuḻ-ūr piṟanta kāvuṭi īteṉku chiṟupocil iḷayar ceyta atiṭ-aṉam Iravatham Mahadevan differing from others, splits ‘kāvuTi Iteṉku’ as ‘kāvuTi-I teṉku’ and translates the inscrip-
tion as follows:
‘KāvuTi (a Jaina nun), native of Kumuḻūr in Erumi-nāṭu. (For her) the Iḷayar of South (teṉku) Chiṟupochil made
the abode.’
He takes Kāvuṭi as the female form of Gāvuḍa found in Kannada inscriptions. His splitting of the name is
doubtful and the subsequent interpretation regarding Kāvuṭi is based on later-day usage of the term gauḍa.
which is not attested to in inscriptions earlier to the tenth century. = ‘The atiṭanam
made by the iḷayar of Chiṟupochil for Kāvuṭi Īteṉ a native of Kumuḻūr in Erumi-nāṭu’
(II)On some other stone beds of the same cavern on the hill.
[Published in IPS, No. 7.]

These label inscriptions are in early Tamil characters of about the seventh-eighth century and
give the names of Kaḍavuḷaṉ Tirunīlaṉ and six others. They may be the names of Jaina ascetics
of the place.

inscription.head:
No. 389.
Kottaguḍi, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
On a pillar set up near the Gaṇēśa temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, [Māṟavarman] . . ., 6th year = 13th Century.
Badly mutilated. Mentions Tuṟaiyūr-nāḍu and Vāṇagaṅgar.

inscription.head:
No. 390.
Kottaguḍi, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
On the pillar set up near the dhvajastamba of the Gaṇēśa temple.
inscription.p: c. 17th-18th Century.

This gives the names of several persons who contributed capital for the making of the image of
the deity. The sculptor (āchāri) was a Irāmaiyaṉ Chiṉṉakkoṇḍaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 391.
Kottaguḍi, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
On a pillar set up in a street of the village.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Vīrapāṇḍya, 7+1st year, Arpasi 13, Monday = c. 13th Century.
Mentions Tuṟaiyūr-nāḍāḻvāṉ. End portion worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 392.
Emanīśvaram, Hamlet of Paramakkuḍi, Paramagudi Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the west base of the Emanīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Śaka 1489, Prabhava, Kanya 12, Monday, Magha = 1567 September 29 =
A.D. 1567, September 29, Monday.

This records that some chief gave the village Māvilaṅgai in Vaḍatalai Chembi-nāḍu to a mer-
chant member of the nagarattār of Madurai as kuḍi-nīṅgā-dēvadāna of the temple of Tiruk-
kayilāyam alias Iyamanīśuram-uḍaiya-Nāyaṉār. The donor chief whose name is worn out be-
longed to the family (gōtra) of Kallavār and had a number of titles like Sammaṭṭi-Nārāyaṇaṉ.
He is said to have received the village as umbiḷikkai (perhaps from the king).

inscription.head:
No. 393.
Paramakkuḍi (Emanīśvaram), Paramagudi Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the west wall of the Amman shrine in the Emanīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 5+1st year = A.D. 1222.
Fragment. Refers to the purchase of some land by a woman called Periyanaṅgai.

inscription.head:
No. 394.
Paramakkuḍi (Emanīśvaram), Paramagudi Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On a stone lying near the Emanīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara 1, •••= A.D. 1268-1312.

It seems to record that an individual of Maṭṭiyūr Śivapuri in Kēraḷasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu gifted some
land as tax-free dēvadāna. Perhaps he purchased the land from the ūrār of Kumārakuṟuchchi
alias Kōdaṇḍarāma-nallūr.

inscription.head:
No. 395.
Pudūr, Tirumangalam Taluk, Madurai District.
On a stone set up near the Siva temple.
inscription.p: Madurai Nāyaka, Tirumalai-nāyaka, Śaka 1560, Pramādi, Āni 16 = A.D. 1639.

This records gift of the village of Tirumalasamudram to the temple of Chokkanāthasvāmi for
conducting certain festivals in the months of Tai, Vaiyāsi, and Āṉi for the merit of (the king)
Tirumalai-Nāyakkar, a descendant of Viśvanātha-Nāyakkar.

inscription.head:
No. 396.
Kōvilānguḷam, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the south base of the Ambalappasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: c. 10th-11th Century.

The inscription is in verse and praises Chōḻa-chēndaṉ-kōṉ of Iḍattuvaḷi as the lord of Kum-
baṉūr, Kuraṇḍi, Kuṉṟattūr, and Puttūr. He is said to be a scion of the family of Chāttaṉ.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 143/2005]

patiyōr mutal pallōrkaḷu maḷamut[tiya] vilaiyāvaṇam ulakaṟiya viḷaṅku

inscription.head:
No. 397.
Kōvilānguḷam, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the west and south bases of the Ambalappasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga I, 48th year = A.D. 1118.

Some portion at the end containing signatories is worn out. This is in verse and records that
a golden vimāna with a maṇḍapam was constructed in Kumbaṉūr in Cheṅgāṭṭirukkai of Veṇ-
bu-vaḷanāḍu to consecrate the image of Mukkuḍaiyōr (Tīrttaṅkara). Two copper images of the
god and the Yakshi were also presented. Land for a temple site and watershed was also given.
It is the ūrār of Kumbanūr who did all these things. The names of the members of the ūrār are
given in lines 8 to 13. Several of them have some patronizing attributes, reflecting their learing
and knowledge in Tamil. The attribute ‘māmattaraṉ’ found as part of some names is interesting.

[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 141—42/2005] The text in this volume has several variant readings, perhaps due to bad state of preservation of the
inscription at present. Moreover it is treated as two separate inscriptions.

inscription.head:
No. 398.
Kōvilānguḷam, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the south base of the Engumaḻagiya-perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman •••Pāṇḍya, 8+3rd year = c. 12th-13th Century.

This records a gift of land as tax-free tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam to the temple of Guṇagaṇābh
araṇa-Viṇṇagar-emberumāṉ at Kumbaṉūr alias Guṇagaṇābharaṇa-nallūr in Veṇbuḷa-nāḍu by a
Kaṉṉināṭṭaraiyaṉ after purchasing it from the ūrār of the village.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 148/2005.]

inscription.head:
No. 399.
Kōvilānguḷam, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the north base of the Engumaḻagiya-perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Kulaśēkhara, 13+4th year = A.D. 1207.

Gift of a cow for a lamp to the temple of Guṇagaṇābharaṇa-viṇṇagar by Nārāyaṇaṉ Kuḍitāṅgi
of the village.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 146/2005.]

inscription.head:
No. 400.
Kōvilānguḷam, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the north base of the Engumaḻagiya-perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Kulaśēkhara, 13+1st year = A.D. 1204.

A local Brāhmaṇa called Vīrarkuṟi Nampiḷḷai gave one old kāsu for a sandhi lamp in the temple
at Kumbaṉūr Guṇagaṇābharaṇa-nallūr in Cheṅgāṭṭuṟukai in Veṇpuḷa-nāḍu.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 147/2005.]

inscription.head:
No. 401.
Kōvilānguḷam, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the north base of the Engumaḻagiya-perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Kulaśēkhara, 13th year = A.D. 1203.
Fragmentary. Gift of money (a paḻaṅkāsu and 2 tiramam) for a lamp.

l.ta:
tippam oṉṟukku tiruviḷakku cantirātittavarai cella iṭṭa paḻaṅkāccu oṉṟu

inscription.head:
No. 402.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 5th year = A.D. 1221.

The oil-merchants (vāṇigar) of the bazaar Paḻivilaṅgi-perunderu in Iḍattuvaḷi, forming the ūrār
of the place, decided to contribute oil, at the rate of one muṭṭai from each oil-mill each day, for
three lamps in the temple of Kuṟaḷmāṇīśvaram-uḍaiyār at Iḍattuvaḷi in Cheṅgāṭṭirukkai.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 135/2005.]

inscription.head:
No. 403.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Kulaśēkhara, 13+5th year = A.D. 1208.

A kaikkōḷa resident of the temple street gave the jamb stone for the entrance into the ard-
ha-maṇḍapa of the temple of Kuṟaḷmāṇīśvaram-udāiyār at Iḍattuvaḷi in Cheṅgāṭṭirukkai in
Veṇbil-nāḍu.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 133/2005.]

l.ta: ṭṭuc ceṅkāṭṭirukkai iṭattuvaḷi nāyaṉāṟ kuṟaḷ māṇī-
kuḻaiñcāṉ caṅkaṉāṉa puravari mārāyaṉ ṉāyaṉār kōyilaṟtta
maṇṭapattil tiruvāyal tiruṉilaikāl ṉaṭṭivittāṉ itu iva-

inscription.head:
No. 404.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Sundarapāṇḍya, 3rd year = 12th-13th Century.

This damaged record says that a gift of taxes on a dēvadāna land was made by Avaninārāyaṇadēvaṉ
for a festival on the day of Satayam (Śatabhishaj), the natal star of Nāyaṉār Chokkāṇḍār.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 131/2005.]

inscription.head:
No. 405.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, •••Sundarapāṇḍya, •••= c. A.D. 1250.

The trustees of the temple assigned some temple land to a maṭhapati of the temple of Kuṟaḷmāṇīś-
varam-udāiyār at Iḍattuvaḷi in Cheṅgāṭṭirukkai for making two flower-gardens for the use of the
temple.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 134/2005, lines 1-14.]

l.ta: mai ceytu kuṭutta nilattukkuc cūḻellaikkuk kīḻellai cīkā-
kku vaṭakkum mēllellai nampāṇṭāṉ ceykkuk kiḻakkum vaṭavellaima-
ṇ pōkattukku teṟku muṭpaṭṭataṭi pala nilam orumāvuṅ kārā-

inscription.head:
No. 406.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 8th year = A.D. 1224.

Records the setting up of the image of Arundavañjēda-Nāchchiyār by Chēgal-Sēvagattēvaṉ, a
member of the Teṉṉilaṅgai-vaḷañjiyar living in the bazaar Vikkiramapāṇḍiyaṉ-perunderu of
Iḍattuvaḷi.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 132/2005.]

l.ta: yūr kiḻavan aruntavañ cēta ṉācciyārai e-

inscription.head:
No. 407.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 8th year = A.D. 1224.

Records the setting up of the image of Liṅgapurāṇa-dēva by the same individual figuring in the
above inscription (No. 406).
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 130/2005.]

inscription.head:
No. 408.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 13+1st, Dhanus, sudi 3, Wednesday,
Śravaṇa = (taking the year as 3+1) A.D. 1219, December 11.

Gift of some land as tax-free dēvadāna for offerings in a service called Ulaganārāyaṇaṉ-sandhi
by Aḻagaṉ Aruḷāḷapperumāḷ alias Ulaganārāyaṇaṉa-chakkaravatti hailing from Perunāṅgūr in
Chāṇāḍu in Madurōdaya-vaḷanāḍu. Detailed description of the boundaries of the land is given.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 125/2005.]

l.ta: cōṇāṭu koṇṭu muṭikoṇṭa cōḻapurattu vīrar a-
ṉ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa tiruvōṇattu nāḷ maturōtaiya-
vaḷa nāṭṭu cā[ṇā]ṭṭup perunāṅkūr aḻakaṉ aruḷāḷaṉ
ulakanārāyaṇaṉ canti celvatāka kuṭutta nilamāvatu iṭattuvaḷi •••••••••

ka maṇṭala nāṭāḻ paṟṟukku mēṟkum teṉṉellai maṉukula tēvip pē-
raiyaṉ paṉaiyōṭu ceykku vaṭakkum mēlellai kō • kaṉ piraḷai ceykku
m arivīrakaṇṭap pēraiya ceykkuk kiḻakkum vaṭavellai vikkirama
mēṟpaṭiyāṟ cūriyaṉ peṟṟāṉ pakkal vilai koṇṭa nilattukkuc cūḻe-
lukku mēṟkum teṉellai ulakamalai nāṭāḻvāṉ vaya-
lukkum nikarili cōḻappēraiyaṉ vayalukku vaṭakkum mēlellai teṉṉa-
vaṉ muttaṉaraṅkaṉukkuc cītaṉaṅkuṭutta vayalukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭavellai cī-
nilamu mivvulaka nārāyaṇaṉ cantikku amutu paṭiyuḷḷiṭṭa vēṇṭuvaṉava-
yiṟṟukku innilattāl vanta kārāṇmai mīyāṭci kaṭamai antarāyam ma-
ṟṟu meppēṟpaṭṭaṉavum iṟaiyili tēvatāṉamāka ippaṭi cantirātittava-
ṉa ulaka nārāyaṇac cakkiravattiyēṉ uṭaiyār kuṟaḷ māṇī
ccakkaravatti eḻuttu pūṅkālanāṭṭuk karaiyirukkai narapati vāḻaiyūrā-
ṉa naralōka cuntaranallūr peṟṟāṉ peṟṟāṉāṉa tamiḻanāṭṭaraiyaṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 409.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Sundarapāṇḍya, 11th year, Mārgaḻi 5 = A.D. 1281(?).

This is said to be an order of Chōḻagaṅgadēvaṉ, obviously a high official. He ordered that
some 15 mā of land purchased by a goldsmith and another person called Viḻuppādarāyar be
made a tax-free dēvadāna of the deities installed by the goldsmith in the temple in the name
of Chōḻagaṅgadēvaṉ. By the terms of the dēvadāna, the kārāṇmai (ownership and cultivation
right) of the gift land will be vested with the donors and the kaḍamai (government revenue/
upper share) will go to the deities. The kaḍamai on this land was however fixed at half of the
prevailing rate.

l.ta:
cōḻakaṅkatēvaṉ ōlai śrīcuntarapāṇṭiya tēvaṟku
yāṇṭu patinoṉṟāvatu mārkaḻimātam añcāntiyati veṇpuḷa nāṭṭuc
ceṅkāṭṭirukkai iṭattuvaḷi uṭaiyār kuṟaḷ māṇī

muṭaiya nāyaṉār
kōyilil eḻum pōtaṉṟu yiruvaṉāṉa iṭattuvaḷit taṭṭāṉ nampērālē
eḻuntaru-

ḷivittamutaliyār iṉakku nalla perumāḷukkum nācciyāṟkum pirā •••pālaṉāṉa

ivvūr pukalōka kaṇṭapperēriyil mēlai maṭaiyāl nīrpāntu viḷaiyum

ākat tiruccūlakkallukkuṭpaṭṭa nilam patiṉañcumāvum ivarkaḷukkuk
kārāṇmaiyum nāyamāṟkuk kaṭamai āka iṉṉilattukku ivarkaḷ kaṭamai
iṟukkumpaṭi payir pāttum pāḻcāvi nīkkip payir niṉṟa nilattukku iṭattuvaḷi
ūrkkā-

lāl māttāl mukkalanē iṟukkavum karuñce veṭṭikkuṟucci uṭaiyār
kuṟaḷ māṇī

ra muṭaiya nāyaṉār tēvatāṉattukku mēṟkum teṉṉellai
peruntūr ellaikku vaṭakkum mēlellai ceṉṉilaikkuṭi ellaikkuk
kiḻakkum vaṭavellai vāṟapaṟṟukkut teṟkum iṉṉāṉ kellaikkuṭpaṭṭa
nilam ūrañcātiyāka vilai koṇṭa nilam mutaliyārum nācciyārkum ni-

lam irupatumāvum tiruvītinācciyāṟku nilam pattumāvum āka
tiruccūlakkallukkuṭpaṭṭa nilam muppatu māvum pōka nīkkik kārāṇmaiyum
nāyamāṟkuk kaṭamai iṟukkavum kaṭamai iṟukkum paṭi payirpāttu pāḻcāvi
nīkkip payir niṉṟa nilattukku pala tēvatāṉattil iṟukkuṅ kaṭamaiyil
oṉṟupātik kaṭamai iṟukkavum ippaṭikku ūrañcātiyāka vilai koṇṭa
paṭiyilē nāmuṅ koṭuttōm ippaṭikku ivvōlai piṭi-
pāṭākak koṇṭu cantirāttavar cella kalliluñ cempilum veṭṭikkoḷka
ivai iṉakku nalla perumāḷāṉa cōḻakaṅkaṉ eḻuttu•

inscription.head:
No. 410.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Kulaśēkhara (Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara I) = A.D. 1286.

This is also said to be an order of Chōḻagaṅgadēvaṉ (alias Aruḷāḷaṉ Aḻagaṉ) mentioned in the
above inscription. It is addressed to a Śivabrāhmaṇa of the Kuṟaḷmāṇīśvaram temple, who
purchased and donated 4 mā and odd of land to the shrine of Eḍuttaduvaliyapiḷḷaiyār. The land
was to be the dēvadāna of the shrine and the kārāṇmai-kāṇi of the land to be enjoyed by a guhai
(same as maṭha).

l.ta: ṇaril aḻakaṉ uṭaiya pirāṉakku nāyiṉār emmaṇṭala
maṭavār muttaraiyaṉ kaṉalum kiṇaṟum nilam a-
kāṇiyum kiṇaṟum ivvūril eṭuttatu valiya pi-

inscription.head:
No. 411.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, [Māṟavarman] Sundarapāṇḍya I, 15th year = A.D. 1231.

Records the setting up of an image of Durgaiyār in the temple by a goldsmith called Reṭṭai
Aḻagaṉ alias Iḍattuvalit-taṭṭāṉ.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 126/2005.]

inscription.head:
No. 412.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 14th year, Mithuna, badi 13, Monday,
Rōhiṇi = A.D. 1230, June 10, Monday.

The trustees of Kuṟaḻmāṇīśvaram temple in Iḍattuvaḷi in Cheṅgāṭṭirukkai in Veṇpu-nāḍu sold
a tank with its cultivable land to Ulagasamaiñjitaṉ a resident of Sīvallavaṉ-perunderu alias
Dēsiyāsiriya-paṭṭaṇam, which was a part of the village, with the condition that the purchaser
had to measure out a fixed quantity of paddy to the temple of Viṉāyagapiḷḷaiyār in the village.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 127/2005.]

l.ta: ḻaṅkiyaruḷiya cīcuntarapāṇṭiya tēvaṟkku yāṇṭu patiṉālāvatu mi-
tuṉa nāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattut tiraiyōtaciyun tiṅkaḷ kiḻamaiyum pe-
ṟṟa urōcaṇi nāḷ veṇpuṇāṭṭuc ceṅkāṭṭirukkai iṭattuvaḷi u-

paṭṭaṇat-

eṭu-

ttatu valiya piḷḷaiyār tēvatāṉañ ciṟumaṇkuḷattukkuc cūḻellaikkuk
kīḻel-

lai nilaitāṅkiyēri nīrnilaikku mēṟku nāṭṭiṉa tiruccūlakkallukku mēṟkum
teṉṉellai
pāṟaiyil kalluralukkum nāṭṭiṉa tiruccūlakkallukku vaṭakkum mēlellai
ikkuḷattu nīrnakkalukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭavellai uṭaiyār kuṟaḷmāṇīccuvara-

vilai
kkuṟaviṟṟu poruḷaṟak koṇṭu caṇṭēcuvaravilaip piramāṇam paṇṇik-

kuṭuttōm patipātamūla paṭṭuṭaip paiñcācāriyarōm ulacamaiñcitaṟku
ippa-

nāṟkala-

mum puṉcekku ikkāl māttāl patiṉāṟu mēṉi kāṟ tiramamum iṟuppārākavum
ippaṭi

paṇṇikku-

ulaka ca-

inscription.head:
No. 413.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the east wall of the central shrine in the Vāḷavanda-amman temple.
inscription.p: This is in verse recording the setting up of the deity Aḻagiya-Āḻvār by Chōḻagaṅgadēvaṉ.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 138/2005.]

inscription.head:
No. 414.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the east wall of the central shrine in the Vāḷavanda-amman temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Kulaśēkhara, 13+2nd year = A.D. 1205.

Portions completely worn out. Tiruvālavāyuḍaiyāṉ Chōḻagaṅgaṉ Aḻagiyapperumāḷ of Iḍattu-
vaḷi seems to have assigned some land as jenmam to somebody (name lost) with the stipulation
to measure out paddy at the rate of 6 kalam per mā to the temple of Aḻagiya-Āḻvār at Iḍattuvaḷi
in Cheṅgāṭtirukkai a part of Veṇbuḷa-nāḍu, in Madurōdaya-vaḷanāḍu in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 137/2005, lines 1—6.]

inscription.head:
No. 415.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On a stone of the well opposite to the choultry.
inscription.p:
This verse inscription refers to the construction of the well inside the big tank in Iḍattuvaḷi and
fixing of the slabs for mounting the pulley, by a Ulakkēḻuḍaiyāṉ.

l.ta: alakil pukaṭc cakara yāṇṭāyirattoṭoru nūṟṟaipattu nāli-

ṉ mēlaḻakarākiṉṟa ilakumati ḷiṭattuvaḷip peruṅkuḷattu •••yarkē
yeḻilamutakkiṇa-
ṟu kaṇṭu vattalkaṟkāliṭṭāṉ ulakkēḻuṭaiyāṉ veṉṟi vi

inscription.head:
No. 416.
Aruppukkōṭṭai, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On a slab set in a field 3 km east of the village.
inscription.p: Sētupati, Tirumalai Sētupati Kātta Regunāthadēva, Dundubhi = A.D. 1682.

This is said to be a deed of gift (bhāgamānipam) by Uḍaiyattēvar younger brother and execu-
tive agent of the king, assigning land in the village of Bommākōṭṭai for the daily worship of
Vāḷavandāḷ-ammai at Aruppukkōṭṭai for the latter's merit.
[Published in Virudhunagar District Inscriptions, No. 140/2005.]

edition.l: maṉa aḻakikatta tēvaṉ mallaṉa[ yapaṭṭi makara-
ca] ṉāyakkaṉ kaṭṭai keñcaṉ paṭi makkañ-

inscription.head:
No. 417.
Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the north wall of the prākāra of the Sahāyavalli shrine in the Tirumūlanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [Rājarāja I], 10th year = A.D. 995.

Fragment. Gift of a lamp in the temple of Tāṉtōṉṟi-paramasvāmi in Tiruchchuḻiyal by a carpen-
ter called Chāttaṉ Chāttaṉ for the merit of a goldsmith called Chēndaṉ Chāttaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 418.
Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the north wall of the prākāra of the Sahāyavalli shrine in the Tirumūlanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman [Rājarāja I], 12th year = A.D. 997.
Fragment. Refers to a daṇḍanāyagam Irāja-tamiḻappēraraiyar. Other details lost.

inscription.head:
No. 419.
Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the door-post of the Praḷayaviṭankar shrine in the Tirumūlanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: This fragment seems to refer to the defeat of a Jananātha.

inscription.head:
No. 420.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk,
Ramanathapuram District.
On the south base of the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 4+3rd = A.D. 954.

This records gift of 75 sheep for a lamp to the Bhaṭārar of the temple of Sundara-paṇḍya-Īś-
varam at Tiruchchuḻiyal Paḷḷimaḍai a dēvadāna in Parittikkuḍi-nāḍu by Chōḻāntaka-Pallavara-
iyaṉ alias Māṟaṉ Ādichchaṉ of Pōḻiyūr in Pōḻiyūr-nāḍu. The sheep were entrusted to Chāttaṉ
Dēvaṉ a kuḍi and paḍaittalaivaṉ of Kīraṉūr in Teṉpula-nāḍu. Paṭaraṉ Chōlai, the kāviti of
Pōḻiyūr stood surety (puṇai) for the latter.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 79.]

l.ta: ṭināṭṭu tēvatāṉan tiruccuḻiyal paḷḷi[ma]ṭai cuntarapāṇṭiya ī
yiṉa māṟaṉāticcaṉ vaitta tiruṉontāviḷakku oṉṟu iravum pakalumuṭṭāmē

erivatāka cōḷāntaka nārāyattiṉ kīḻ uḻakkiṉāl ney uḻakkāḻākku
inney-

kku cāvāmūvāp pērāṭāka aṭutta āṭu eḻupattaiñcu ivai koṇṭu
aṭṭuvāṉāyi-

ṉāṉ [teṉpu]la nāṭṭu kīraṉūrkkuṭi paṭaittalaivaṉ cāttaṉ tēvaṉ,
ivaṉukkup puṇai

pōḻiyūrnāṭṭup pōḻiyūrk kāviti paṭāraṉ cōlai, iviḷakku rakṣippār
tiruccu

inscription.head:
No. 421.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk,
Ramanathapuram District.
On the south base of the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 4+4 = A.D. 955.

Gift of 25 sheep for a lamp to the temple by Kiḻavaṉ Aruḷāḻi of Tiruchchuḻiyal for the merit of
his son. A shepherd, called Kuḻuvāṇai Pūti of Peruñchennilakkuḍi in Paruttikkuḍi-nāḍu took the
sheep in lease (aḍai) for the supply of ghee.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 80.]

inscription.head:
No. 422.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk, Ramanathapuram
District.
On the north base of the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: [Pāṇḍya?], 26th year = c. 9th Century.
Incomplete. Some gift by a Āḷavvukkōṉ Kāḷipēṟu and his daughter.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 33.]

l.ta: ḷi pēṟṟum eṉ makaḷ appi ciṅkamum

inscription.head:
No. 423.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk,
Ramanathapuram District.
On the north base of the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 4+7th year = A.D. 958.

This records a gift of 20 sheep for a lamp to a maṭha of the Mahāvratis attached to the temple of
Paḷḷipaḍai Sundarapāṇḍiya-Īśvaram in Parittikkuḍi-nāḍu. Three shepherds (āyaṉ) of Maṟukkīḻ
in Chōḻa-nāḍu took the sheep in lease for supply of ghee as stipulated.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 88.]

inscription.head:
No. 424.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk,
Ramanathapuram District.
On the north base of the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 4+5th year = A.D. 956.

This lists the names of a number of shepherds (āyaṉ, veṭṭikkuḍi-paḍaittalaivaṉ) who took
on lease the sheep (chemmari-veḷḷāḍu) of the temple Paḷḷimaḍai Sundarapāṇḍiya-Īśvaram in
Tiruchchuḻiyal, a dēvadāna of Parittikkuḍi-nāḍu and the quantity of ghee to be supplied by
them to the temple.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 82.]

inscription.head:
No. 425.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk,
Ramanathapuram District.
On the north base of the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Chaḍaiyamāṟan (Rājasimha ?), 2nd year = A.D. 907.

A carpenter of Kalichal-maṅgalam in Aḷaṟṟu-nāḍu gave 25 sheep to the temple of Sundarapāṇḍi-
ya-Īchchuvaram in Tiruchchuriyal. It seems the group called attāṇi took charge of the sheep
and assigned the same on lease to two other persons. Mentions the sabhā of Vāṉavaṉmādēvi a
brahmadēya in Veṇbu-nāḍu, whose role is not clear.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 46.]

l.ta: kOccaṭaiya māṟaṟku yāṇṭu iraṇṭu ivvāṇṭu paruttikkuṭi nā-
ṭṭut tēvatāṉam tiruccuriyal cuntarapāṇṭiya īccuvarattu tēvarkku
aḷaṟṟunāṭṭu kalicalmaṅkalattut taccaṉ • ṉ ulakaṉēṉ veṇpunāṭṭup pi-

añcu caṭaiyaṉappi koṇṭāṉ panniraṇṭarai, nāṭaṉ panniraṇṭarai, ivvi-

inscription.head:
No. 426.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk,
Ramanathapuram District.
On the north base of the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 13th year = A.D. 960.

Gift of 100 sheep for two lamps to the temple of Sundarapāṇḍiya-Īśvaram by Pōḻi-
yūr-nāṭṭu-Mūvēndavēḷaṉ alias Araṅgam Pūdi, from Chuḍuvūr in Poḻiyūr-nāḍu.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 90.]

l.ta:
śrī cōḻaṉ talaikoṇṭa kōvīrapāṇṭiya tēvaṟku yāṇṭu patiṉ mūṉṟu
ivvāṇṭu paruttikkuṭi nā-

ney āḻākku mēṟpaṭi kiḻavaṉappi koṇṭa āṭu

inscription.head:
No. 427.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk,
Ramanathapuram District.
On the north base of the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p:
Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya, sudi 11, Monday, Rōhiṇi =
c. 13th Century.

Fragment. Seems to be some arrangement for the distribution of the shares (aṟudikkarai) in the
brahmadēya village.

inscription.head:
No. 428.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk,
Ramanathapuram District.
On the north base of the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: c. 9th Century.

Gift of 50 sheep by Chāttaṅgāri, for a lamp to the temple of Tirukkāṭṭām-paḷḷi-dēva at Kuṟaṇḍi
in Veṇbu-nāḍu. The ūrār and gaṇattār undertook to protect the gift.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 34.]

l.ta: veṇpuṇāṭṭuk kuṟaṇṭi tirukkāṭṭām paḷḷit tEvarkku tiru no-
ntā viḷakkoṉṟiṉukku ivvūr cakāttaṅkāri aṭṭiṉa āṭu ai-
mpatu ivai ivvūr ūrārum kaṇattārum kāval

inscription.head:
No. 429.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk,
Ramanathapuram District.
On the north base of the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Vīrapāṇḍya, 4+5th year = A.D. 956.
This incomplete record mentions the names of a number of temple girls. Their role is not clear.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 83.]

l.ta:
śrīcōḻaṉṟalai koṇṭa kōvīrapāṇṭiyaṟku yāṇṭu nāṉku itaṉe[ti*]

inscription.head:
No. 430.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk,
Ramanathapuram District.
On the parapet stone of the entrance, right side, into the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māran Chaḍaiyan, 26th year = A.D. 794.

Gift of 100 sheep for a lamp by a native of Kuṇṇūr (in •••nilakkuḍi-nāḍu) to the temple of
Tirukkāṭṭām-paḷḷi in Kuṟaṇḍi in Veṇbu-nāḍu.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 32.]

inscription.head:
No. 431.
Paḷḷimaḍam, hamlet of Tiruchchuḻi, Aruppukkottai Taluk,
Ramanathapuram District.
On the parapet stone of the entrance, left side, into the Kālanāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟan Chaḍaiyan, 35+6 = A.D. 809.

A gift of 50 sheep by (the villagers of) Pāmbārūr to the temple of Tirukkāṭṭām-paḷḷi in Kuṟaṇḍi
in Veṇbu-nāḍu.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 39.]

l.ta: ñāḻi ney aṭṭa aṭutta āṭu ai-

inscription.head:
No. 432.
Sankaranayinarkōyil, Sankaranayinarkoyil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the main gōpura of the Śankaralingasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Viśvāvasu, Vaigāsi 24, Monday, Uttarāshāḍha = c. 17th-18th Century.

This records the rights and privileges enjoyed by the communities of kuḍumbar (also called
Dēvēndira or Deyvēndira kuḍumbar) and the paṟaiya as given by some unnamed Pāṇḍya king.
It narrates that once upon a time when the world was facing severe drought the Pāṇḍya, Chōḻa
and Chēra kings went to the court of Dēvēndra (Indra) seeking rain. As the god was whiling
away his time giving false promises the Pāṇḍya king got angry and left the court but only after
taking forcefully some children of the celestial ladies for making them the heads of four fami-
lies of the kuḍumbar. He also brought with them all the required seeds and pair of livestock for
cultivation. For the eldest member among the celestial children he bestowed the title Deyvēn-
dra-kuḍumbaṉ and for three others the titles of vāriyaṉ, akkasālai, and iḷakatāri respectively.
Upon the efforts of these people the cultivation of crops thrived making the king happy. Hence
the king bestowed on them various honours like white elephant, silver parasol, day-time torch
etc. Now on the basis of the document which had sanctioned the above privileges to the kuḍum-
baṉs, the paṟaiya community also got some privileges but of lesser ranking than those of the
kuḍumbaṉ community (lines 9—14).

l.ta:
tiṅkaḷ kiḻamaiyum uttirāṭamum peṟṟa nāḷ
teyvēntira kuṭumpaṉ vālāttumpaṭi muṉ eṭutta tūvāparayukattil ukkira
peruvaḷutiyum cēraṉum

cōḻaṉum lōkam vaṟumai paṭṭu irukkiṟa kālattilē tēvēntiraṉ pakkal
maḻai kēḷkka pōṉa iṭattilē makavāṉum maṉam kuḻirntu irummeṉa cēraṉum
cōḻaṉum vaṇaṅki

irukka pāṇṭiyaṉ teyvēntiraṉuṭaṉē kūṭi irukka teyvēntiraṉum
varucai ceyyiṟāppōlē kavaṭu niṉaikka pāṇṭiyaṉum kōpittu yeḷuntu
tevvakaṉṉi makkaḷ

nālu kuṭumpattārai kaippiṭiyāk kūṭṭi koṇṭu ceṉṉal viraiyum kaṉṉal
viraiyum katali viraiyum paṉai viraiyum itu mutalāṉa palavittum oru iṣavum
curaviyum koṇṭu pūmiyilē vantāṉ

nālvar mutavaṉai teyvēntira kuṭumpaṉ paṭṭamṅkaṭṭi mūṉṟu pērukku
vāriyaṉ akkālai iḷakatāripaṭṭam kaṭṭi oru nāḷaikku ••••••kaṉṟu veṭṭi
veḷḷāmai kaṇṭa paṭi yiṉālē rācāvum manammakintu

veḷḷai yāṉaiyum veḷḷi vaṭṭa kuṭaiyum keṟāṭiyum pakal pantam pāvāṭai
raṭṭai cilampum raṭṭaic coṭukkum naṉmaikku patiṉāṟu pantakkālum
tīmmaikku raṇṭu tērum pañcavaṉṉa virutum patineṭṭu me-
kuṭṭi kuṭumpaṉum allāttar kuṭumpaṉum kūṭikkoṇṭu cumukamāka [kuṭi-]
yēṟa ātipūṟuvam rācākkaḷ kuṭutta paṭṭayam pāttu paṟaiyārukku uṇṭā-
ṉa varucai nammaikku mūṉṟu kāl pantakālum oru cilampum orukkoṭukkum
oṟṟai mārāppum oru pantamum kīḻpāvāṭaiyum mañci kaṇṭiyakkompu
yillāta vīṭum tīmmaikkuk kaṭṭaṇamum paṭṭaya piṟakaram kaṭṭaḷai iṭṭōm

kuṭumpakaḷukku kutirai kuṭaiyum iṭṭu kuṭai [virutā] •••koṇṭa viraipāṭu
kuṭuttu inta varucai

piṟakāram kaṭṭaḷai iṭuvittōm ••••••••••••keṅkaik karaiyilē kārāmpacuvai
koṉṟa •••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 433.
Tiruvāḍānai, Tiruvadanai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the main gōpura of the Ādiratnēśvara temple, right of entrance.
inscription.p:
Sētupatidēvar, Śaka 1557, Yuva, Tai 14, sudi paurṇami, Pushya =
A.D. 1636, January 12.

This records an order of Tirumalaiyaṉ, for the merit of the king Sētupatidēvar, that nobody
should misappropriate the income from the temple villages in possession of the temple of
Āḍāṉai-Nāyakar. In the imprecation it is warned that anybody who dares to indulge in taking
bride (kaikkūli) in these villages would be abused of surrendering his wife, niece and daughter-
in-law to his enemies.

inscription.head:
No. 434.
Tiruvāḍānai, Tiruvadanai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the main gōpura of the Ādiratnēśvara temple, right of entrance.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya (?), Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān, 17th year = c. 13th Century.

Damaged badly. This is an order of the king remitting taxes on some land lying in the village
of Añjukōṭṭai.

edition.l: • • kāl uḷḷa paḻantē[ vatāṉa-
m paḷ] ḷiccantam nīkki nīkki • •

inscription.head:
No. 435.
Tiruvāḍānai, Tiruvadanai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On the main gōpura of the Ādiratnēśvara temple, left of entrance.
inscription.p: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I, 16th year = A.D. 1232.
This very mutilated inscription seems to record a tax-free gift of land by the king.

inscription.head:
No. 436.
Tiruvāḍānai, Tiruvadanai Taluk, Ramanathapuram District.
On a slab set up near the tank in the Ādiratnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Sētupati, Muttu-vayiravanātha Sētupati Kāttadēvar, Śaka 1642, Vikāri =
A.D. 1720.

This is about the construction of a pavilion (kūḍam) for the ablution ceremony in the tank
called Sūryapuṭkaraṇi in front of the temple of Āḍāṉai-Nāyakar Aṉbirpiriyāda-Ammaṉ for the
merit of Muttu-vayiravanātha Sētupati-kāttadēvar. The latter is given several high-sounding
attributes.
